actions.en: Tonify the Spleen and augment qi
Moisten the Lung and stop cough
Harmonize the actions of other herbs

indications.en: Spleen and Stomach vacuity with fatigue and weakness
Palpitations and shortness of breath
Cough with copious phlegm
Cramping pain of the abdomen and limbs
Sores, carbuncles, and toxic swellings

contraindications_zh: 不宜与海藻、京大戟、红大戟、甘遂、芫花同用（十八反）。湿盛胀满、水肿者不宜用。
contraindications_en: Should not be used concurrently with hai zao (Sargassum), jing da ji (Pekinensis Radix), hong da ji (Knoxiae Radix), gan sui (Kansui Radix), and yuan hua (Genkwa Flos) per the Eighteen Antagonisms (shiba fan). Not suitable in dampness repletion with distention, fullness, or edema.

dosage_range.notes: 调和诸药用量宜小（1.5–3g），补益用量宜大（6–10g）

processing_methods.name: 生甘草
processing_methods.effect: 清热解毒，润肺止咳

processing_methods.name: 炙甘草
processing_methods.effect: 补脾益气，缓急止痛

safety_notes_zh: 长期大量服用可引起假性醛固酮增多症（水肿、低钾、高血压）。
safety_notes_en: Prolonged high-dose use may cause pseudoaldosteronism (edema, hypokalemia, hypertension).

classical_references.passage: 味甘，平。主五脏六腑寒热邪气，坚筋骨，长肌肉，倍力，金疮肿，解毒。久服轻身延年。

description_zh: 豆科植物甘草、胀果甘草或光果甘草的干燥根及根茎。归心、肺、脾、胃经。在方剂中常作使药，调和诸药，被誉为国老。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 甘草主含三萜皂苷（甘草酸、甘草次酸）与黄酮类（甘草苷、异甘草素），药理示其抗炎（类糖皮质激素样）、抗病毒、保肝、祛痰镇咳与解毒作用，并能调和诸药。
summary.en: Gancao contains triterpenoid saponins (glycyrrhizin, glycyrrhetinic acid) and flavonoids (liquiritin, isoliquiritigenin); pharmacology shows glucocorticoid-like anti-inflammatory, antiviral, hepatoprotective, expectorant-antitussive and detoxifying effects and harmonizes formula actions.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 慢性乙型肝炎辅助（甘草酸制剂）

modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Cough with sputum and sore throat

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 药物与食物中毒解毒辅助
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Adjunct in drug and food poisoning

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》以甘草酸与甘草苷作为含量测定指标；复方甘草片、甘草酸制剂等广泛临床应用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chinese Pharmacopoeia uses glycyrrhizin and liquiritin as assay markers; compound licorice tablets and glycyrrhizin injections in wide clinical use.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 长期大量使用可致假性醛固酮增多症（水肿、低钾、高血压）；与利尿剂、洋地黄、糖皮质激素相互作用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chronic high-dose use causes pseudo-hyperaldosteronism (edema, hypokalemia, hypertension); interacts with diuretics, digitalis and corticosteroids.

evidence_notes.note_zh: WHO 建议甘草酸长期摄入不超过 100 mg/日。
evidence_notes.note_en: WHO suggests long-term glycyrrhizin intake not exceed approximately 100 mg/day.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 十八反：不可与海藻、大戟、甘遂、芫花同用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Classical eighteen-antagonism: avoid with haizao, daji, gansui and yuanhua.

description_en: The dried root and rhizome of Glycyrrhiza uralensis, G. inflata, or G. glabra (Fabaceae). Enters the Heart, Lung, Spleen, and Stomach channels. Sweet and neutral, it tonifies the Spleen, moistens the Lung, moderates spasms, resolves toxicity, and harmonizes the actions of other medicinals. Traditionally honored as the 'National Elder' (guo lao) for its ubiquitous role as an envoy herb in classical formulas.

actions.en: Augment the wei qi and stabilize the exterior

indications.en: Spleen qi vacuity with reduced appetite and loose stools

indications.zh: 中气下陷，久泻脱肛，子宫脱垂
indications.en: Sinking of middle qi with chronic diarrhea, prolapse of rectum or uterus
Exterior vacuity with spontaneous sweating
Qi-vacuity edema with dysuria

indications.zh: 气血不足，疮疡内陷，脓成不溃或久溃不敛
indications.en: Qi and blood vacuity with non-healing sores or ulcers
Qi-vacuity with blood stasis causing hemiplegia

contraindications_zh: 表实邪盛，气滞湿阻，食积内停，阴虚阳亢，疮疡初起或溃后热毒尚盛者，均不宜用。
contraindications_en: Not suitable in repletion exterior pathogens, qi stagnation with dampness obstruction, food stagnation, yin vacuity with yang hyperactivity, or early-stage sores or post-ulceration when heat-toxin is still vigorous.

dosage_range.notes: 一般煎服9–30g；补气升阳宜炙用；固表止汗、利水消肿、托疮生肌宜生用；大剂量可用至30–120g（如补阳还五汤）

processing_methods.name: 生黄芪
processing_methods.effect: 益卫固表，利水消肿，托毒生肌

processing_methods.name: 炙黄芪
processing_methods.effect: 补中益气，升阳举陷作用增强

safety_notes_zh: 实证及阴虚阳亢、热毒炽盛者忌用。单用大剂量偶见腹胀、胸闷。与免疫抑制剂合用需注意（可能拮抗）。
safety_notes_en: Contraindicated in repletion patterns, yin vacuity with yang hyperactivity, and blazing heat-toxin. Occasional abdominal distention and chest oppression with high single doses. Caution when combined with immunosuppressants (possible antagonism).

classical_references.passage: 味甘，微温。主痈疽久败疮，排脓止痛，大风癞疾，五痔鼠瘘，补虚，小儿百病。

description_zh: 豆科植物蒙古黄芪或膜荚黄芪的干燥根。归脾、肺经。为补气升阳之要药，被誉为补气之长，常与当归相须为用（当归补血汤）以补气生血。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 黄芪含皂苷、多糖和异黄酮类成分，具有免疫调节、强心、抗氧化、抗疲劳及肾脏保护等作用。
summary.en: Astragalus contains saponins, polysaccharides, and isoflavones that produce immunomodulatory, cardiotonic, antioxidant, anti-fatigue, and renoprotective effects.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 肿瘤放化疗辅助（改善免疫与生活质量）
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Adjuvant to cancer chemo/radiotherapy (immune support, quality of life)

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 慢性心力衰竭辅助治疗

evidence_notes.note_zh: 黄芪多糖提高巨噬细胞和 NK 细胞活性，增强非特异性免疫。
evidence_notes.note_en: Astragalus polysaccharides enhance macrophage and NK cell activity, boosting nonspecific immunity.

evidence_notes.note_zh: WHO 专论收录黄芪用于辅助治疗慢性病和免疫支持。
evidence_notes.note_en: WHO monograph recognizes Astragalus as supportive therapy for chronic illness and immune function.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 与免疫抑制剂同用应谨慎；可能增强环磷酰胺疗效并减轻骨髓抑制。
evidence_notes.note_en: Use caution with immunosuppressants; may potentiate cyclophosphamide efficacy while reducing myelosuppression.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》以黄芪甲苷（≥0.040%）和毛蕊异黄酮葡萄糖苷为质量指标。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chinese Pharmacopoeia specifies astragaloside IV (≥0.040%) and calycosin-7-glucoside as quality markers.

description_en: The dried root of Astragalus membranaceus var. mongholicus or A. membranaceus (Fabaceae). Enters the Spleen and Lung channels. Sweet and slightly warm, it is the premier herb for tonifying qi and raising yang, augmenting the defensive qi to secure the exterior, and promoting urination to reduce edema. Frequently paired with dang gui in Dang Gui Bu Xue Tang to generate blood through vigorous qi supplementation.

actions.en: Moisten the intestines and unblock the bowels

indications.en: Blood vacuity with sallow complexion, dizziness, and palpitations
Irregular menstruation, amenorrhea, and dysmenorrhea
Vacuity-cold abdominal pain
Intestinal dryness with constipation
Wind-damp bi pain
Traumatic injury and sores/abscesses

contraindications_zh: 湿盛中满、大便溏泄者忌服。热盛出血者不宜。
contraindications_en: Contraindicated in dampness repletion with epigastric fullness, loose stools, or diarrhea. Not suitable in bleeding due to repletion heat.

dosage_range.notes: 煎服6–12g；大剂量补血养血可用15–30g；润肠通便可用10–30g。酒炒活血之力增强。

processing_methods.name: 当归身
processing_methods.effect: 补血养血

processing_methods.name: 当归头
processing_methods.effect: 活血兼止血

processing_methods.name: 当归尾
processing_methods.effect: 活血破血之力较强

processing_methods.name: 酒当归
processing_methods.effect: 增强活血通经止痛之功

safety_notes_zh: 甘温滑润，湿盛中满、大便溏泄者忌服。月经过多、出血倾向者慎用。与抗凝药物（华法林）合用可增加出血风险。过量可致腹痛腹泻。
safety_notes_en: Sweet, warm, and slippery in nature; contraindicated in dampness repletion with epigastric fullness, loose stools, or diarrhea. Use with caution in excessive menstruation or bleeding tendency. Concurrent use with anticoagulants (warfarin) may increase bleeding risk. Overdose may cause abdominal pain and diarrhea.

classical_references.passage: 味甘，温。主咳逆上气，温疟寒热洗洗在皮肤中，妇人漏下，绝子，诸恶疮疡金疮，煮饮之。

description_zh: 伞形科植物当归的干燥根。归肝、心、脾经。为补血之要药、妇科圣药，补血活血、调经止痛；李东垣谓其“头止血，身养血，尾破血，全活血”。与黄芪相须为当归补血汤。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 当归含挥发性藁本内酯与水溶性阿魏酸及当归多糖，药理研究显示其促进造血、改善微循环、抗血小板、抗炎与平滑肌调节作用。
summary.en: Danggui contains volatile ligustilide, water-soluble ferulic acid and polysaccharides; research shows hematopoietic support, microcirculation improvement, antiplatelet, anti-inflammatory and smooth-muscle modulating effects.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 血瘀型心脑血管病辅助

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》以阿魏酸作为质量控制指标之一。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chinese Pharmacopoeia uses ferulic acid as a quality marker.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 含香豆素类成分，与华法林合用可能增加出血风险。
evidence_notes.note_en: Contains coumarin constituents; may potentiate warfarin and increase bleeding risk.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 当归补血汤（当归:黄芪=1:5）为经典补气生血方，药理研究显示协同促进造血。
evidence_notes.note_en: Danggui Buxue Tang (danggui:huangqi 1:5) is classic qi-blood tonic; studies show synergistic hematopoietic effect.

description_en: The dried root of Angelica sinensis (Apiaceae). Enters the Liver, Heart, and Spleen channels. Sweet, pungent, and warm, it is the foremost herb for tonifying and invigorating blood and the most widely used gynecological medicinal. Li Dongyuan noted that the head stops bleeding, the body nourishes blood, and the tail breaks stasis, while the whole root invigorates blood overall.

actions.en: Fortify the Spleen and augment qi

indications.en: Spleen vacuity with reduced appetite, abdominal distention, and diarrhea
Phlegm-rheum with dizziness and palpitations
Edema
Spontaneous sweating due to Spleen qi vacuity
Restless fetus in pregnancy

contraindications_zh: 阴虚内热、津液亏耗、燥渴者不宜用。
contraindications_en: Not suitable in yin vacuity with internal heat, fluid depletion, or dry thirst.

dosage_range.notes: 健脾止泻常用6–12g；燥湿利水用9–15g；大剂量(30–60g)可用于顽固性便秘以补气通便。

processing_methods.name: 生白术
processing_methods.effect: 健脾燥湿，利水消肿之力较胜

processing_methods.name: 炒白术
processing_methods.effect: 缓和燥性，增强健脾止泻之功

processing_methods.name: 焦白术
processing_methods.effect: 健脾止泻，多用于脾虚久泻

safety_notes_zh: 性温而燥，阴虚内热、口干舌燥者不宜单用。现代研究显示本品无明显毒性，临床常规用量安全。
safety_notes_en: Warm and drying in nature; not suitable for sole use in yin vacuity with internal heat, dry mouth, or dry tongue. Modern research shows no significant toxicity at standard clinical dosages.

classical_references.passage: 味苦，温。主风寒湿痹，死肌，痉疸，止汗，除热，消食，作煎饵。久服轻身延年不饥。

description_zh: 菊科植物白术的干燥根茎。归脾、胃经。为补气健脾、燥湿利水之要药，被誉为“脾脏补气健脾第一要药”，与茯苓相须用以健脾利湿。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 白术的主要活性成分为倍半萜内酯（白术内酯 I、II、III）及多糖，具有健脾益气、调节胃肠动力、利尿、抗炎和免疫调节作用。
summary.en: Atractylodis macrocephalae Rhizoma contains sesquiterpene lactones (atractylenolides I–III) and polysaccharides, with prokinetic/antispasmodic bidirectional gastrointestinal effects, diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory activity.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 功能性消化不良及脾虚泄泻
癌症化疗后恶病质辅助治疗（白术内酯 III）

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中药学》（Bensky）指出白术对胃肠道具有双向调节作用，既可促进胃排空，也可抑制肠道过度蠕动。
evidence_notes.note_en: Bensky notes that Bai Zhu exerts bidirectional regulation of gastrointestinal motility, both promoting gastric emptying and inhibiting intestinal hyperactivity.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 陈氏药理学提到白术多糖可增强网状内皮系统吞噬功能，具有免疫增强作用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen note that Bai Zhu polysaccharides enhance reticuloendothelial phagocytosis, indicating immunostimulant activity.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》2020年版规定白术按白术内酯 III 计含量不得少于 0.35 mg/g。
evidence_notes.note_en: The 2020 Chinese Pharmacopoeia specifies a minimum content of 0.35 mg/g atractylenolide III.

description_en: The dried rhizome of Atractylodes macrocephala (Asteraceae). Enters the Spleen and Stomach channels. Sweet, bitter, and warm, it is regarded as the primary herb for fortifying the Spleen and augmenting qi while drying dampness and promoting urination. Commonly paired with fu ling to strengthen the Spleen and drain dampness, as in Si Jun Zi Tang.

actions.en: Quiet the Heart and calm the spirit

indications.en: Edema with scanty urination
Phlegm-rheum with dizziness and palpitations
Spleen vacuity with reduced appetite, loose stools, and diarrhea
Restlessness of the spirit with palpitations, anxiety, and insomnia
Vaginal discharge due to Spleen vacuity with dampness

contraindications_zh: 虚寒精滑、气虚下陷者忌用。阴虚津亏者慎用。
contraindications_en: Contraindicated in vacuity cold with seminal efflux and qi vacuity with sinking. Use with caution in yin vacuity with fluid depletion.

dosage_range.notes: 煎服9–15g，大剂量可用至30g。茯苓皮偏于利水消肿；茯神偏于宁心安神；赤茯苓偏于清热利湿。

processing_methods.name: 白茯苓
processing_methods.effect: 健脾利湿之功通用

processing_methods.name: 赤茯苓
processing_methods.method: 菌核近外皮淡红色部分
processing_methods.effect: 利水渗湿、清热之力较强

processing_methods.name: 茯神
processing_methods.effect: 宁心安神之力尤佳

processing_methods.name: 茯苓皮
processing_methods.effect: 专于利水消肿

safety_notes_zh: 药性平和，为补脾利湿之佳品，一般无毒副作用。虚寒精滑、气虚下陷者慎用。现代研究显示本品安全范围广。
safety_notes_en: Mild in nature; an excellent herb for tonifying the spleen and draining dampness, generally without toxic side effects. Use with caution in vacuity cold with seminal efflux and qi vacuity with sinking. Modern research shows a wide safety margin.

classical_references.passage: 味甘，平。主胸胁逆气，忧恚惊邪恐悸，心下结痛，寒热烦满咳逆，口焦舌干，利小便。久服安魂养神，不饥延年。

description_zh: 多孔菌科真菌茯苓的干燥菌核。归心、脾、肾经。利水渗湿而不伤正气，为补脾利湿之佳品；与白术相须为用以健脾利湿（四君子汤、苓桂术甘汤）。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 茯苓主含 β-葡聚糖（茯苓多糖）与羊毛甾烷型三萜酸（茯苓酸），药理示其利尿、免疫调节、抗肿瘤、镇静及保肝作用。
summary.en: Fuling contains beta-1,3-glucan (pachyman) and lanostane triterpenoid acids (pachymic acid); pharmacology shows diuretic, immunomodulatory, antitumor, sedative and hepatoprotective effects.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》以茯苓酸含量作为质量评价指标。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chinese Pharmacopoeia uses pachymic acid content as quality marker.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 四君子汤、五苓散等经典方均以茯苓为核心利水渗湿药。
evidence_notes.note_en: Core diuretic-dampness herb in classical formulas such as Si Jun Zi Tang and Wu Ling San.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 性平和缓，适用面广，虚实皆宜，长期使用安全性良好。
evidence_notes.note_en: Mild and neutral; suitable across vacuity and repletion patterns with good long-term safety.

description_en: The dried sclerotium of the fungus Poria cocos (Polyporaceae). Enters the Heart, Spleen, and Kidney channels. Sweet, bland, and neutral, it promotes urination and percolates dampness without injuring upright qi, making it an ideal Spleen-fortifying diuretic. Paired with bai zhu for synergistic Spleen-strengthening and dampness-draining action in Si Jun Zi Tang and Ling Gui Zhu Gan Tang.

actions.en: Induce sweating and release the exterior
Diffuse the Lung and calm wheezing

indications.zh: 风寒表实证，恶寒发热无汗
indications.en: Wind-cold exterior repletion with aversion to cold, fever, and absence of sweating
Cough and wheezing with profuse thin sputum
Wind-water edema presenting in upper body
Wind-cold-damp bi with painful joints and limbs
Yin-type carbuncles and flowing abscesses (when combined with warming herbs)

contraindications_zh: 表虚自汗、阴虚盗汗、肺虚作喘者均忌服。高血压、心脏病、失眠及甲亢患者慎用。
contraindications_en: Contraindicated in exterior vacuity with spontaneous sweating, yin vacuity with night sweats, and lung vacuity with dyspnea. Use with caution in patients with hypertension, heart disease, insomnia, and hyperthyroidism.

dosage_range.notes: 发汗解表宜生用；平喘止咳多蜜炙；一般用2–9g，体质虚弱及老人儿童宜减量

processing_methods.name: 生麻黄
processing_methods.effect: 发汗解表、利水消肿之力强

processing_methods.name: 蜜麻黄
processing_methods.effect: 缓和辛散之性，润肺止咳平喘力胜，多用于表证已解而咳喘未愈

safety_notes_zh: 本品含麻黄碱，能兴奋中枢神经、升高血压、加快心率。过量可致失眠、心悸、血压升高。运动员禁用（列入兴奋剂名单）。单胺氧化酶抑制剂服用者禁用。孕妇慎用。
safety_notes_en: This herb contains ephedrine, which stimulates the central nervous system, raises blood pressure, and increases heart rate. Overdose may cause insomnia, palpitations, and elevated blood pressure. Prohibited for athletes (listed as a banned stimulant). Contraindicated for those taking monoamine oxidase inhibitors (MAOIs). Use with caution during pregnancy.

classical_references.passage: 味苦，温。主中风伤寒头痛，温疟，发表出汗，去邪热气，止咳逆上气，除寒热，破癥坚积聚。

description_zh: 麻黄科植物草麻黄、中麻黄或木贼麻黄的干燥草质茎。归肺、膀胱经。为辛温解表发汗之峻剂，宣肺平喘之要药，伤寒太阳表实证主药。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 麻黄主含麻黄碱类拟交感胺生物碱，具支气管扩张、升压、发汗、利尿及中枢兴奋作用。
summary.en: Ephedra contains ephedrine-type sympathomimetic alkaloids producing bronchodilation, pressor, diaphoretic, diuretic, and CNS-stimulant effects.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 支气管哮喘（历史药理学用途）
鼻塞与上呼吸道充血（伪麻黄碱）

evidence_notes.note_zh: 麻黄碱具明显心血管和中枢兴奋作用；禁用于高血压、冠心病、甲亢、青光眼、前列腺肥大及与 MAO-I 合用；美国等多个司法辖区已限制或禁止膳食补充剂使用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Ephedrine has marked cardiovascular and CNS stimulation; contraindicated in hypertension, coronary disease, hyperthyroidism, glaucoma, prostatic hypertrophy, and with MAO-inhibitors; dietary supplement use is restricted or banned in the US and other jurisdictions.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 麻黄碱激动 α 和 β 肾上腺素受体，产生支气管扩张和升压作用；总碱量显著影响剂量。
evidence_notes.note_en: Ephedrine activates α- and β-adrenergic receptors, producing bronchodilation and pressor effects; total alkaloid content critically determines dosing.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 麻黄辛温发汗解表、宣肺平喘、利水消肿，表虚、阴虚及高血压者忌用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Acrid-warm, releases exterior, diffuses lung qi to stop wheezing, and promotes urination; contraindicated in exterior vacuity, yin vacuity, and hypertension.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》规定麻黄碱和伪麻黄碱总量不得少于 0.80%。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chinese Pharmacopoeia requires combined ephedrine and pseudoephedrine ≥0.80%.

pregnancy.note_zh: 麻黄发汗力强，可致子宫兴奋，孕妇禁用。
pregnancy.note_en: Contraindicated in pregnancy. Strong diaphoretic action may stimulate uterine excitability.

lactation.note_zh: 麻黄碱可经乳汁排出，哺乳期慎用，须在合格中医师指导下使用。
lactation.note_en: Ephedrine is excreted in breast milk; use with caution under qualified practitioner supervision.

pediatric.note_zh: 小儿用量须严格控制，过量可致心悸、失眠。
pediatric.note_en: Dosage must be strictly controlled in children; overdose may cause palpitations and insomnia.

description_en: The dried herbaceous stem of Ephedra sinica, E. intermedia, or E. equisetina (Ephedraceae). Enters the Lung and Bladder channels. Pungent, bitter, and warm, it is the most potent diaphoretic for exterior repletion patterns due to wind-cold, the key herb for diffusing the Lung and calming wheezing, and the principal medicinal in Ma Huang Tang for greater yang exterior repletion.

actions.en: Induce sweating and release the muscle layer
Calm surging qi and direct rebellious qi downward

indications.en: Wind-cold exterior vacuity with spontaneous sweating and aversion to wind
Wind-cold exterior repletion without sweating
Pain from cold congealing blood
Phlegm-rheum with dizziness and palpitations
Water accumulation with inhibited urination
Running piglet qi (ben tun)

contraindications_zh: 温热病及阴虚阳盛、血热妄行诸证忌用。孕妇及月经过多者慎用。
contraindications_en: Contraindicated in warm-heat disease, yin vacuity with yang repletion, and reckless movement of blood due to blood heat. Use with caution during pregnancy and in excessive menstruation.

dosage_range.notes: 煎服3–9g；治表证宜生用；温通经脉、助阳化气可用至9–15g。

processing_methods.name: 生桂枝
processing_methods.effect: 发汗解肌，温通经脉

processing_methods.name: 炒桂枝
processing_methods.effect: 缓和辛散之性，偏于温通

safety_notes_zh: 辛温助热，温热病、阴虚阳盛、血热妄行诸证忌用。孕妇、月经过多者慎用。现代研究显示桂枝过敏者偶见。过量可致头晕、眼花、咳嗽等。
safety_notes_en: Acrid and warm, augments heat; contraindicated in warm-heat disease, yin vacuity with yang repletion, and reckless movement of blood due to blood heat. Use with caution during pregnancy and in excessive menstruation. Modern research shows occasional allergic reactions to gui zhi. Overdose may cause dizziness, blurred vision, and cough.

classical_references.passage: 牡桂，味辛，温。主上气咳逆，结气喉痹，吐吸，利关节，补中益气。久服通神，轻身不老。

description_zh: 樟科植物肉桂的干燥嫩枝。归心、肺、膀胱经。为发散风寒、温通经脉之要药；与白芍相须为用调和营卫（桂枝汤），与茯苓相配温阳化气利水（苓桂术甘汤）。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 桂枝主含挥发油，以桂皮醛为主要活性成分，药理示其解热发汗、扩张外周血管、抗炎、抗病毒与抗血小板聚集作用。
summary.en: Guizhi contains essential oil dominated by cinnamaldehyde; pharmacology shows antipyretic-diaphoretic, peripheral vasodilation, anti-inflammatory, antiviral and antiplatelet effects.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Cold joint and shoulder-arm bi pain

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 月经不调、痛经与胸痹辅助
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Dysmenorrhea, menstrual irregularity and chest bi (adjunct)

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》以桂皮醛含量作为含量测定指标。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chinese Pharmacopoeia uses cinnamaldehyde as the assay marker.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 桂枝汤被誉为《伤寒论》群方之首，桂枝为其君药。
evidence_notes.note_en: Guizhi Tang is considered the chief formula of the Shanghan Lun with guizhi as sovereign herb.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 温热病、阴虚阳盛、血热妄行及孕妇慎用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Avoid in warm-heat disease, yin-vacuity with yang repletion, blood-heat bleeding and pregnancy.

description_en: The dried tender twig of Cinnamomum cassia (Lauraceae). Enters the Heart, Lung, and Bladder channels. Pungent, sweet, and warm, it releases the muscle layer and harmonizes the constructive and defensive, warms and unblocks the channels, and assists yang transformation of qi. Its pairing with bai shao in Gui Zhi Tang to harmonize ying and wei established the archetype of the harmonizing method.

actions.en: Soften the Liver and alleviate pain

indications.en: Blood vacuity with sallow complexion and irregular menstruation
Spontaneous sweating and night sweats

indications.zh: 胁痛、腹痛、四肢挛急疼痛
indications.en: Hypochondriac pain, abdominal pain, and cramping pain of the extremities
Headache and dizziness from ascendant Liver yang
Liver-Spleen disharmony with abdominal pain and diarrhea

contraindications_zh: 不宜与藜芦同用（十八反）。阳衰虚寒之证及中寒腹痛泄泻者慎用。
contraindications_en: Should not be used concurrently with li lu (Veratri Radix et Rhizoma) per the Eighteen Antagonisms (shiba fan). Use with caution in yang decline with vacuity cold and cold in the middle with abdominal pain and diarrhea.

dosage_range.notes: 养血柔肝常用6–15g；平肝止痛、敛阴止汗可用至15–30g；大剂量缓急止痛可用30g以上。

processing_methods.name: 生白芍
processing_methods.effect: 养血敛阴，平肝止痛之力较强

processing_methods.name: 炒白芍
processing_methods.effect: 药性和缓，养血和营，多用于脾虚泄泻

processing_methods.name: 酒白芍
processing_methods.effect: 减其寒性，善于和中缓急，调经止痛

safety_notes_zh: 性微寒而酸敛，虚寒证及中寒腹痛泄泻者慎服。与藜芦相反，不可同用。现代研究未见明显毒性，但长期大量使用应注意配伍。
safety_notes_en: Slightly cold and sour-astringent in nature; use with caution in vacuity cold patterns and cold in the middle with abdominal pain and diarrhea. Antagonizes li lu (Veratri Radix et Rhizoma); must not be used together. Modern research shows no significant toxicity, but prolonged high-dose use requires attention to formulation.

classical_references.passage: 味苦，平。主邪气腹痛，除血痹，破坚积，寒热疝瘕，止痛，利小便，益气。

description_zh: 毛茛科植物芍药的干燥根，水煮去皮后晒干。归肝、脾经。为养血柔肝、缓急止痛之要药，与桂枝相须为用以调和营卫，与甘草相伍为芍药甘草汤以缓急止痛。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

active_constituents.notes: principal active constituent, 1.5–7% of dried root
minor in Paeonia lactiflora root compared to cortex

summary.zh: 白芍的主要活性成分为单萜苷类（芍药苷、芍药内酯苷），具有解痉止痛、抗炎、免疫调节及保肝作用；芍药甘草汤的镇痛机制主要归功于芍药苷。
summary.en: Principal constituents of Paeoniae Radix Alba are monoterpene glycosides (paeoniflorin, albiflorin), with antispasmodic, analgesic, anti-inflammatory, immunomodulatory, and hepatoprotective activity; paeoniflorin largely accounts for the analgesic effect of Shaoyao Gancao Tang.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 痛经与平滑肌痉挛性疼痛
类风湿关节炎（白芍总苷制剂）
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Rheumatoid arthritis (total glucosides of paeony preparations)

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 自身免疫性肝炎辅助治疗

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中药学》（Bensky）引用中国临床研究，白芍总苷（TGP）已作为处方药用于类风湿关节炎的辅助治疗。
evidence_notes.note_en: Bensky cites Chinese clinical trials showing total glucosides of paeony (TGP) used as adjunct therapy for rheumatoid arthritis.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 陈氏药理学指出白芍与甘草配伍（芍药甘草汤）具有协同的解痉止痛作用，对平滑肌和骨骼肌痉挛均有效。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen note the synergistic antispasmodic effect of Bai Shao with Gan Cao (Shaoyao Gancao Tang) on both smooth and skeletal muscle.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》2020年版规定白芍按芍药苷（C23H28O11）计含量不得少于1.6%。
evidence_notes.note_en: The 2020 Chinese Pharmacopoeia specifies that Bai Shao must contain not less than 1.6% paeoniflorin (C23H28O11).

description_en: The dried root of Paeonia lactiflora (Ranunculaceae), boiled and peeled before drying. Enters the Liver and Spleen channels. Bitter, sour, and cool, it is the principal herb for nourishing blood, softening the Liver, and moderating spasms to alleviate pain. Paired with gui zhi it harmonizes constructive and defensive qi; paired with gan cao in Shao Yao Gan Cao Tang it relaxes spasm and relieves pain.

actions.en: Course the Liver and resolve depression
Raise yang and lift sinking qi
Reduce fever and check malarial disorders

indications.en: Shaoyang pattern of cold damage with alternating chills and fever
Liver qi constraint with distending pain of the chest and hypochondrium
Irregular menstruation and dysmenorrhea
Sinking of qi with chronic diarrhea and rectal prolapse
Malarial chills and fever

contraindications_zh: 肝阳上亢、肝风内动、阴虚火旺及气机上逆者忌用或慎用。
contraindications_en: Contraindicated or use with caution in liver yang hyperactivity, internal stirring of liver wind, yin vacuity with effulgent fire, and counterflow qi.

dosage_range.notes: 和解退热宜生用，用量较重(6–10g)；疏肝解郁宜醋炙，用量宜中(3–9g)；升阳举陷用量宜小(3–6g)。

processing_methods.name: 生柴胡
processing_methods.effect: 和解退热，解表力强

processing_methods.name: 醋柴胡
processing_methods.effect: 引药入肝，疏肝解郁止痛

processing_methods.name: 鳖血柴胡
processing_methods.effect: 抑制升散，滋阴退虚热

safety_notes_zh: 柴胡升散，肝阳上亢及阴虚火旺者服之可致头目眩晕加重。大剂量或长期服用可引起肝脏不良反应，需监测肝功能。
safety_notes_en: Chai hu has an ascending, dispersing nature; in liver yang hyperactivity and yin vacuity with effulgent fire it may worsen dizziness. High doses or prolonged use may cause adverse hepatic reactions; liver function should be monitored.

classical_references.passage: 味苦，平。主心腹，去肠胃中结气，饮食积聚，寒热邪气，推陈致新。久服轻身明目益精。

description_zh: 伞形科植物柴胡（北柴胡）或狭叶柴胡（南柴胡）的干燥根。归肝、胆经。为和解少阳之要药，小柴胡汤之君药；与黄芩相须为用以和解少阳，与白芍相配以疏肝柔肝。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

active_constituents.notes: principal bioactives; saikosaponin d is hepatoprotective but can be hepatotoxic at high doses

summary.zh: 柴胡的主要活性成分为柴胡皂苷（a、c、d）、挥发油及多糖，具有解热、抗炎、保肝、免疫调节及中枢镇静作用；其注射剂曾广泛用于发热治疗。
summary.en: Bupleuri Radix contains saikosaponins (a, c, d), volatile oil, and polysaccharides, with antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, hepatoprotective, immunomodulatory, and central sedative effects; chai hu injection was long used in China for febrile illness.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 慢性病毒性肝炎（小柴胡汤辅助治疗）
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Chronic viral hepatitis (Xiao Chai Hu Tang adjunct)

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 肝郁气滞型抑郁与经前综合征
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Liver-qi stagnation depression and premenstrual syndrome

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中药学》（Bensky）记载柴胡是治疗少阳证的主药，尤以小柴胡汤为代表；现代研究支持其解热和免疫调节作用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Bensky describes Chai Hu as the chief herb for shaoyang patterns, exemplified by Xiao Chai Hu Tang; modern studies support its antipyretic and immunomodulatory actions.

evidence_notes.note_zh: Brinker 记载日本曾有小柴胡汤与干扰素合用或用于肝硬化患者时发生间质性肺炎和肝损伤的报告，不宜长期大剂量使用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Brinker cites Japanese reports of interstitial pneumonitis and hepatotoxicity when Xiao Chai Hu Tang is combined with interferon or used in cirrhotic patients, cautioning against prolonged high-dose use.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 陈氏药理学指出柴胡皂苷 d 在高剂量时具有溶血性及肝毒性，临床应按规定剂量使用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen note that saikosaponin d is hemolytic and hepatotoxic at high doses, reinforcing the need for dose compliance.

description_en: The dried root of Bupleurum chinense (bei chai hu) or B. scorzonerifolium (nan chai hu) (Apiaceae). Enters the Liver and Gallbladder channels. Bitter, pungent, and cool, it is the essential herb for harmonizing the lesser yang and the chief medicinal in Xiao Chai Hu Tang. Paired with huang qin to harmonize the lesser yang, and with bai shao to course the Liver and soften its rigidity.

indications.en: Damp-warm or summer-warm disease with chest oppression, nausea and vomiting
Damp-heat focal distention, dysentery and jaundice
Lung heat cough, high fever with irritability and thirst
Blood-heat bleeding such as hematemesis and epistaxis
Carbuncles, sores, and toxic swellings
Restless fetus in pregnancy with heat signs

contraindications_zh: 脾胃虚寒、无湿热实火及妊娠胎寒不安者忌用。
contraindications_en: Contraindicated in spleen-stomach vacuity cold, absence of damp-heat or repletion fire, and cold-type restless fetus during pregnancy.

dosage_range.notes: 煎服3–10g；清热燥湿多用生黄芩；安胎多用炒黄芩；止血用炒炭

processing_methods.name: 生黄芩
processing_methods.effect: 清热燥湿，泻火解毒

processing_methods.name: 酒黄芩
processing_methods.effect: 借酒升腾，善清上焦肺热及头目之火

processing_methods.name: 黄芩炭
processing_methods.effect: 清热止血，用于血热出血

safety_notes_zh: 苦寒伤胃，脾虚便溏者慎用。临床偶见肝功能损害报告，长期服用应监测肝酶。
safety_notes_en: Bitter and cold, may injure the stomach; use with caution in spleen vacuity with loose stools. Occasional reports of liver function impairment; liver enzymes should be monitored during prolonged use.

classical_references.passage: 味苦，平。主诸热黄疸，肠澼泄痢，逐水，下血闭，恶疮疽蚀火疡。

description_zh: 唇形科植物黄芩的干燥根。归肺、胆、脾、大肠、小肠经。为清上焦肺热与少阳胆热之要药，亦为中医安胎之常用药。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 黄芩主含黄酮类成分（黄芩苷、黄芩素等），具有抗炎、抗病毒、抗氧化、保肝及解热作用。
summary.en: Scutellaria yields flavonoids led by baicalin and baicalein with anti-inflammatory, antiviral, antioxidant, hepatoprotective, and antipyretic activity.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 上呼吸道病毒感染辅助治疗
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Adjunct for upper respiratory viral infection

evidence_notes.note_zh: 黄芩苷抑制 NF-κB 通路，对流感、冠状病毒等显示体外抑制作用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Baicalin inhibits NF-κB signaling and shows in vitro activity against influenza and coronaviruses.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 长期或大剂量含黄芩复方补充剂已有肝损伤病例报告；肝病患者慎用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Cases of hepatotoxicity reported with long-term, high-dose Scutellaria-containing supplements; caution in hepatic disease.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 经典配伍用于清上焦湿热、肺热咳嗽及胎热不安。
evidence_notes.note_en: Classically paired to clear upper-burner damp-heat, lung-heat cough, and restless fetus from heat.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》规定黄芩苷含量不得少于 9.0%。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chinese Pharmacopoeia specifies baicalin content ≥9.0%.

description_en: The dried root of Scutellaria baicalensis (Lamiaceae). Enters the Lung, Gallbladder, Spleen, Large Intestine, and Small Intestine channels. Bitter and cold, it is the key herb for clearing heat from the upper burner Lung and lesser yang Gallbladder, draining fire and resolving toxicity, and calming the fetus. It forms a classical pairing with chai hu for lesser yang harmonization.

actions.en: Restore the pulse and stem collapse
Tonify the Spleen and augment the Lung
Calm the spirit and benefit the mind

indications.en: Collapse with cold extremities and faint pulse
Spleen vacuity with reduced appetite and fatigue
Lung qi vacuity with wheezing, cough, and shortness of breath
Fluid damage with thirst and wasting-thirst disorder
Qi and blood vacuity from chronic illness and emaciation
Palpitations, insomnia, and forgetfulness from Heart qi vacuity

contraindications_zh: 不宜与藜芦同用（十八反），不宜与五灵脂同用（十九畏）。服用期间不宜饮茶及食萝卜（减效）。实证、热证而正气不虚者忌服。
contraindications_en: Should not be used concurrently with li lu (Veratri Radix et Rhizoma) per the Eighteen Antagonisms (shiba fan), and should not be used with wu ling zhi (Trogopterori Faeces) per the Nineteen Incompatibilities (shijiu wei). During administration, tea and radish should be avoided as they may reduce efficacy. Contraindicated in repletion and heat patterns when the zheng qi is not deficient.

dosage_range.notes: 一般煎服3–9g，宜文火另煎兑服；研末吞服1–2g

pregnancy.note_zh: 妊娠期使用须由合格中医师评估；红参性偏温，过量可助阳化热，不宜自行服用。
pregnancy.note_en: Use during pregnancy requires assessment by a qualified TCM practitioner. Red ginseng is warming and may aggravate heat patterns; do not self-administer.

lactation.note_zh: 哺乳期资料有限，须在合格中医师指导下使用。
lactation.note_en: Limited lactation data; use only under qualified practitioner supervision.

pediatric.note_zh: 小儿脏腑娇嫩，不宜自行服用人参。任何儿童用药须由合格中医师或儿科医师评估。
pediatric.note_en: Children require specialist dosing. Do not self-administer ginseng to children; any pediatric use must be assessed by a qualified TCM practitioner or pediatrician.

acute_use_note.context_zh: 独参汤、参附汤等古方载用于元气虚脱、肢冷脉微之急症。
acute_use_note.context_en: Classical formulae such as Du Shen Tang and Shen Fu Tang are recorded for acute qi collapse with cold extremities and faint pulse.
acute_use_note.classical_dose_zh: 古籍记载急救虚脱可用 15–30g 浓煎顿服。此为历史文献剂量记录，仅供学术参考；现代急重症须由合格医师在医疗机构处置，不得以本条目为临床依据。

processing_methods.name: 生晒参
processing_methods.effect: 性较平和，补气生津，适用于气阴两伤

processing_methods.name: 红参
processing_methods.effect: 性偏温，补阳益气，适用于阳虚气弱、久病体虚

safety_notes_zh: 滥用可致人参滥用综合征（高血压、失眠、神经过敏、腹泻、皮疹）。高血压、实热证及新感外邪者忌服。与华法林合用可能降低其抗凝效果。
safety_notes_en: Abuse may cause ginseng abuse syndrome (hypertension, insomnia, nervousness, diarrhea, skin rash). Contraindicated in hypertension, repletion heat patterns, and new contraction of external pathogens. Concurrent use with warfarin may reduce its anticoagulant effect.

classical_references.passage: 味甘，微寒。主补五脏，安精神，定魂魄，止惊悸，除邪气，明目开心益智。久服轻身延年。

description_zh: 五加科植物人参的干燥根及根茎。归脾、肺、心、肾经。大补元气之要药，被誉为百草之王，为挽救元气虚脱之第一良药（独参汤）。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 人参主含人参皂苷（Rb1、Rg1、Rg3 等）及多糖，为典型的适应原药物，现代研究涉及抗疲劳、免疫调节、神经保护、降血糖等；并有显著的药物-药物相互作用（CYP 酶、华法林）需关注。
summary.en: Panax ginseng contains ginsenosides (Rb1, Rg1, Rg3, etc.) and panaxan polysaccharides. It is a classical adaptogen with preclinical and clinical research on fatigue, immunomodulation, neuroprotection, and glucose regulation; clinically significant drug interactions (CYP enzymes, warfarin) require attention.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 气虚欲脱、休克辅助（参附汤等）
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Collapse of qi, adjunct in shock (e.g. Shen Fu Tang)

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 2 型糖尿病血糖控制辅助
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Adjunct for type 2 diabetes glycemic control

evidence_notes.note_zh: WHO 专著 Radix Ginseng 收录其用于体虚、疲劳及恢复期的适应证，并记载失眠、高血压的慎用情况。
evidence_notes.note_en: WHO monograph on Radix Ginseng lists indications for debility, fatigue, and convalescence, noting cautions in insomnia and hypertension.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《草药禁忌》（Brinker）指出人参可经 CYP3A4/2D6 影响药物代谢，并可能降低华法林的抗凝效果；与 MAOI（苯乙肼）有病例报告不良反应。
evidence_notes.note_en: Brinker notes Panax ginseng may alter drug metabolism via CYP3A4/2D6 and has been reported to reduce warfarin's anticoagulant effect; case reports describe adverse reactions with MAOIs (phenelzine).

evidence_notes.note_zh: Chen & Chen 记载 Rg1 偏兴奋，Rb1 偏镇静，临床反映其「双向调节」特性。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen note that Rg1 tends to be stimulating while Rb1 is sedating, supporting the clinical impression of bidirectional regulation.

description_en: The dried root and rhizome of Panax ginseng (Araliaceae). Enters the Spleen, Lung, Heart, and Kidney channels. Sweet, slightly bitter, and warm, it powerfully supplements original qi, rescues from collapse, and tonifies the Spleen and Lung. Honored as the 'King of Herbs,' it is the first-choice medicinal for rescuing devastated qi, as in Du Shen Tang (Solitary Ginseng Decoction).

indications.en: Repletion-heat constipation with accumulation and abdominal pain
Damp-heat dysentery, jaundice with scanty dark urine
Blood heat causing hematemesis/epistaxis, red eyes and sore throat
Blood stasis amenorrhea and postpartum stasis
Traumatic injury and intestinal abscess
Heat-toxin sores, burns (topical)

contraindications_zh: 妇人怀孕、月经期、哺乳期慎用或忌用。脾胃虚寒、血虚气弱及无实热积滞、瘀血者忌服。
contraindications_en: Use with caution or avoid during pregnancy, menstruation, and lactation. Contraindicated in spleen-stomach vacuity cold, blood vacuity with qi weakness, and absence of repletion heat accumulation or blood stasis.

dosage_range.notes: 煎服3–15g；用于泻下通便宜后下（煎5–10分钟）或开水泡服；外用适量。研末吞服每次0.5–2g。

processing_methods.name: 生大黄
processing_methods.effect: 泻下攻积力强，清热泻火解毒

processing_methods.name: 酒大黄
processing_methods.effect: 泻下力缓，善清上焦血分热毒，活血祛瘀

processing_methods.name: 熟大黄
processing_methods.effect: 泻下力减，偏于活血祛瘀，多用于瘀血证

processing_methods.name: 大黄炭
processing_methods.effect: 凉血化瘀止血

safety_notes_zh: 苦寒攻下，易伤胃气。脾胃虚寒者不宜。孕妇、月经期、哺乳期妇女忌用或慎用。长期服用可致继发性便秘、结肠黑变病。大剂量可引起腹痛、腹泻、恶心呕吐。
safety_notes_en: Bitter and cold with a purgative action; may easily damage stomach qi. Not suitable in spleen-stomach vacuity cold. Contraindicated or use with caution during pregnancy, menstruation, and lactation. Prolonged use may cause secondary constipation and melanosis coli. High doses may cause abdominal pain, diarrhea, nausea, and vomiting.

classical_references.passage: 味苦，寒。主下瘀血，血闭寒热，破癥瘕积聚，留饮宿食，荡涤肠胃，推陈致新，通利水谷，调中化食，安和五脏。

description_zh: 蓼科植物掌叶大黄、唐古特大黄或药用大黄的干燥根及根茎。归脾、胃、大肠、肝、心包经。为泻下攻积之要药，被誉为“将军”，《伤寒论》大承气汤、桃核承气汤等以之为君。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

active_constituents.notes: principal laxative prodrugs, cleaved by colonic flora to rheinanthrone
astringent secondary effect that can cause rebound constipation

summary.zh: 大黄含蒽醌类（大黄酸、大黄素、芦荟大黄素）、番泻苷及鞣质，具有泻下、抗菌、抗炎、止血、利胆及一定抗肿瘤作用；泻下作用主要由番泻苷在结肠经细菌代谢释放。
summary.en: Rhei Radix et Rhizoma contains anthraquinones (rhein, emodin, aloe-emodin), sennosides, and tannins, with stimulant laxative, antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, hemostatic, choleretic, and some antineoplastic activity; the laxative effect is mediated by colonic bacterial conversion of sennosides to rheinanthrone.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 急性便秘及术前肠道准备
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Acute constipation and preoperative bowel preparation

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 上消化道出血（生大黄粉鼻饲）
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Upper gastrointestinal bleeding (raw powder via nasogastric tube)

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 急性胰腺炎及肠梗阻辅助治疗
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Adjunct for acute pancreatitis and bowel obstruction

evidence_notes.note_zh: WHO 专论收录大黄作为短期使用的刺激性泻药，并警告不宜长期使用以免电解质紊乱与结肠黑变病。
evidence_notes.note_en: WHO monographs document Rhubarb as a short-term stimulant laxative and warn against chronic use due to risk of electrolyte disturbance and pseudomelanosis coli.

evidence_notes.note_zh: Brinker 提醒妊娠期、哺乳期及肠梗阻患者禁用大黄，长期使用可致低钾血症和依赖性。
evidence_notes.note_en: Brinker notes contraindications in pregnancy, lactation, and bowel obstruction; chronic use can cause hypokalemia and laxative dependence.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中药学》（Bensky）指出大黄后下（约 5–10 分钟）以保留泻下作用，久煎则鞣质成分突出，作用由泻转涩。
evidence_notes.note_en: Bensky notes that Da Huang should be added late (5–10 min) to preserve its purgative action; prolonged decoction shifts the effect toward astringent due to tannin release.

pregnancy.note_zh: 大黄苦寒攻下，孕妇禁用。泻下作用可致子宫收缩，有流产之虞。
pregnancy.note_en: Contraindicated in pregnancy. The purgative action may stimulate uterine contractions and risk miscarriage.

lactation.note_zh: 大黄泻下成分可经乳汁排出，致婴儿腹泻。哺乳期禁用。
lactation.note_en: Purgative constituents are excreted in breast milk and may cause diarrhea in the nursing infant. Avoid during lactation.

pediatric.note_zh: 小儿脾胃脆弱，非必要不宜用大黄。确需使用须由合格中医师严格控制剂量。
pediatric.note_en: Children have delicate digestion; da huang should only be used when clearly necessary under strict dosage control by a qualified practitioner.

description_en: The dried root and rhizome of Rheum palmatum, R. tanguticum, or R. officinale (Polygonaceae). Enters the Spleen, Stomach, Large Intestine, Liver, and Pericardium channels. Bitter and cold, it is the foremost purgative for draining accumulation, clearing heat, cooling blood, and expelling stasis. Known as 'The General,' it serves as chief in Zhang Zhongjing's Da Cheng Qi Tang and Tao He Cheng Qi Tang.

actions.en: Regulate qi and fortify the Spleen
Direct rebellious qi downward and harmonize the Stomach
Reduce food stagnation and open the appetite

indications.zh: 脾胃气滞，脘腹胀满，食少呕吐
indications.en: Spleen-Stomach qi stagnation with epigastric distention, reduced appetite, and vomiting
Damp-phlegm or cold-phlegm cough with copious sputum
Chest bi with qi stagnation
Food stagnation with belching and acid regurgitation
Phlegm-damp obstructing the middle with dizziness

contraindications_zh: 气虚体燥、阴虚燥咳及吐血者慎用。温病热证者忌用。
contraindications_en: Use with caution in qi vacuity with dryness, yin-deficient dry cough, and hematemesis. Contraindicated in warm disease with heat patterns.

dosage_range.notes: 煎服3–10g；理气健脾可用3–6g；化痰止咳可用6–10g。陈久者良，陈放三年以上称“陈皮”。

processing_methods.name: 生陈皮
processing_methods.effect: 理气燥湿化痰之力较强

processing_methods.name: 炒陈皮
processing_methods.effect: 缓和燥性，增强健脾和胃之功

safety_notes_zh: 辛温香燥，阴虚津亏、内热燥渴及咯血者慎用。现代研究未见明显毒性，但过量或长期使用可耗气伤津。
safety_notes_en: Acrid, warm, aromatic, and drying; use with caution in yin vacuity with fluid depletion, internal heat with dry thirst, and hemoptysis. Modern research shows no significant toxicity, but excessive or prolonged use may consume qi and damage fluids.

classical_references.passage: 橘柚，味辛，温。主胸中瘕热逆气，利水谷。久服去臭，下气，通神。

description_zh: 芸香科植物橘及其栽培变种的干燥成熟果皮。归脾、肺经。陈久者良，故称陈皮，为理气健脾、燥湿化痰之要药；与半夏相须为用即二陈汤之主药。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

active_constituents.notes: major component of peel essential oil
trace amounts in Citrus reticulata pericarp

summary.zh: 陈皮含挥发油（柠檬烯）、黄酮类（橙皮苷、川陈皮素、橘皮素）及微量辛弗林，具有促胃肠动力、祛痰、抗炎及抗氧化作用。
summary.en: Citri reticulatae Pericarpium contains essential oil (limonene), flavonoids (hesperidin, nobiletin, tangeretin), and trace synephrine, with prokinetic, expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activity.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 功能性消化不良与脾虚气滞腹胀
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Functional dyspepsia and spleen-qi stagnation bloating

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中药学》（Bensky）指出陈皮越陈越良，陈化过程中刺激性挥发油减少，理气和中之效更显。
evidence_notes.note_en: Bensky notes the traditional preference for aged Chen Pi, as aging reduces irritant volatile components while preserving qi-regulating activity.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 陈氏药理学记载陈皮多甲氧基黄酮（川陈皮素、橘皮素）具有显著的抗炎及抗肿瘤活性。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen report that polymethoxylated flavones (nobiletin, tangeretin) show significant anti-inflammatory and antineoplastic activity in preclinical studies.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》2020年版以橙皮苷作为陈皮的含量测定指标，要求不得少于 3.5%。
evidence_notes.note_en: The 2020 Chinese Pharmacopoeia specifies hesperidin as the marker with a minimum content of 3.5% in Chen Pi.

description_en: The dried mature pericarp of Citrus reticulata and its cultivars (Rutaceae). Enters the Spleen and Lung channels. Pungent, bitter, and warm, aged specimens are preferred (hence the name 'aged peel'). It is the primary herb for regulating qi, fortifying the Spleen, drying dampness, and transforming phlegm. Paired with ban xia it forms the core of Er Chen Tang for phlegm disorders.

actions.en: Moisten the intestines and unblock the bowels

indications.en: Heat entering ying and blood levels with maculopapular rash
Crimson tongue with vexation, thirst, and impaired consciousness

indications.zh: 血热吐血衄血，尿血便血
indications.en: Blood heat causing hematemesis, epistaxis, hematuria, and bloody stool
Fluid damage with thirst and wasting-thirst disorder
Yin-vacuity fever and steaming bone tidal fever
Constipation from intestinal dryness due to fluid depletion

contraindications_zh: 脾虚湿滞、腹满便溏者不宜使用。
contraindications_en: Not suitable in spleen vacuity with dampness stagnation, abdominal fullness, and loose stools.

dosage_range.notes: 鲜品用量加倍至12–30g，捣汁饮用；煎服干品9–15g；清热凉血宜生用

processing_methods.name: 鲜地黄
processing_methods.effect: 清热生津、凉血之力较胜，多用于热病伤津

processing_methods.name: 生地黄
processing_methods.effect: 清热凉血、养阴生津

safety_notes_zh: 性寒滋腻，脾胃虚弱、食少便溏、胸闷多痰者不宜使用。不宜与萝卜、葱、蒜同食（古训）。
safety_notes_en: Cold and enriching-cloying in nature; not suitable in spleen and stomach vacuity with poor appetite, loose stools, chest oppression, and copious phlegm. Should not be taken with radish, scallion, or garlic (traditional precaution).

classical_references.passage: 味甘，寒。主折跌绝筋，伤中，逐血痹，填骨髓，长肌肉。作汤除寒热积聚，除痹。生者尤良。久服轻身不老。

description_zh: 玄参科植物地黄的新鲜或干燥块根。归心、肝、肾经。为清热凉血、养阴生津之要药，温病营血分证之首选。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 生地黄主含环烯醚萜苷类（梓醇等）及多糖，具有免疫调节、抗炎、降血糖、养阴生津作用。
summary.en: Raw Rehmannia contains iridoid glycosides (catalpol) and polysaccharides, with immunomodulatory, anti-inflammatory, hypoglycemic and yin-nourishing activities.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Febrile yin damage with thirst and dry mouth

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 自身免疫性疾病辅助治疗

modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Yin-deficient heat bleeding and maculopapular rashes

evidence_notes.note_zh: 清热凉血养阴生津，主治温病热入营血。
evidence_notes.note_en: Clears heat, cools blood, and nourishes yin; principal for warm disease entering nutritive and blood levels.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 梓醇具有神经保护及降血糖作用，被广泛研究。
evidence_notes.note_en: Catalpol exhibits neuroprotective and hypoglycemic activities extensively documented in pharmacology literature.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》规定生地黄含梓醇不得少于0.20%。
evidence_notes.note_en: The Chinese Pharmacopoeia requires not less than 0.20% catalpol in raw Rehmannia.

description_en: The fresh or dried tuberous root of Rehmannia glutinosa (Scrophulariaceae). Enters the Heart, Liver, and Kidney channels. Sweet, bitter, and cold, it is the foremost herb for clearing heat and cooling blood, nourishing yin and generating fluids, and is the first choice for warm-disease patterns involving the constructive and blood levels.

actions.en: Restore yang and rescue from collapse
Warm the channels and unblock the vessels

indications.en: Yang collapse with cold extremities and faint pulse
Heart yang vacuity with chest bi and cardiac pain
Spleen-Kidney yang vacuity with cold abdominal pain
Kidney yang decline with impotence and cold uterus
Yang-vacuity edema
Cold-damp bi pain
Exterior invasion with underlying yang vacuity

contraindications_zh: 不宜与半夏、瓜蒌、贝母、白蔹、白及同用（十八反）。孕妇忌用。阴虚阳亢、真热假寒证禁用。
contraindications_en: Should not be used concurrently with ban xia, gua lou, bei mu, bai lian, and bai ji per the Eighteen Antagonisms (shiba fan). Contraindicated during pregnancy. Contraindicated in yin vacuity with yang hyperactivity and true heat with false cold patterns.

dosage_range.notes: 煎服3–15g，回阳救逆可用至15–30g；本品有毒，宜先煎30–60分钟至口尝无麻辣感为度。

processing_methods.name: 盐附子
processing_methods.effect: 便于贮存，减毒

processing_methods.name: 黑顺片
processing_methods.effect: 临床最常用，回阳补火散寒

processing_methods.name: 白附片
processing_methods.effect: 毒性较减，功效同黑顺片

processing_methods.name: 淡附片
processing_methods.effect: 适宜浮肿及高血压患者

processing_methods.name: 炮附片
processing_methods.effect: 温阳散寒力强，多用于寒痹

safety_notes_zh: 本品含乌头碱类生物碱，有大毒。必须炮制后入药，且需先煎久煎（30–60分钟以上）以水解乌头碱为毒性较低的苯甲酰乌头原碱及乌头原碱。生附子毒性极强，禁止内服。中毒症状包括口舌麻木、恶心呕吐、心悸、心律失常、肢体麻木，严重者可致心源性猝死。与半夏、瓜蒌、贝母、白蔹、白及相反。孕妇禁用。服后忌酒及茶。
safety_notes_en: This herb contains aconitine alkaloids and is highly toxic. Must be processed before medicinal use and decocted first for a prolonged time (30-60 minutes or more) to hydrolyze aconitine into the less toxic benzoylhypaconine and hypaconine. Raw fu zi is extremely toxic; internal use of the raw form is prohibited. Poisoning symptoms include numbness of the mouth and tongue, nausea and vomiting, palpitations, cardiac arrhythmia, and limb numbness; severe cases may lead to sudden cardiac death. Antagonizes ban xia, gua lou, bei mu, bai lian, and bai ji. Contraindicated during pregnancy. Avoid alcohol and tea after taking this herb.

classical_references.passage: 味辛，温。主风寒咳逆邪气，温中，金疮，破癥坚积聚，血瘕，寒湿踒躄，拘挛膝痛，不能行步。

description_zh: 毛茛科植物乌头的子根的加工品。归心、肾、脾经。为“回阳救逆第一品药”，被誉为“走而不守”之峻药；与干姜相须为用（四逆汤）以回阳救逆，与白术相配以温阳化湿。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 附子（乌头子根加工品）含双酯型二萜生物碱（乌头碱、新乌头碱、次乌头碱）与强心成分去甲乌药碱；炮制可使双酯型碱水解为毒性较低的单酯型与胺醇型，药理示强心、抗休克、镇痛与抗炎作用，但治疗窗窄。
summary.en: Fuzi (processed daughter root of Aconitum) contains highly toxic diester diterpene alkaloids (aconitine, mesaconitine, hypaconitine) plus the cardiotonic higenamine; processing hydrolyzes diester alkaloids to less toxic monoester and amine-alcohol forms. Pharmacology shows cardiotonic, anti-shock, analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects within a narrow therapeutic window.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 亡阳虚脱、心力衰竭与心源性休克辅助
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Yang collapse, heart failure and cardiogenic shock (adjunct, with strict supervision)
Cold-damp arthralgia and cold joint pain

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》严格限定乌头碱、次乌头碱、新乌头碱总量，并规定炮制品（黑顺片、白附片、炮附片）的使用，必须先煎久煎。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chinese Pharmacopoeia enforces strict upper limits on total diester alkaloid content; only processed forms (heishunpian, baifupian, paofupian) are permitted and must be decocted first and long.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 中毒表现为口舌麻木、心律失常（室性心动过速、室颤）、低血压甚至死亡；治疗窗窄，孕妇禁用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Toxicity presents as perioral numbness, ventricular arrhythmias (VT/VF), hypotension and death; narrow therapeutic window; contraindicated in pregnancy.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 不可与半夏、瓜蒌、贝母、白蔹、白及同用（十八反）；与降压、抗心律失常药需谨慎配合。
evidence_notes.note_en: Classical eighteen-antagonism: avoid with banxia, gualou, beimu, bailian, baiji; caution with antihypertensives and antiarrhythmics.

evidence_notes.note_zh: WHO 专论确认其严重心脏毒性与全球多起中毒案例。
evidence_notes.note_en: WHO monograph documents serious cardiotoxicity and numerous global poisoning case reports.

pregnancy.note_zh: 附子大辛大热，有毒，孕妇禁用。
pregnancy.note_en: Contraindicated in pregnancy. Fuzi is acrid, hot, and toxic.

description_en: The processed lateral root of Aconitum carmichaelii (Ranunculaceae). Enters the Heart, Kidney, and Spleen channels. Pungent, sweet, and hot, it is the supreme medicinal for restoring yang and rescuing from collapse. A potent and mobile agent, it pairs with gan jiang in Si Ni Tang to rescue depleted yang, and with bai zhu to warm yang and transform dampness.

actions.en: Ascend to the head and eyes and regulate menstruation below

indications.en: Irregular menstruation, amenorrhea, and dysmenorrhea
Stabbing chest and hypochondriac pain; traumatic injury
Headache and wind-damp bi pain
Postpartum abdominal pain from blood stasis
Abdominal masses and accumulations

contraindications_zh: 阴虚火旺、舌红口干、多汗及月经过多者不宜使用。孕妇慎用。气升痰喘不宜。
contraindications_en: Not suitable in yin vacuity with effulgent fire, red tongue with dry mouth, excessive sweating, and excessive menstruation. Use with caution during pregnancy. Not suitable in ascending qi with phlegm-dyspnea.

dosage_range.notes: 煎服3–10g；研末吞服每次1–1.5g。治头痛常用3–6g，活血行气用6–10g。

processing_methods.name: 生川芎
processing_methods.effect: 活血行气，祛风止痛

processing_methods.name: 酒川芎
processing_methods.effect: 增强活血通经止痛之功

safety_notes_zh: 辛温香窜，升散力强，阴虚火旺者服之易致头目眩晕加重。用量过大可引起呕吐、眩晕。孕妇及月经过多者慎用。
safety_notes_en: Acrid, warm, aromatic, and penetrating with strong ascending-dispersing action; in yin vacuity with effulgent fire, it may worsen dizziness. Excessive dosage may cause vomiting and dizziness. Use with caution during pregnancy and in excessive menstruation.

classical_references.passage: 芎䓖，味辛，温。主中风入脑，头痛，寒痹，筋挛缓急，金疮，妇人血闭无子。

description_zh: 伞形科植物川芎的干燥根茎。归肝、胆、心包经。为血中之气药，被誉为“头痛要药”，上行头目、下调经水、中开郁结、旁通络脉。与当归相须为用以养血活血。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

active_constituents.notes: developed as an isolated drug for cerebrovascular disease in China

summary.zh: 川芎的主要活性成分为川芎嗪、藁本内酯、阿魏酸等，具有扩张脑血管、抗血小板聚集、改善微循环、镇痛及抗炎作用；川芎嗪已作为单体药物用于缺血性脑血管疾病。
summary.en: Chuanxiong Rhizoma contains tetramethylpyrazine, ligustilide, ferulic acid, and senkyunolides, with cerebrovasodilator, antiplatelet, microcirculation-enhancing, analgesic, and anti-inflammatory activity; tetramethylpyrazine is marketed in China as an isolated drug for ischemic cerebrovascular disease.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 缺血性脑血管病与冠心病（川芎嗪制剂）
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Ischemic cerebrovascular disease and coronary artery disease (tetramethylpyrazine preparations)

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中药学》（Bensky）引用中国临床研究支持川芎嗪注射剂用于缺血性中风的辅助治疗。
evidence_notes.note_en: Bensky cites Chinese clinical trials supporting tetramethylpyrazine injection as an adjunct for ischemic stroke.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 陈氏药理学指出川芎与抗凝药（如华法林）合用时可能增加出血风险。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen caution that co-administration of Chuan Xiong with anticoagulants (e.g., warfarin) may increase bleeding risk.

evidence_notes.note_zh: Brinker 提到川芎的阴虚火旺及月经过多者慎用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Brinker advises caution in patients with yin vacuity with effulgent fire and in menorrhagia due to blood-moving activity.

description_en: The dried rhizome of Ligusticum chuanxiong (Apiaceae). Enters the Liver, Gallbladder, and Pericardium channels. Pungent and warm, it is described as 'qi within the blood,' ascending to the head and eyes and descending to regulate menses, with the ability to open constraint and penetrate the network vessels. Acclaimed as the essential herb for headache, it pairs with dang gui for nourishing and invigorating blood.

actions.en: Direct rebellious qi downward and stop vomiting
Disperse focal distention and dissipate nodulation
Topically reduce swelling and alleviate pain

indications.en: Damp-phlegm and cold-phlegm cough with copious sputum
Stomach qi rebellion with nausea and vomiting
Focal distention and fullness of the chest and epigastrium
Plum-pit qi (globus hystericus)
Goiter and phlegm nodules

contraindications_zh: 不宜与乌头类药物同用（十八反）。阴虚燥咳、血证、热痰及津伤口渴者禁服。
contraindications_en: Should not be used concurrently with aconite-type herbs per the Eighteen Antagonisms (shiba fan). Contraindicated in yin-deficient dry cough, bleeding disorders, heat-phlegm, and thirst from fluid damage.

dosage_range.notes: 一般3–9g，炮制品如法半夏、姜半夏、清半夏用法略异。生半夏仅供外用，内服需严格炮制。

processing_methods.name: 清半夏
processing_methods.effect: 减毒，长于燥湿化痰

processing_methods.name: 姜半夏
processing_methods.effect: 降逆止呕之功增强

processing_methods.name: 法半夏
processing_methods.effect: 燥性稍缓，偏于燥湿和胃

safety_notes_zh: 生半夏有毒，内服必须炮制。其毒性成分可致口舌麻木、咽喉肿胀。与川乌、草乌、附子相反，不可同用。孕妇慎用。误服生品中毒应以生姜汁、醋漱口及催吐处理。
safety_notes_en: Raw ban xia is toxic; it must be processed before internal use. Its toxic components may cause numbness of the mouth and tongue and throat swelling. Antagonizes chuan wu, cao wu, and fu zi; must not be used together. Use with caution during pregnancy. In case of poisoning from raw herb ingestion, rinse the mouth with fresh ginger juice and vinegar and induce vomiting.

classical_references.passage: 味辛，平。主伤寒寒热，心下坚，下气，喉咽肿痛，头眩，胸张，咳逆，肠鸣，止汗。

description_zh: 天南星科植物半夏的干燥块茎。归脾、胃、肺经。为燥湿化痰、降逆止呕之要药，与陈皮相须为用以理气化痰（二陈汤）。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

active_constituents.notes: irritant components largely inactivated by processing
implicated in mucosal irritation of raw herb

summary.zh: 半夏生品含尿黑酸、刺激性凝集素及少量生物碱，具有强烈的口腔黏膜刺激性；经姜制、矾制或白矾煮制后毒性显著降低，保留其燥湿化痰、降逆止呕作用。
summary.en: Raw Pinelliae Rhizoma contains homogentisic acid, irritant lectins, and trace alkaloids that cause severe oral mucosal irritation; toxicity is substantially reduced by ginger/alum processing, which preserves its phlegm-drying and antiemetic activity.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 妊娠呕吐及化疗诱导性呕吐
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Morning sickness and chemotherapy-induced nausea and vomiting

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中药学》（Bensky）强调半夏必须炮制使用，未经炮制的生半夏对口腔及消化道黏膜有强烈刺激作用，临床仅外用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Bensky emphasizes that Pinellia must be processed before internal use; raw herb is severely irritating to oral and GI mucosa and is used only externally.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 陈氏药理学指出半夏的抗呕吐作用已在临床试验中验证，且与其水提物对胃肠道5-HT3受体的影响有关。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen report that the antiemetic effect of Pinellia has been validated in clinical trials and is linked to 5-HT3 receptor modulation by aqueous extracts.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《草药禁忌》（Brinker）指出半夏传统与乌头反，并列入十八反；妊娠期使用应以姜半夏等炮制品为限，并由合格医师指导。
evidence_notes.note_en: Brinker notes the traditional 'eighteen incompatibilities' prohibition against combining with Aconitum species, and that pregnancy use should be limited to processed forms under professional supervision.

pregnancy.note_zh: 半夏有毒，孕妇禁用。传统列为妊娠禁忌药。
pregnancy.note_en: Contraindicated in pregnancy. Traditionally classified as a pregnancy-prohibited substance.

lactation.note_zh: 哺乳期资料有限，须在合格中医师指导下使用。
lactation.note_en: Limited lactation data; use only under qualified practitioner supervision.

pediatric.note_zh: 小儿慎用，须严格控制剂量并用制半夏。
pediatric.note_en: Use with caution in children; strict dosage control required, use only processed forms.

description_en: The dried tuber of Pinellia ternata (Araceae). Enters the Spleen, Stomach, and Lung channels. Pungent and warm, it is the key herb for drying dampness and transforming phlegm and for directing rebellious qi downward to stop vomiting. Paired with chen pi it forms the core of Er Chen Tang, the foundational formula for phlegm patterns.

actions.en: Release the exterior and dispel cold
Warm the middle and stop vomiting
Resolve fish and crab toxicity, mitigate Pinellia and Arisaema toxicity

indications.en: Wind-cold common cold with aversion to cold and fever
Stomach cold with vomiting
Cold phlegm cough
Food poisoning from fish and crab
Morning sickness with vomiting in pregnancy

contraindications_zh: 阴虚内热及热盛者忌用。
contraindications_en: Contraindicated in yin vacuity with internal heat and repletion heat.

dosage_range.notes: 煎服3–9g，或捣汁冲服；外用适量

processing_methods.name: 生姜
processing_methods.effect: 解表散寒、温中止呕、化痰止咳

processing_methods.name: 煨姜
processing_methods.effect: 温中止呕、止泻作用强而发散力减，用于脾胃虚寒腹痛吐泻

safety_notes_zh: 阴虚火旺、目疾、痔疮、痈肿疮疖者不宜多食。晚间不宜过量食用（古谚：晚吃姜似砒霜）。
safety_notes_en: Not suitable for excessive consumption in yin vacuity with effulgent fire, eye diseases, hemorrhoids, or carbuncles and boils. Should not be consumed in repletion in the evening (traditional proverb: 'eating ginger at night is like taking arsenic').

classical_references.passage: 味辛，温。主胸满咳逆上气，温中止血，出汗，逐风湿痹，肠澼下痢。生者尤良。久服去臭气，通神明。

description_zh: 姜科植物姜的新鲜根茎。归肺、脾、胃经。被誉为呕家圣药，亦为半夏、天南星中毒之首选解药，常作为配伍中的引药调和诸药。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 生姜主含姜辣素及挥发油成分，具有止吐、抗炎、促胃动力及抗血小板聚集作用。
summary.en: Fresh ginger contains gingerols, shogaols and volatile sesquiterpenes with antiemetic, anti-inflammatory, prokinetic and antiplatelet activities.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 妊娠呕吐、晕动病及化疗恶心
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Nausea of pregnancy, motion sickness and chemotherapy-induced nausea

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 骨关节炎疼痛辅助治疗

modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Wind-cold common cold and cold-stomach vomiting

evidence_notes.note_zh: WHO专论收录生姜（Rhizoma Zingiberis Recens）用于晕动病及术后恶心呕吐。
evidence_notes.note_en: WHO monograph lists fresh ginger for motion sickness and post-operative nausea/vomiting.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 抗血小板作用理论上与华法林及抗凝药可能协同，高剂量者需警惕出血倾向。
evidence_notes.note_en: Antiplatelet effect may theoretically potentiate warfarin and anticoagulants; bleeding risk at high doses.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 解表散寒、温中止呕、化痰止咳，称「呕家圣药」。
evidence_notes.note_en: Releases exterior, warms middle to stop vomiting, transforms phlegm; called the 'sage herb for vomiting.'

description_en: The fresh rhizome of Zingiber officinale (Zingiberaceae). Enters the Lung, Spleen, and Stomach channels. Pungent and warm, it is celebrated as the 'sage herb for vomiting' (ou jia sheng yao) and serves as the primary antidote for Pinellia and Arisaema toxicity. Frequently employed as an envoy herb to harmonize the actions of other medicinals in decoctions.

actions.en: Tonify the middle and augment qi
Nourish blood and calm the spirit
Moderate the harshness of other medicinals

indications.zh: 脾胃虚弱，食少便溏，倦怠乏力
indications.en: Spleen-Stomach vacuity with reduced appetite, loose stools, and fatigue
Blood vacuity with sallow complexion
Visceral agitation (zang zao) in women with restlessness
Palpitations, fearful throbbing, and insomnia
Moderating the toxicity of drastic medicinals

contraindications_zh: 湿盛中满、食积虫积、齿病、痰热咳嗽者不宜。糖尿病者慎用。
contraindications_en: Not suitable in dampness repletion with epigastric fullness, food stagnation, parasitic accumulation, dental disease, or phlegm-heat cough. Use with caution in diabetes.

dosage_range.notes: 煎服6–15g（约3–12枚），擘开入煎。大剂量可用至30g。与生姜同煎以调和营卫。

processing_methods.name: 生大枣
processing_methods.effect: 补中益气，养血安神

processing_methods.name: 炒大枣
processing_methods.effect: 性偏温燥，健脾止泻

safety_notes_zh: 甘温助湿，湿盛中满、痰热咳嗽、食积虫积、齿病者不宜。糖尿病患者宜少用。过量食用可致腹胀。
safety_notes_en: Sweet and warm, may promote dampness; not suitable in dampness repletion with epigastric fullness, phlegm-heat cough, food stagnation, parasitic accumulation, or dental disease. Use sparingly in diabetes. Excessive consumption may cause abdominal distention.

classical_references.passage: 味甘，平。主心腹邪气，安中养脾，助十二经，平胃气，通九窍，补少气少津液，身中不足，大惊，四肢重。

description_zh: 鼠李科植物枣的干燥成熟果实。归脾、胃、心经。为补中益气、养血安神之要药，常与生姜相须为用以调和营卫（如桂枝汤）；《金匮要略》甘麦大枣汤用之治疗妇人脏躁。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

active_constituents.notes: related saponins shared with suan zao ren, contributing to mild sedative effect
relatively high content among food herbs

summary.zh: 大枣富含多糖、环腺苷酸（cAMP）、三萜酸及微量皂苷，具有补益脾胃、调和药性、免疫增强及护肝作用，是方剂中常用的调和药。
summary.en: Jujubae Fructus is rich in polysaccharides, cyclic nucleotides (cAMP), triterpenic acids, and trace saponins, with spleen-tonifying, immunomodulatory, and hepatoprotective effects; it is widely used as a harmonizing herb in classical formulas.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Spleen-stomach vacuity with poor appetite and fatigue

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 血虚萎黄及更年期妇女情绪波动（甘麦大枣汤）
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Blood vacuity sallowness and perimenopausal emotional lability (Gan Mai Da Zao Tang)

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 药物毒性缓解（方剂调和）
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Mitigation of harsh drug effects (formula harmonization)

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中药学》（Bensky）指出大枣在经方中常作为调和药，减缓峻烈药物的副作用，如十枣汤中以大枣煎汤送服甘遂、大戟、芫花。
evidence_notes.note_en: Bensky notes that Da Zao serves as a harmonizer that mitigates the harshness of other herbs, as in Shi Zao Tang where jujube decoction is used to administer gan sui, da ji, and yuan hua.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 陈氏药理学记载大枣多糖可增强淋巴细胞增殖并改善化疗后白细胞减少。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen note that Jujube polysaccharides enhance lymphocyte proliferation and ameliorate chemotherapy-induced leukopenia in animal models.

evidence_notes.note_zh: Brinker 提醒糖尿病患者大量食用大枣可能影响血糖控制，湿盛中满者慎用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Brinker cautions that large amounts of Da Zao may affect glycemic control in diabetics and should be used sparingly in dampness with abdominal fullness.

description_en: The dried ripe fruit of Ziziphus jujuba (Rhamnaceae). Enters the Spleen, Stomach, and Heart channels. Sweet and warm, it tonifies the middle, augments qi, nourishes blood, and calms the spirit. Commonly paired with sheng jiang to harmonize constructive and defensive qi, as in Gui Zhi Tang; Zhang Zhongjing's Gan Mai Da Zao Tang uses it to treat visceral agitation (zang zao).

actions.en: Warm the middle and dispel cold
Restore yang and unblock the vessels
Warm the Lung and transform rheum
Warm the channels and stop bleeding （炮姜）

indications.en: Spleen-Stomach vacuity-cold with cold epigastric and abdominal pain
Vomiting and diarrhea from cold
Yang collapse with cold extremities and faint pulse
Cold rheum lodged in the Lung with cough and wheezing
Yang vacuity bleeding, hematemesis, epistaxis, and uterine bleeding

contraindications_zh: 阴虚内热、血热妄行者忌用。孕妇慎用。热证及阴虚火旺者禁用。
contraindications_en: Contraindicated in yin vacuity with internal heat and reckless movement of blood due to blood heat. Use with caution during pregnancy. Contraindicated in heat patterns and yin vacuity with effulgent fire.

dosage_range.notes: 煎服3–10g；回阳救逆可用至10–15g；止血宜炒炭用。

processing_methods.name: 生干姜
processing_methods.effect: 温中散寒，回阳通脉，温肺化饮

processing_methods.name: 炮姜
processing_methods.effect: 性较温和，温经止血，温中止泻，多用于虚寒性出血及腹泻

processing_methods.name: 干姜炭
processing_methods.effect: 专于温经止血

safety_notes_zh: 辛热燥烈，阴虚内热、血热妄行者忌用。孕妇慎用。过量可致咽喉灼痛、口干。与生姜相比，干姜守而不走，温中回阳力胜。
safety_notes_en: Acrid, hot, and intensely drying; contraindicated in yin vacuity with internal heat and reckless movement of blood due to blood heat. Use with caution during pregnancy. Overdose may cause burning throat pain and dry mouth. Compared to fresh ginger, dried ginger is stabilizing rather than dispersing, with superior ability to warm the middle and restore yang.

classical_references.passage: 味辛，温。主胸满咳逆上气，温中止血，出汗，逐风湿痹，肠澼下痢。生者尤良。久服去臭气，通神明。

description_zh: 姜科植物姜的干燥根茎。归脾、胃、肾、心、肺经。守而不走，与附子相须为用以回阳救逆（四逆汤）；与五味子、细辛相配温肺化饮（小青龙汤）。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 干姜含姜辣素类（6-姜酚、6-姜烯酚）与挥发油（姜烯等），药理示其温中散寒、促进消化液分泌、止吐、抗炎、抗血小板与保护胃黏膜作用。
summary.en: Ganjiang contains pungent gingerols (6-gingerol) and shogaols plus the sesquiterpene zingiberene; pharmacology shows warming, digestive stimulation, antiemetic, anti-inflammatory, antiplatelet and gastroprotective effects.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 虚寒性脘腹冷痛、呕吐泄泻
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Cold-vacuity epigastric pain, vomiting and diarrhea
Cough and wheezing from cold phlegm-fluid

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 化疗相关恶心呕吐辅助

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》以 6-姜酚与挥发油含量作为质量评价指标。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chinese Pharmacopoeia uses 6-gingerol and essential oil content as quality markers.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 干姜、生姜、炮姜功效各异：干姜温中，生姜解表，炮姜温经止血。
evidence_notes.note_en: Ganjiang (dried), shengjiang (fresh) and paojiang (blast-fried) differ: dried warms middle, fresh releases exterior, blast-fried warms channels and stops bleeding.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 大剂量可能增强抗凝药作用；阴虚内热、孕妇慎用。
evidence_notes.note_en: High doses may potentiate anticoagulants; cautious use in yin-vacuity heat and pregnancy.

description_en: The dried rhizome of Zingiber officinale (Zingiberaceae). Enters the Spleen, Stomach, Kidney, Heart, and Lung channels. Pungent and hot, it is characterized as 'guarding without roaming' (shou er bu zou), in contrast to the fresh form. It pairs with fu zi in Si Ni Tang to rescue yang, and with wu wei zi and xi xin to warm the Lung and transform thin mucus in Xiao Qing Long Tang.

actions.en: Moisten the intestines and unblock the bowels

indications.en: Cough and wheezing from various causes
Wind-cold cough
Wind-heat cough
Dry-heat cough with scanty sputum
Intestinal dryness with constipation

contraindications_zh: 阴虚咳嗽及大便溏泻者禁服。婴儿慎用。孕妇慎用。
contraindications_en: Contraindicated in yin-deficient cough and loose stools or diarrhea. Use with caution in infants. Use with caution during pregnancy.

dosage_range.notes: 煎服5–10g，宜打碎后下；婴幼儿慎用，用量酌减

processing_methods.name: 生杏仁
processing_methods.effect: 去皮尖入药，润肺止咳平喘

processing_methods.name: 炒杏仁
processing_methods.effect: 缓和寒性，增强止咳作用，用于肺寒咳喘

safety_notes_zh: 本品有小毒，含苦杏仁苷，水解释放氢氰酸。过量服用（尤其生品）可致中毒（头晕、恶心、紫绀、呼吸困难，严重者呼吸麻痹）。成人中毒量40–60粒，小儿10–20粒。婴儿尤其禁忌。
safety_notes_en: This herb is slightly toxic and contains amygdalin, which releases hydrocyanic acid upon hydrolysis. Excessive ingestion (especially of the raw herb) may cause poisoning (dizziness, nausea, cyanosis, dyspnea; severe cases may lead to respiratory paralysis). The toxic dose is 40-60 seeds for adults and 10-20 for children. Especially contraindicated in infants.

classical_references.passage: 味甘，温。主咳逆上气雷鸣，喉痹下气，产乳金疮，寒心奔豚。

description_zh: 蔷薇科植物山杏、西伯利亚杏、东北杏或杏的干燥成熟种子。归肺、大肠经。为治咳喘之要药，随证配伍可用于各种咳喘证。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

active_constituents.notes: hydrolyzed to HCN and benzaldehyde — source of both antitussive action and cyanide toxicity
lubricates intestine, basis of moistening laxative action
hydrolyzes amygdalin; inactivated by proper processing

summary.zh: 苦杏仁主要活性成分为苦杏仁苷，经 β-葡萄糖苷酶水解释放微量氢氰酸，抑制呼吸中枢而止咳平喘；过量则致命，须焯制去皮去苦并严控剂量。
summary.en: The principal constituent of bitter apricot seed is amygdalin, which is hydrolyzed by beta-glucosidase to release small amounts of hydrogen cyanide that mildly depress the respiratory center, producing antitussive and antiasthmatic effects; overdose is lethal, so blanching and strict dose limits are essential.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Cough and wheezing from external or internal causes

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中药学》（Bensky）强调苦杏仁必须焯制、剂量不超 10 g，婴儿禁用生品。
evidence_notes.note_en: Bensky emphasizes that bitter apricot seed must be blanched, doses kept at or below 10 g, and raw seed is contraindicated in infants.

evidence_notes.note_zh: Brinker 详述苦杏仁苷水解产生氰化物的毒性风险，误服生杏仁可致中毒死亡。
evidence_notes.note_en: Brinker details cyanide toxicity from amygdalin hydrolysis; ingestion of raw kernels has caused fatal poisoning.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》以苦杏仁苷为含量测定指标，并规定炮制去毒工艺。
evidence_notes.note_en: The Chinese Pharmacopoeia assays amygdalin content and prescribes detoxifying processing methods.

description_en: The dried mature seed of Prunus armeniaca, P. sibirica, P. mandshurica, or P. armeniaca var. ansu (Rosaceae). Enters the Lung and Large Intestine channels. Bitter and slightly warm, it is the principal herb for stopping cough and calming wheezing, capable of both diffusing and descending Lung qi. With appropriate pairings it addresses cough and dyspnea of any etiology.

actions.en: Release the exterior and vent rashes

indications.en: Wind-heat headache, toothache, and mouth sores
Swollen painful throat
Incomplete eruption of measles
Yang-toxin maculopapular rash

indications.zh: 中气下陷，久泻脱肛，子宫脱垂
indications.en: Sinking of middle qi with chronic diarrhea, rectal or uterine prolapse

contraindications_zh: 麻疹已透、阴虚火旺、肝阳上亢及上盛下虚者忌用。
contraindications_en: Contraindicated when measles rash has fully erupted, in yin vacuity with effulgent fire, liver yang hyperactivity, and upper repletion with lower vacuity.

dosage_range.notes: 煎服3–10g；发表透疹、清热解毒宜生用；升阳举陷宜蜜炙

processing_methods.name: 生升麻
processing_methods.effect: 发表透疹、清热解毒之力较强

processing_methods.name: 蜜升麻
processing_methods.effect: 升阳举陷作用增强，减其发散之性

safety_notes_zh: 升散力强，过量可致头痛、震颤、四肢强直等。阴虚阳浮者误服可引动虚火。
safety_notes_en: Strong ascending-dispersing action; overdose may cause headache, tremor, and limb rigidity. Mistaken use in yin vacuity with floating yang may stir up vacuity fire.

classical_references.passage: 味甘、苦，平、微寒。主解百毒，杀百老物殃鬼，辟温疫瘴气邪气蛊毒。久服不夭。

description_zh: 毛茛科植物大三叶升麻、兴安升麻或升麻的干燥根茎。归肺、脾、胃、大肠经。与柴胡同用为升阳举陷之经典配伍（补中益气汤）。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 升麻主含三萜皂苷（升麻苷）及阿魏酸类，具有抗炎、解热、抗病毒及轻度雌激素样作用。
summary.en: Cimicifuga rhizome contains cycloartane triterpene glycosides and ferulic acid derivatives with anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, antiviral and mild estrogen-modulating activity.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 气虚下陷之脱肛、子宫脱垂
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Qi-sinking prolapse of rectum or uterus

evidence_notes.note_zh: 发表透疹、清热解毒、升举阳气，为补中益气汤要药。
evidence_notes.note_en: Vents rashes, clears heat-toxicity, and raises sunken yang; a key ingredient in Bu Zhong Yi Qi Tang.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 中国升麻（Cimicifuga foetida）不同于北美黑升麻（C. racemosa），用于更年期研究者为后者。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chinese Cimicifuga foetida differs from North American black cohosh (C. racemosa), the species studied for menopause.

description_en: The dried rhizome of Cimicifuga heracleifolia, C. dahurica, or C. foetida (Ranunculaceae). Enters the Lung, Spleen, Stomach, and Large Intestine channels. Pungent, sweet, and cool, it releases the exterior and vents rashes, clears heat and resolves toxicity, and raises sunken yang qi. Its classical pairing with chai hu in Bu Zhong Yi Qi Tang exemplifies the method of raising yang and upbearing the sinking.

indications.en: Blood vacuity with sallow complexion, dizziness, and palpitations
Irregular menstruation and uterine bleeding
Liver-Kidney yin vacuity with weak low back and knees
Steaming bone tidal fever, night sweats, and spermatorrhea
Wasting-thirst disorder, deafness, and blurred vision
Premature graying of hair

contraindications_zh: 脾胃虚弱、气滞痰多、腹满便溏者忌用。不宜与萝卜、葱、蒜同食（古训）。
contraindications_en: Contraindicated in spleen and stomach vacuity, qi stagnation with copious phlegm, abdominal fullness, and loose stools. Should not be taken with radish, scallion, or garlic (traditional precaution).

dosage_range.notes: 煎服9–15g，大剂量可用至30g；滋腻易碍脾，常配砂仁、陈皮以制之

processing_methods.name: 熟地黄
processing_methods.effect: 由生地黄经九蒸九晒（或酒蒸）制成，由清热凉血转为补血滋阴

processing_methods.name: 熟地炭
processing_methods.effect: 滋阴止血，用于虚损性出血

safety_notes_zh: 性黏腻滋补，脾胃虚弱者久服易碍运化。腹胀便溏、痰湿内盛者忌用。服药期间忌食生冷。
safety_notes_en: Rich, cloying, and nourishing; prolonged use in spleen and stomach vacuity may impair transportation and transformation. Contraindicated in abdominal distention with loose stools and internal phlegm-dampness repletion. Avoid raw and cold foods during administration.

classical_references.passage: 味甘，寒。主折跌绝筋，伤中，逐血痹，填骨髓，长肌肉。作汤除寒热积聚，除痹。生者尤良。久服轻身不老。

description_zh: 玄参科植物地黄的块根经黄酒拌蒸加工炮制而成。归肝、肾经。为补血滋阴填精之要药，四物汤、六味地黄丸、左归丸之君药。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 熟地黄经九蒸九晒炮制后，梓醇含量降低而多糖及5-HMF增加，具有补血、免疫调节及造血促进作用。
summary.en: Prepared Rehmannia, steamed and sun-dried repeatedly, has reduced catalpol but elevated polysaccharides and 5-HMF, with blood-tonifying, immunomodulatory and hematopoietic-promoting activity.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Blood-deficient sallow complexion and menstrual irregularity

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 肝肾阴虚腰膝酸软、遗精
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Liver-Kidney yin vacuity with lumbar soreness and spermatorrhea

evidence_notes.note_zh: 滋阴补血、益精填髓，为四物汤、六味地黄丸君药。
evidence_notes.note_en: Nourishes yin, tonifies blood, and replenishes essence; sovereign herb in Si Wu Tang and Liu Wei Di Huang Wan.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 炮制后多糖含量升高，增强免疫和促造血作用为现代主要药理发现。
evidence_notes.note_en: Processing increases polysaccharide content, which underlies much of its modern immunomodulatory and hematopoietic activity.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》熟地黄以毛蕊花糖苷为指标成分。
evidence_notes.note_en: The Chinese Pharmacopoeia uses verbascoside (acteoside) as a marker for prepared Rehmannia.

description_en: The dried tuberous root of Rehmannia glutinosa (Scrophulariaceae), processed by steaming with yellow wine. Enters the Liver and Kidney channels. Sweet and slightly warm, it is the foremost herb for tonifying blood, nourishing yin, and replenishing essence to supplement marrow. It serves as the chief medicinal in Si Wu Tang, Liu Wei Di Huang Wan, and Zuo Gui Wan.

indications.en: Spleen vacuity with reduced appetite and loose stools
Lung vacuity with chronic cough and wheezing
Kidney vacuity with spermatorrhea, leukorrhea, and urinary frequency
Wasting-thirst disorder from vacuity heat
Chronic diarrhea from Spleen vacuity

contraindications_zh: 湿盛中满或有实邪积滞者不宜单用。
contraindications_en: Not suitable as sole agent in dampness repletion with epigastric fullness or repletion pathogen accumulation.

dosage_range.notes: 煎服15–30g，大剂量可用至60–250g（张锡纯善用）；研末吞服每次6–10g；补阴宜生用，健脾止泻宜炒用

processing_methods.name: 生山药
processing_methods.effect: 补肾生精，益肺阴之力较胜

processing_methods.name: 炒山药
processing_methods.effect: 健脾止泻作用增强，用于脾虚泄泻

safety_notes_zh: 性味平和，一般无毒副作用。含尿囊素、薯蓣皂苷，对糖尿病有辅助降糖作用但不可替代药物。鲜品外敷偶见接触性皮炎。
safety_notes_en: Mild in nature; generally without toxic side effects. Contains allantoin and diosgenin saponins; may aid glucose control in diabetes but cannot replace medications. Fresh herb applied topically may occasionally cause contact dermatitis.

classical_references.passage: 味甘，温。主伤中，补虚羸，除寒热邪气，补中益气力，长肌肉。久服耳目聪明，轻身不饥延年。

description_zh: 薯蓣科植物薯蓣的干燥根茎。归脾、肺、肾经。平补肺脾肾三脏，气阴双补而不滋腻，为药食同源之佳品，六味地黄丸之要药。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 山药（薯蓣）含甾体皂苷（薯蓣皂苷元、薯蓣皂苷）、粘液多糖、尿囊素与淀粉酶，现代研究显示其具降血糖、免疫调节、护肠黏膜及抗氧化作用，对应传统补脾养胃、生津益肺、补肾涩精之功。
summary.en: Chinese yam contains steroidal saponins (diosgenin, dioscin), mucilage polysaccharides, allantoin, and amylases, with preclinical evidence for hypoglycemic, immunomodulatory, gastric mucosa-protective, and antioxidant effects, aligning with its traditional spleen-stomach tonifying and kidney-essence securing role.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Spleen vacuity with poor appetite, loose stools, chronic diarrhea

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中药学》（Bensky）指出山药性平味甘，药食两用，为平补三焦之要药。
evidence_notes.note_en: Bensky describes Shan Yao as neutral and sweet, both food and medicine, a key gentle tonic for all three jiao.

evidence_notes.note_zh: Chen & Chen 综述山药多糖在动物糖尿病模型中具降糖及改善胰岛素敏感性作用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen summarize that yam polysaccharides lower blood glucose and improve insulin sensitivity in animal diabetes models.

description_en: The dried rhizome of Dioscorea opposita (Dioscoreaceae). Enters the Spleen, Lung, and Kidney channels. Sweet and neutral, it is a balanced tonic that simultaneously augments qi and nourishes yin across the Spleen, Lung, and Kidney without creating stagnation or greasiness. Widely valued as both food and medicinal, it is a core component of Liu Wei Di Huang Wan.

actions.en: Clear the Heart and eliminate vexation
Clear the Stomach and stop vomiting

indications.en: Damp-heat focal distention with vomiting and acid regurgitation
Damp-heat dysentery with tenesmus
High fever with impaired consciousness and exuberant Heart fire
Vexation and insomnia
Blood-heat bleeding such as hematemesis and epistaxis
Carbuncles, sores, red eyes, and toothache

contraindications_zh: 脾胃虚寒者忌用。苦寒伤胃，易败胃气，不宜久服大量。阴虚津伤者慎用。
contraindications_en: Contraindicated in spleen-stomach vacuity cold. Bitter and cold, may injure the stomach and impair stomach qi; not suitable for prolonged use or large doses. Use with caution in yin vacuity with fluid damage.

dosage_range.notes: 内服煎汤一般2–5g；清心、清胃可用3–6g；外用适量，研末调敷

processing_methods.name: 生黄连
processing_methods.effect: 清热燥湿，泻火解毒之力最强，多用于湿热泻痢、高热神昏

processing_methods.name: 姜黄连
processing_methods.effect: 缓和苦寒之性，和胃止呕，善治胃热呕吐

processing_methods.name: 酒黄连
processing_methods.effect: 借酒上行，善清头目之火，治目赤肿痛、口舌生疮

safety_notes_zh: 本品大苦大寒，过用或久服易伤脾胃阳气。含小檗碱，新生儿及G6PD缺乏者慎用，可诱发溶血。不宜与猪肉同食（古训）。
safety_notes_en: This herb is extremely bitter and cold; excessive or prolonged use may damage spleen and stomach yang qi. Contains berberine; use with caution in neonates and those with G6PD vacuity, as it may induce hemolysis. Should not be taken with pork (traditional precaution).

classical_references.passage: 味苦，寒。主热气目痛，眦伤泣出，明目，肠澼腹痛下痢，妇人阴中肿痛。久服令人不忘。

description_zh: 毛茛科植物黄连、三角叶黄连或云连的干燥根茎。归心、脾、胃、肝、胆、大肠经。为清热燥湿、泻火解毒之要药，尤善清中焦湿热与心火。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 黄连主含异喹啉生物碱（以小檗碱为代表），具广谱抗菌、抗炎、降血糖、调血脂及抗心律失常等现代药理作用。
summary.en: Coptis contains isoquinoline alkaloids led by berberine, with broad-spectrum antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, hypoglycemic, lipid-lowering, and antiarrhythmic pharmacological activity.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 细菌性腹泻与肠道感染

evidence_notes.note_zh: 传统视黄连为苦寒清热燥湿要药，现代研究证实其生物碱抗菌活性。
evidence_notes.note_en: Traditionally the premier bitter-cold herb for damp-heat; modern studies confirm alkaloid-based antimicrobial action.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 小檗碱对痢疾杆菌、金黄色葡萄球菌、幽门螺杆菌等均有抑制作用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Berberine inhibits Shigella, Staphylococcus aureus, and Helicobacter pylori among others.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 孕妇及新生儿禁用——小檗碱可置换胆红素诱发核黄疸；并注意其 CYP3A4/P-gp 相互作用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Contraindicated in pregnancy and neonates — berberine displaces bilirubin and may precipitate kernicterus; caution with CYP3A4/P-gp interactions.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》以小檗碱含量（不少于 5.5%）为质量控制指标。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chinese Pharmacopoeia specifies berberine content (≥5.5%) as the quality marker.

description_en: The dried rhizome of Coptis chinensis, C. deltoidea, or C. teeta (Ranunculaceae). Enters the Heart, Spleen, Stomach, Liver, Gallbladder, and Large Intestine channels. Bitter and cold, it is the primary herb for clearing heat and drying dampness, draining fire and resolving toxicity, with particular efficacy in treating middle-burner damp-heat and Heart fire.

indications.en: Externally contracted warm diseases with high fever, vexation and thirst
Lung heat dry cough
Steaming bone tidal fever and night sweats
Internal heat wasting-thirst disorder
Intestinal dryness with constipation

contraindications_zh: 脾胃虚寒、大便溏泄者忌用。
contraindications_en: Contraindicated in spleen-stomach vacuity cold with loose stools and diarrhea.

dosage_range.notes: 煎服6–12g；清热泻火宜生用，滋阴降火宜盐水炒

processing_methods.name: 生知母
processing_methods.effect: 清热泻火之力较强，用于气分实热

processing_methods.name: 盐知母
processing_methods.effect: 引药下行，滋阴降火力胜，善治下焦阴虚火旺

safety_notes_zh: 性寒质润，脾虚便溏者忌用。久服大量可碍脾助湿。
