evidence_notes.note_en: Sweet and cold, entering lung and stomach — clears heat, generates fluids, and stops vomiting in warm diseases.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 水煎液有解热、止吐及促进分泌作用；多糖显示抗氧化活性。
evidence_notes.note_en: Decoction shows antipyretic, antiemetic, and secretagogue effects; polysaccharides show antioxidant activity.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》以性状和浸出物为质量控制指标。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chinese Pharmacopoeia uses macroscopic and extractive controls as quality markers.

description_en: The fresh or dried rhizome of Phragmites communis (Poaceae). Enters the Lung and Stomach channels. Sweet, cold, and lightweight, it clears heat without damaging the Stomach and generates fluids without retaining pathogenic factors. An excellent herb for warm-disease fluid damage and Stomach-heat vomiting. Wu Jutong used fresh reed rhizome decoction as the liquid medium in Yin Qiao San.

actions.en: Clear the Liver and drain fire

indications.zh: 肝火上炎，目赤肿痛，头痛眩晕
indications.en: Liver fire flaring upward with red painful eyes, headache, and dizziness
Eye pain worse at night
Scrofula and goiter
Breast abscess with swelling and pain
Hypertension with ascendant Liver yang

contraindications_zh: 脾胃虚寒者慎用。气虚者不宜单用久服，恐伤中气。
contraindications_en: Use with caution in spleen-stomach vacuity cold. Not suitable for prolonged sole use in qi vacuity, as it may damage middle qi.

dosage_range.notes: 内服煎汤9–15g；治瘰疬、瘿瘤可用至30g；熬膏或入丸散。

processing_methods.name: 生夏枯草
processing_methods.effect: 清肝火、散郁结，为治肝火目疾及瘰疬之要药

safety_notes_zh: 长期大量服用可损伤胃气，脾虚便溏者不宜。现代临床用于高血压病，宜辨证属肝火或肝阳者；阴虚阳亢者须配伍滋阴之品。
safety_notes_en: Prolonged high-dose use may damage stomach qi; not suitable in spleen vacuity with loose stools. In modern clinical use for hypertension, it is suitable when the pattern involves liver fire or liver yang; in yin vacuity with yang hyperactivity, it should be combined with yin-nourishing herbs.

classical_references.passage: 味苦辛，寒。主寒热，瘰疬，鼠瘘，头疮，破癥，散瘿结气，脚肿湿痹，轻身。

description_zh: 唇形科植物夏枯草的干燥果穗。归肝、胆经。夏至后花穗枯黄而得名。禀纯阳之气，得阴气而枯，故善清肝火、散郁结，为治肝火目赤、痰火瘰疬之要药。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 夏枯草主含三萜酸（熊果酸、齐墩果酸）及酚酸，具有降压、抗炎、抗病毒及免疫调节作用。
summary.en: Prunella spike contains triterpene acids (ursolic and oleanolic acids) and phenolic acids with antihypertensive, anti-inflammatory, antiviral and immunomodulatory effects.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 高血压肝阳上亢之头痛眩晕
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Liver-yang rising hypertension with headache and dizziness

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 瘰疬、甲状腺肿、乳腺增生

evidence_notes.note_zh: 清肝泻火、明目、散结消肿，为治瘰疬、肝火目赤的常用药。
evidence_notes.note_en: Clears Liver fire, brightens eyes and dissipates nodules; commonly used for scrofula and Liver-fire red eyes.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 熊果酸显示抗炎、抗肿瘤及轻度降压的实验证据。
evidence_notes.note_en: Ursolic acid shows experimental evidence of anti-inflammatory, antitumor and mild antihypertensive activity.

description_en: The dried fruiting spike of Prunella vulgaris (Lamiaceae). Enters the Liver and Gallbladder channels. Bitter, pungent, and cold, named for withering after the summer solstice. Endowed with pure yang qi that disperses when encountering yin, it excels at clearing Liver fire and dissipating nodules, making it the key herb for Liver fire with red eyes and for phlegm-fire scrofula.

indications.en: Damp-heat dysentery and jaundice
Damp-heat leukorrhea and heat strangury
Damp-heat beriberi and flaccid paralysis of the legs

indications.zh: 阴虚发热，骨蒸盗汗，遗精
indications.en: Yin-vacuity fever, steaming bone tidal fever, night sweats, and seminal emission
Sores, swellings, and damp-skin eruptions with itching

contraindications_zh: 苦寒伤胃，脾胃虚寒者慎用或忌用。凡无湿热实火及阴虚无火者禁服。
contraindications_en: Bitter and cold, may injure the stomach; use with caution or avoid in spleen-stomach vacuity cold. Contraindicated when there is no damp-heat, repletion fire, or yin vacuity with fire.

dosage_range.notes: 内服煎汤3–12g；外用适量，研末调敷或煎水浸洗。清湿热用生品，退虚热宜盐水炒。

processing_methods.name: 生黄柏
processing_methods.effect: 清热燥湿、泻火解毒力强，用于湿热下注、疮毒

processing_methods.name: 盐黄柏
processing_methods.effect: 引药下行入肾，善退虚热、坚阴止遗，用于阴虚火旺、骨蒸盗汗

processing_methods.name: 黄柏炭
processing_methods.effect: 清湿热而兼止血，用于湿热便血、崩漏

safety_notes_zh: 苦寒，脾胃虚寒者慎用。久服或大量使用恐伤脾阳、损耗津液。孕妇慎用。现代研究提示其活性成分小檗碱对肝肾功能的长期影响应予注意。
safety_notes_en: Bitter and cold; use with caution in spleen-stomach vacuity cold. Prolonged or high-dose use may damage spleen yang and consume fluids. Use with caution during pregnancy. Modern research suggests that the long-term effects of berberine (its active constituent) on liver and kidney function should be monitored.

classical_references.passage: 味苦，寒。主五脏肠胃中结热，黄疸，肠痔，止泄痢，女子漏下赤白，阴阳蚀疮。

description_zh: 芸香科植物黄皮树的干燥树皮。归肾、膀胱经。为清下焦湿热、退虚热坚阴之要药。与知母相须为用以滋阴降火（知柏地黄丸），与苍术配伍以清热燥湿（二妙散）。黄柏入下焦，黄连入中焦，黄芩入上焦，三者分治三焦湿热。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 黄柏主含异喹啉类生物碱（小檗碱、巴马汀、黄柏碱）与柠檬苦素，药理示其抗菌、抗炎、降糖、降压、抑制醛糖还原酶与免疫调节作用。
summary.en: Huangbai contains isoquinoline alkaloids (berberine, palmatine, phellodendrine) and limonoids; pharmacology shows antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, hypoglycemic, antihypertensive, aldose-reductase inhibitory and immunomodulatory effects.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 湿热下注（泌尿生殖系感染、带下）
2 型糖尿病辅助（黄柏/小檗碱制剂）

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》以小檗碱作为含量测定指标；收载川黄柏（Phellodendron chinense）与关黄柏（P. amurense）。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chinese Pharmacopoeia uses berberine as the assay marker and lists Phellodendron chinense (chuan huangbai) and P. amurense (guan huangbai).

evidence_notes.note_zh: 小檗碱可置换胆红素并抑制 UGT1A1，新生儿及孕晚期禁用以防核黄疸；与环孢素、大环内酯类等经 CYP3A4 代谢药物相互作用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Berberine displaces bilirubin and inhibits UGT1A1; contraindicated in neonates and late pregnancy due to kernicterus risk; interacts with CYP3A4 substrates (cyclosporine, macrolides).

evidence_notes.note_zh: 脾胃虚寒者忌用；盐黄柏入下焦，用于肾阴虚火旺。
evidence_notes.note_en: Avoid in spleen-stomach cold-vacuity; salt-fried form targets lower burner for kidney yin-vacuity fire.

description_en: The dried bark of Phellodendron chinense (Rutaceae). Enters the Kidney and Bladder channels. Bitter and cold, it is the key herb for clearing lower-burner damp-heat and withdrawing vacuity heat to firm yin. Paired with zhi mu for enriching yin and downbearing fire in Zhi Bai Di Huang Wan; paired with cang zhu for clearing heat and drying dampness in Er Miao San.

indications.en: Damp-heat jaundice

indications.zh: 阴肿阴痒、带下、湿疹瘙痒
indications.en: Damp-heat leukorrhea, genital itching, and eczema

indications.zh: 肝火头痛目赤，耳聋胁痛
indications.en: Liver-fire headache, red eyes, deafness, and hypochondriac pain
Bitter taste in mouth and dry throat
Acute childhood convulsions with high fever and clouded consciousness

contraindications_zh: 大苦大寒，脾胃虚寒者慎用，无湿热实火者忌服。脾虚便溏、阴虚津伤者不宜。
contraindications_en: Extremely bitter and cold; use with caution in spleen-stomach vacuity cold; contraindicated when there is no damp-heat or repletion fire. Not suitable in spleen vacuity with loose stools and yin vacuity with fluid damage.

dosage_range.notes: 内服煎汤3–6g；龙胆大苦大寒，用量不宜过大，以免伤胃损阳。外用适量，煎水洗或研末敷患处。

processing_methods.name: 生龙胆
processing_methods.effect: 清热燥湿、泻肝胆实火力强，用于肝胆湿热、实火上炎诸证

processing_methods.name: 酒龙胆
processing_methods.effect: 缓其苦寒之性，引药上行，用于头目肝火之证

safety_notes_zh: 大苦大寒，易伤脾胃，空腹慎服，以免致呕。久服或大量使用可致纳呆、头痛、面赤。脾胃虚寒、阳虚畏寒者禁用。须严格辨证，中病即止。
safety_notes_en: Extremely bitter and cold, may easily damage the spleen and stomach; use with caution on an empty stomach to avoid vomiting. Prolonged or high-dose use may cause loss of appetite, headache, and facial flushing. Contraindicated in spleen-stomach vacuity cold and yang vacuity with aversion to cold. Must be strictly pattern-differentiated; discontinue once the condition improves.

classical_references.passage: 味苦涩，寒。主骨间寒热，惊痫邪气，续绝伤，定五脏，杀蛊毒。

description_zh: 龙胆科植物条叶龙胆、龙胆、三花龙胆或坚龙胆的干燥根及根茎。归肝、胆经。为泻肝胆实火、清下焦湿热之要药。李东垣龙胆泻肝汤以本品为君，治肝胆实火上炎、下焦湿热诸证。苦寒沉降，非实火湿热不可妄用。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 龙胆草含裂环环烯醚萜苷及生物碱，具苦味健胃、保肝、利胆、抗炎及降压作用。
summary.en: Gentiana contains secoiridoid glycosides and alkaloids with bitter stomachic, hepatoprotective, choleretic, anti-inflammatory, and antihypertensive activity.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 龙胆苦苷刺激苦味受体反射性促进胃液分泌，并显示保肝利胆作用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Gentiopicroside stimulates bitter receptors to reflexively promote gastric secretion and shows hepatoprotective and choleretic action.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 龙胆大苦大寒，清肝胆湿热下焦湿热，为龙胆泻肝汤主药。
evidence_notes.note_en: Intensely bitter and cold — clears liver-gallbladder and lower-burner damp-heat; chief herb of Long Dan Xie Gan Tang.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》以龙胆苦苷含量（不少于 3.0%）为质量指标。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chinese Pharmacopoeia specifies gentiopicroside (≥3.0%) as the quality marker.

description_en: The dried root and rhizome of Gentiana manshurica, G. scabra, G. triflora, or G. rigescens (Gentianaceae). Enters the Liver and Gallbladder channels. Bitter and cold, it is the primary herb for draining Liver-Gallbladder repletion fire and clearing lower-burner damp-heat. Li Dongyuan's Long Dan Xie Gan Tang uses it as chief to treat ascending Liver-Gallbladder fire and lower-burner damp-heat conditions.

indications.en: Warm-disease fever and warm-toxin maculopapular eruption
Sore swollen throat and mouth or tongue ulcers

indications.zh: 大头瘟疫（流行性腮腺炎）
indications.en: Epidemic parotitis (mumps)
Erysipelas, abscesses, and toxic sores

indications.zh: 流感、肝炎等外感温热疫毒证
indications.en: Influenza and viral hepatitis with warm-toxin pattern

contraindications_zh: 体虚无实火热毒者忌服。脾胃虚寒者慎用。
contraindications_en: Contraindicated in constitutional vacuity without repletion fire or heat-toxin. Use with caution in spleen-stomach vacuity cold.

dosage_range.notes: 内服煎汤9–15g；大剂量治疗时疫可用至30g。外用适量，煎水含漱或研末调敷。

processing_methods.name: 生板蓝根
processing_methods.effect: 清热解毒、凉血利咽，临床均用生品

safety_notes_zh: 性寒味苦，不宜长期大量服用，可损伤脾胃阳气。现代报告偶见过敏反应及胃肠不适。非热毒证忌用，儿童用量宜减。
safety_notes_en: Cold in nature and bitter in flavor; not suitable for prolonged high-dose use, as it may damage spleen and stomach yang qi. Modern reports note occasional allergic reactions and gastrointestinal discomfort. Contraindicated when there is no heat-toxin pattern; dosage should be reduced for children.

classical_references.passage: 治时行疫疠，咽喉肿塞，兼治诸般热毒。

description_zh: 十字花科植物菘蓝的干燥根。归心、胃经。为治温病热毒、咽喉肿痛之要药。与大青叶同出一源（大青叶为本植物之叶），功用相近而板蓝根偏于解毒利咽，大青叶偏于凉血消斑。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

active_constituents.notes: indirubin is the active moiety studied for CML

summary.zh: 板蓝根含吲哚类（靛蓝、靛玉红）、表告依春及多糖，具有抗病毒、抗菌、抗内毒素及免疫调节作用；靛玉红曾被研究用于慢性粒细胞白血病。
summary.en: Isatidis Radix contains indole compounds (indigo, indirubin), epigoitrin, and polysaccharides, with antiviral, antibacterial, anti-endotoxin, and immunomodulatory activity; indirubin has been studied as a treatment for chronic myelogenous leukemia.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 上呼吸道病毒感染（流感、腮腺炎）
慢性粒细胞白血病（靛玉红）

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中药学》（Bensky）指出板蓝根是中国广泛使用的抗病毒清热解毒药，常与金银花、连翘配伍。
evidence_notes.note_en: Bensky notes that Ban Lan Gen is widely used in China as an antiviral heat-clearing herb, commonly paired with jin yin hua and lian qiao.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 陈氏药理学记载中国临床研究发现靛玉红对慢性粒细胞白血病有一定缓解作用，并是 GSK-3β 抑制剂研究的先导化合物。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen cite Chinese clinical research showing indirubin activity in CML, and its role as a lead compound for GSK-3β inhibitor development.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》2020年版以表告依春作为板蓝根的含量测定指标。
evidence_notes.note_en: The 2020 Chinese Pharmacopoeia uses epigoitrin as the quantitative marker for Ban Lan Gen.

description_en: The dried root of Isatis indigotica (Brassicaceae). Enters the Heart and Stomach channels. Bitter and cold, it is a key herb for clearing heat-toxin in warm diseases and for sore, swollen throat. Derived from the same plant as da qing ye (the leaf), they share similar functions though ban lan gen excels at resolving toxicity and benefiting the throat while da qing ye excels at cooling blood and dispersing macules.

actions.en: Promote lactation and relieve breast abscess

indications.en: Breast abscess with swelling and pain
Toxic sores and furuncles
Scrofula, intestinal abscess, and lung abscess
Damp-heat jaundice
Heat strangury and red painful eyes

contraindications_zh: 阳虚外寒、脾胃虚寒者忌服。用量过大可致缓泻。
contraindications_en: Contraindicated in yang vacuity with exterior cold and spleen-stomach vacuity cold. Excessive dosage may cause mild laxative effects.

dosage_range.notes: 内服煎汤9–15g，大剂量可至30–60g；鲜品可捣汁或捣敷；外用适量，鲜品捣烂敷患处治乳痈、疔疮。

processing_methods.name: 鲜蒲公英
processing_methods.effect: 清热解毒、消肿散结力强，外敷治乳痈、疔疮尤宜

processing_methods.name: 干蒲公英
processing_methods.effect: 作用与鲜品同而药力稍缓，便于煎服

safety_notes_zh: 药性平和，为清热解毒常用药，但苦寒仍可伤胃，脾胃虚寒者慎用。偶见过敏反应。大剂量久服可引起轻度腹泻。
safety_notes_en: Mild in nature; a commonly used heat-clearing and toxin-resolving herb, but its bitter-cold nature may still injure the stomach; use with caution in spleen-stomach vacuity cold. Occasional allergic reactions. Prolonged high-dose use may cause mild diarrhea.

classical_references.passage: 蒲公英，治乳痈红肿，捣汁和酒服。

description_zh: 菊科植物蒲公英、碱地蒲公英或同属数种植物的干燥全草。归肝、胃经。为治乳痈之要药，内服外敷皆宜。性虽苦寒而不甚伤胃，兼能利湿通淋，故为清热解毒药中较为平和之品。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

active_constituents.name_zh: 倍半萜内酯（蒲公英酸葡萄糖苷）

summary.zh: 蒲公英含三萜、倍半萜内酯、菊苣酸与菊糖等，现代研究显示其具利胆、利尿、抗炎及抑菌作用，呼应传统清热解毒、消痈散结之功。
summary.en: Dandelion (Taraxacum) contains triterpenes, sesquiterpene lactones, chicoric acid, and inulin, with reported choleretic, diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial activities consistent with its traditional heat-clearing, toxin-resolving, and abscess-dispersing use.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 消化不良、食欲不振（欧洲传统用药）
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Dyspepsia and loss of appetite (European traditional use)

evidence_notes.note_zh: WHO 专著收录 Taraxaci Radix 与 Herba，肯定其作为苦味健胃药与利胆剂的传统应用。
evidence_notes.note_en: WHO monographs cover Taraxaci Radix and Herba, supporting their traditional use as bitter digestives and choleretic agents.

evidence_notes.note_zh: Chen & Chen 综述蒲公英对金黄色葡萄球菌等致乳腺炎病原有抑制作用，并有护肝利胆报告。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen summarize dandelion's inhibitory activity against S. aureus and other mastitis pathogens and its hepatoprotective/choleretic effects.

description_en: The dried whole herb of Taraxacum mongolicum and related species (Asteraceae). Enters the Liver and Stomach channels. Bitter, sweet, and cold, it is the essential herb for treating breast abscess (ru yong), suitable for both internal administration and external application. Though bitter and cold, it does not significantly damage the Stomach, and its additional ability to drain dampness and relieve strangury makes it a relatively mild member of the heat-clearing toxin-resolving category.

actions.en: Dissolve phlegm and benefit the throat

indications.en: Sore swollen throat
Copious phlegm with cough and wheezing
Scrofula and phlegm nodules
Abscesses and toxic sores
Throat obstruction with loss of voice

contraindications_zh: 病无实热及脾虚便溏者不宜。孕妇忌服。
contraindications_en: Not suitable when there is no repletion heat and in spleen vacuity with loose stools. Contraindicated during pregnancy.

dosage_range.notes: 内服煎汤3–10g；入丸散用量酌减。外用适量，煎水洗或研末吹喉。

processing_methods.name: 生射干
processing_methods.effect: 清热解毒、消痰利咽力强，治咽喉肿痛、痰盛咳喘

safety_notes_zh: 性寒有小毒，用量不宜过大，脾虚便溏者慎服。孕妇禁用。过量可致胃肠刺激、腹痛腹泻。
safety_notes_en: Cold in nature and slightly toxic; dosage should not be excessive; use with caution in spleen vacuity with loose stools. Contraindicated during pregnancy. Overdose may cause gastrointestinal irritation, abdominal pain, and diarrhea.

classical_references.passage: 咳而上气，喉中水鸡声，射干麻黄汤主之。

description_zh: 鸢尾科植物射干的干燥根茎。归肺经。为治咽喉肿痛、痰壅气逆之要药。张仲景射干麻黄汤以本品配麻黄、细辛等，治寒饮伏肺、喉中水鸡声之证，为宣肺化痰利咽之代表方。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 射干主含异黄酮类化合物（射干苷、鸢尾黄素等），具有抗炎、抗病毒、祛痰及抑菌作用。
summary.en: Belamcanda rhizome contains isoflavonoids (tectoridin, tectorigenin, irigenin) with anti-inflammatory, antiviral, expectorant and antibacterial activity.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 咽喉肿痛、急性扁桃体炎

evidence_notes.note_zh: 主治咽喉肿痛、痰涎壅盛，为射干麻黄汤主药。
evidence_notes.note_en: Principal herb in She Gan Ma Huang Tang for throat swelling with copious phlegm.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 射干提取物对金葡菌、肺炎双球菌及多种呼吸道病毒有抑制作用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Extracts inhibit S. aureus, pneumococcus and several respiratory viruses in vitro.

description_en: The dried rhizome of Belamcanda chinensis (Iridaceae). Enters the Lung channel. Bitter and cold, it is the key herb for treating sore, swollen throat with phlegm obstruction and qi counterflow. Zhang Zhongjing's She Gan Ma Huang Tang combines it with ma huang and xi xin for cold-rheum lurking in the Lung with wheezing and a sound like water gurgling in the throat.

actions.en: Clear heat and cool the blood

indications.en: Warm-toxin maculopapular eruption and blood-heat hematemesis or epistaxis
Red swollen painful eyes
Liver constraint with hypochondriac pain, amenorrhea, and dysmenorrhea
Abdominal masses and traumatic injury
Abscesses and sores

contraindications_zh: 不宜与藜芦同用（十八反）。血虚无瘀之证及痈疽已溃者慎用。月经过多及孕妇慎用。
contraindications_en: Should not be used concurrently with li lu (Veratri Radix et Rhizoma) per the Eighteen Antagonisms (shiba fan). Use with caution in blood vacuity without stasis and already-ulcerated abscesses. Use with caution in excessive menstruation and during pregnancy.

dosage_range.notes: 内服煎汤6–12g；活血散瘀、治癥瘕可用至15g；入丸散酌减。

processing_methods.name: 生赤芍
processing_methods.effect: 清热凉血、散瘀止痛力强，用于血热、血瘀诸证

processing_methods.name: 炒赤芍
processing_methods.effect: 减缓寒性，活血而不伤中，用于瘀血腹痛而脾胃不甚虚者

processing_methods.name: 酒赤芍
processing_methods.effect: 增强活血散瘀之力，用于经闭痛经、跌打损伤

safety_notes_zh: 性寒凉而散瘀，无瘀血者不宜用。与藜芦相反，不可同用。月经过多、血虚及孕妇慎服。现代研究未见明显毒性。
safety_notes_en: Cold and cool in nature with stasis-dispersing action; not suitable when there is no blood stasis. Antagonizes li lu (Veratri Radix et Rhizoma); must not be used together. Use with caution in excessive menstruation, blood vacuity, and pregnancy. Modern research shows no significant toxicity.

classical_references.passage: 味苦，平。主邪气腹痛，除血痹，破坚积，寒热疝瘕，止痛，利小便，益气。

description_zh: 毛茛科植物芍药或川赤芍的干燥根，直接晒干而不去皮。归肝经。与白芍同源而功用迥异：白芍养血柔肝、敛阴止汗，主补；赤芍清热凉血、散瘀止痛，主泻。故有「白补赤泻、白敛赤散」之说。血热血瘀诸证用赤，血虚肝郁挛急用白。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 赤芍与白芍同源（Paeonia lactiflora），未经去皮加工，含芍药苷、苯甲酰芍药苷及丹皮酚，具有抗血小板聚集、改善微循环、抗炎及保肝作用。
summary.en: Paeoniae Radix Rubra shares source species with Bai Shao but is used unpeeled; it contains paeoniflorin, benzoylpaeoniflorin, and paeonol, with antiplatelet, microcirculation-improving, anti-inflammatory, and hepatoprotective activity.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 血瘀证（冠心病、脑血管病）辅助治疗
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Adjunct for blood-stasis conditions (coronary and cerebrovascular disease)
Heat entering the blood with maculopapular rash

evidence_notes.note_zh: Bensky 对比赤芍与白芍：白芍偏养血柔肝，赤芍偏清热凉血、活血散瘀。
evidence_notes.note_en: Bensky contrasts Chi Shao (cooling, blood-moving) with Bai Shao (blood-nourishing, liver-softening), despite their shared botanical origin.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 陈氏药理学指出赤芍提取物可抑制血小板聚集、延长凝血时间，临床用于血瘀型心脑血管疾病。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen report that Chi Shao extracts inhibit platelet aggregation and prolong clotting time, supporting clinical use in blood-stasis cardiovascular disease.

description_en: The dried root of Paeonia lactiflora or P. veitchii (Ranunculaceae), dried directly without peeling. Enters the Liver channel. Bitter and cool, it shares a botanical source with bai shao yet their functions differ markedly: bai shao nourishes blood, softens the Liver, and astringes yin (supplementing); chi shao clears heat, cools blood, and dispels stasis (draining). Hence the classical dictum: 'white supplements, red drains.'

actions.en: Clear heat and cool the blood

indications.zh: 温病热入营血，身热口干，神昏舌绛
indications.en: Warm disease with heat entering the nutritive and blood levels, fever, thirst, delirium, and crimson tongue
Febrile disease with yin damage and constipation from fluid depletion
Yin-vacuity fever and steaming bone taxation heat
Sore swollen throat and diphtheria
Scrofula, phlegm nodules, and toxic sores

contraindications_zh: 不宜与藜芦同用（十八反）。脾胃虚寒、食少便溏者忌服。
contraindications_en: Should not be used concurrently with li lu (Veratri Radix et Rhizoma) per the Eighteen Antagonisms (shiba fan). Contraindicated in spleen-stomach vacuity cold with poor appetite and loose stools.

dosage_range.notes: 内服煎汤9–15g；治热入营血、咽喉肿痛可用至30g；入丸散酌减。

processing_methods.name: 生玄参
processing_methods.effect: 清热凉血、滋阴降火、解毒散结，临床均用生品

safety_notes_zh: 性寒而滋腻，易碍脾助湿，脾胃虚寒、食少便溏者忌服。与藜芦相反，不可同用。不宜长期大量服用。
safety_notes_en: Cold and enriching-cloying in nature, may impair the spleen and promote dampness; contraindicated in spleen-stomach vacuity cold with poor appetite and loose stools. Antagonizes li lu (Veratri Radix et Rhizoma); must not be used together. Not suitable for prolonged high-dose use.

classical_references.passage: 味苦，微寒。主腹中寒热积聚，女子产乳余疾，补肾气，令人目明。

description_zh: 玄参科植物玄参的干燥根。归肺、胃、肾经。色黑入肾，咸能软坚，善治肾阴不足、虚火上炎之咽喉肿痛、瘰疬痰核。吴鞠通增液汤以玄参配麦冬、生地，治热病伤阴、津枯便秘，开「增水行舟」之法。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 玄参含哈巴俄苷等环烯醚萜类成分，具有抗炎、降压、轻度降糖及镇静作用，为清热凉血、滋阴解毒的药理基础。
summary.en: Scrophularia contains iridoid glycosides such as harpagoside with anti-inflammatory, hypotensive, mild hypoglycemic and sedative effects, underlying its heat-clearing, blood-cooling and yin-nourishing actions.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Heat entering the nutritive-blood level with maculopapular eruptions

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中药学》（Bensky）指出玄参十八反反藜芦，与半夏、附子等十八反禁忌须遵守。
evidence_notes.note_en: Bensky notes Xuan Shen is part of the eighteen antagonisms and is incompatible with Li Lu (Veratrum).

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》以哈巴俄苷及哈巴苷为质量控制指标。
evidence_notes.note_en: The Chinese Pharmacopoeia uses harpagoside and harpagide as quality markers.

description_en: The dried root of Scrophularia ningpoensis (Scrophulariaceae). Enters the Lung, Stomach, and Kidney channels. Sweet, bitter, salty, and cold, its dark color enters the Kidney, and its salty quality softens hardness. It excels at treating Kidney-yin insufficiency with vacuity fire flaring upward, causing sore throat and scrofula. Wu Jutong's Zeng Ye Tang pairs it with mai dong and sheng di to treat fluid exhaustion and constipation in warm disease.

actions.en: Cool the blood and relieve steaming bone fever

indications.zh: 温病后期，邪伏阴分，夜热早凉
indications.en: Late-stage warm disease with pathogen lurking in the yin aspect and fever at night that abates by morning
Yin-vacuity fever and steaming bone taxation heat
Summerheat exterior syndrome with fever and headache
Malarial alternating chills and fever
Damp-heat jaundice

contraindications_zh: 苦寒，脾胃虚寒者慎用。产后血虚、内寒腹泻者忌服。
contraindications_en: Bitter and cold; use with caution in spleen-stomach vacuity cold. Contraindicated in postpartum blood vacuity and diarrhea from internal cold.

dosage_range.notes: 内服煎汤6–12g，不宜久煎；治疟疾可用至20–40g，宜鲜品绞汁服或后下，以免其有效成分青蒿素因高温久煎而破坏。

processing_methods.name: 鲜青蒿
processing_methods.effect: 截疟力最强，古法以鲜品绞汁服用，现代研究证实其抗疟有效成分青蒿素不耐高温，鲜品绞汁或冷浸保存药力最佳

processing_methods.name: 干青蒿
processing_methods.effect: 清透虚热、凉血除蒸，入汤剂宜后下，煎煮时间不宜超过10分钟，以免青蒿素及挥发油损失

safety_notes_zh: 煎煮须注意：本品含青蒿素等不耐热成分，久煎则有效成分大量损失，故应后下或鲜品绞汁服用。苦寒性质易伤脾胃，脾虚便溏者慎用。孕妇慎服。过量可致恶心、腹痛。
safety_notes_en: Decoction note: this herb contains artemisinin and other heat-sensitive components; prolonged decoction causes significant loss of active constituents, so it should be added near the end or taken as fresh-squeezed juice. Its bitter-cold nature may easily damage the spleen and stomach; use with caution in spleen vacuity with loose stools. Use with caution during pregnancy. Overdose may cause nausea and abdominal pain.

classical_references.passage: 夜热早凉，热退无汗，热自阴来者，青蒿鳖甲汤主之。

description_zh: 菊科植物黄花蒿的干燥地上部分。归肝、胆经。为清透阴分伏热之要药。吴鞠通青蒿鳖甲汤以青蒿配鳖甲，治温病后期邪伏阴分、夜热早凉之证，取其「先入后出」——鳖甲入阴搜邪，青蒿领邪外透。二十世纪屠呦呦从本品中分离出青蒿素，成为现代抗疟名药，亦佐证葛洪《肘后方》「青蒿一握，水二升渍，绞取汁」之古法用意——避免高温破坏有效成分。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

active_constituents.notes: trace amounts in raw herb; aqueous decoction destroys activity

summary.zh: 青蒿含倍半萜内酯青蒿素及其衍生物，为当代抗恶性疟疾一线药物的原型，屠呦呦团队因此于 2015 年获诺贝尔生理学或医学奖。
summary.en: Artemisia annua yields the sesquiterpene lactone endoperoxide artemisinin and its derivatives, prototype of frontline antimalarials for Plasmodium falciparum; Tu Youyou's team received the 2015 Nobel Prize in Physiology or Medicine for this discovery.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 恶性疟疾（以提取物或青蒿素衍生物为主）
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: P. falciparum malaria (as extract or artemisinin derivatives)

evidence_notes.note_zh: WHO 推荐青蒿素为基础的联合疗法 (ACT) 为无并发症恶性疟疾的首选治疗。
evidence_notes.note_en: WHO recommends artemisinin-based combination therapy (ACT) as first-line treatment for uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中药学》（Bensky）记载青蒿素遇热易破坏，故传统煎煮难显抗疟作用，屠呦呦团队采用低温乙醚提取。
evidence_notes.note_en: Bensky notes that artemisinin is heat-sensitive and traditional decoction largely destroys it; Tu Youyou's team used cold ether extraction.

evidence_notes.note_zh: Chen & Chen 综述青蒿素通过血红素催化产生自由基破坏疟原虫膜结构。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen summarize that artemisinin exerts antimalarial action via heme-catalyzed free radicals that damage Plasmodium membranes.

description_en: The dried aerial part of Artemisia annua (Asteraceae). Enters the Liver and Gallbladder channels. Bitter, pungent, and cold, it is the key herb for clearing and venting vacuity heat lurking in the yin level. Wu Jutong's Qing Hao Bie Jia Tang pairs it with bie jia, following the principle of 'entering first, then exiting'—bie jia enters the yin level to search out pathogens while qing hao leads them outward. In the twentieth century, Tu Youyou isolated artemisinin from this herb, validating Ge Hong's ancient low-temperature extraction technique.

actions.en: Moisten the intestines and unblock the bowels

indications.en: Blood vacuity and fluid depletion with dry intestines and constipation

indications.zh: 年老体弱、产后、病后津伤便秘
indications.en: Constipation from fluid injury in the elderly, postpartum, or after illness
Habitual constipation
Wasting-thirst (xiao ke) with scanty urination
Wind painful obstruction with itching, sores, and lichen

contraindications_zh: 脾虚便溏、肠滑泄泻者慎用。大剂量（60–120g）服用可引起中毒反应（恶心、呕吐、四肢麻木），须控制用量。
contraindications_en: Use with caution in spleen vacuity with loose stools and intestinal slippery diarrhea. High doses (60-120g) may cause toxic reactions (nausea, vomiting, limb numbness); dosage must be controlled.

dosage_range.notes: 打碎入煎。润下药中药性最为平和，适合老人、产妇、虚人便秘。

processing_methods.name: 生火麻仁
processing_methods.effect: 润肠通便力较强

processing_methods.name: 炒火麻仁
processing_methods.effect: 缓和油腻之性，便于煎出有效成分

safety_notes_zh: 一次性过量服用（60g以上）曾有中毒报告，表现为恶心呕吐、肢体麻木、烦躁不安。严格控制用量，打碎后入煎以利吸收。
safety_notes_en: Cases of poisoning from single excessive doses (over 60g) have been reported, manifesting as nausea, vomiting, limb numbness, and restlessness. Dosage must be strictly controlled; crush before decocting to facilitate absorption.

classical_references.passage: 味甘，平。主补中益气，肥健不老神仙。

description_zh: 桑科植物大麻的干燥成熟果实。归脾、胃、大肠经。为润下药代表药，药性平和，质润多脂，能润燥滑肠而不伤正气，故老人、产妇及体虚津亏之肠燥便秘皆可应用，与大黄、芒硝等攻下药迥别。常配杏仁、当归、白芍，即仲景麻子仁丸之法。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

active_constituents.notes: seed trace only; non-psychoactive at culinary/medicinal doses

summary.zh: 火麻仁富含脂肪油和蛋白质，通过润肠通便及滋养作用缓解津亏便秘，并具一定调脂作用。
summary.en: Hemp seed is rich in fixed oil and protein, relieving dryness constipation by lubricating the bowel and nourishing, with modest lipid-modulating effects.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 老年及产后津亏性便秘
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Dryness constipation in the elderly and postpartum

evidence_notes.note_zh: 火麻仁甘平润下，用于津枯肠燥便秘，老人、产妇及体虚者尤宜。
evidence_notes.note_en: Sweet and neutral, moistens the intestines for dryness constipation — especially in the elderly, postpartum, and debilitated.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 润滑作用源自脂肪油；动物实验显示调脂及缓泻效应。
evidence_notes.note_en: Lubricant action derives from the fixed oil; animal studies show lipid-modulating and mild laxative effects.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》以脂肪油含量（不少于 30%）为质量指标，要求去除药典外大麻活性品种。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chinese Pharmacopoeia specifies fixed oil content (≥30%) and excludes psychoactive Cannabis chemotypes.

description_en: The dried ripe fruit of Cannabis sativa (Moraceae). Enters the Spleen, Stomach, and Large Intestine channels. Sweet and neutral, it is the representative moistening laxative, with a mild nature that moistens dryness and lubricates the intestines without injuring upright qi. Suitable for elderly, postpartum, and constitutionally weakened patients with intestinal dryness constipation, as in Zhang Zhongjing's Ma Zi Ren Wan.

actions.en: Moisten the intestines and unblock the bowels
Direct qi downward and move stagnation
Moisten dryness and lubricate the intestines

indications.en: Dry-intestine constipation with abdominal distention and qi stagnation
Food accumulation and qi stagnation with distention and constipation
Edema and distention, foot-qi (beriberi) with swelling
Urinary difficulty
Postpartum dry-intestine constipation

contraindications_zh: 孕妇慎用。阴虚津亏者及大便不实者忌用。
contraindications_en: Use with caution during pregnancy. Contraindicated in yin vacuity with fluid depletion and unformed stools.

dosage_range.notes: 打碎入煎。较火麻仁润下之力稍强，且兼行气利水，适合兼气滞、水肿之便秘。

processing_methods.name: 生郁李仁
processing_methods.effect: 润肠通便，利水消肿

processing_methods.name: 炒郁李仁
processing_methods.effect: 缓其润滑之性

safety_notes_zh: 含苦杏仁苷类成分，过量可引起恶心呕吐。孕妇慎用以防动胎。
safety_notes_en: Contains amygdalin-type components; overdose may cause nausea and vomiting. Use with caution during pregnancy to prevent disturbing the fetus.

classical_references.passage: 味酸，平。主大腹水肿，面目四肢浮肿，利小便水道。

description_zh: 蔷薇科植物欧李、郁李或长柄扁桃的干燥成熟种子。归脾、大肠、小肠经。润下药之一，润肠通便之力较火麻仁为强，且能下气利水，故尤宜于大肠气滞、津枯肠燥之便秘，及水肿脚气、小便不利者，体现润下兼行气利水之特色。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

active_constituents.notes: principal laxative component acting by intestinal lubrication
trace cyanogenic glycoside; dose limits apply

summary.zh: 郁李仁富含脂肪油并含少量苦杏仁苷类成分，通过润滑肠道和轻度刺激肠蠕动发挥润下通便及利水消肿作用。
summary.en: Prunus seed (Yu Li Ren) is rich in fatty oil with small amounts of cyanogenic glycosides, producing moistening laxative and mild diuretic effects via intestinal lubrication and gentle peristaltic stimulation.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 津枯肠燥便秘（尤宜老人、产后）
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Constipation from depleted fluids and intestinal dryness (especially elderly and postpartum)

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中药学》（Bensky）指出郁李仁较火麻仁泻下力强，孕妇慎用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Bensky notes Yu Li Ren is stronger than Huo Ma Ren as a purgative and should be used cautiously in pregnancy.

evidence_notes.note_zh: Chen & Chen 介绍郁李仁兼具润肠和利尿作用，常用于便秘合并水肿者。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen describe combined moistening laxative and diuretic effects, useful when constipation coexists with edema.

description_en: The dried ripe seed of Prunus humilis, P. japonica, or P. pedunculata (Rosaceae). Enters the Spleen, Large Intestine, and Small Intestine channels. Pungent, bitter, sweet, and neutral, its laxative action is stronger than huo ma ren, and it additionally directs qi downward and promotes urination. It is particularly suited for constipation due to Large Intestine qi stagnation with fluid depletion, and for edema with inhibited urination.

actions.en: Unblock painful obstruction and alleviate pain
Release the exterior and disperse cold
Expel latent wind from the Shaoyin channels

indications.en: Wind-cold-damp painful obstruction with pain of the lower back and knees
Weakness of the lower limbs with impaired flexion and extension
Shaoyin-type headache and toothache
Wind-cold common cold with headache and body aches
Damp itching of the skin

contraindications_zh: 阴虚血燥者慎用。气血亏虚之痹证须配补益药。
contraindications_en: Use with caution in yin vacuity with blood dryness. In bi-syndrome from qi and blood vacuity, supplementing herbs should be combined.

processing_methods.name: 生独活
processing_methods.effect: 祛风湿，通痹止痛

safety_notes_zh: 含呋喃香豆素类成分，外用或大量内服偶见光敏反应。阴虚火旺者慎用。
safety_notes_en: Contains furanocoumarin compounds; occasional photosensitivity reactions with topical use or large oral doses. Use with caution in yin vacuity with effulgent fire.

classical_references.passage: 味苦，平。主风寒所击，金疮止痛，奔豚，痫痓，女子疝瘕。久服轻身耐老。

description_zh: 伞形科植物重齿毛当归的干燥根。归肾、膀胱经。为祛风湿、止痹痛之要药，性善下行，主入肾经，长于祛下半身之风寒湿痹，与羌活（善走上半身、祛太阳经风湿）相对为用。仲景独活寄生汤以独活配桑寄生、杜仲治风寒湿痹日久、肝肾两亏、腰膝冷痛，为千古名方。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 独活主含香豆素类（蛇床子素、哥伦比亚素）及挥发油，药理示其抗炎、镇痛、解痉、降压与光敏活性。
summary.en: Duhuo contains coumarins (osthole, columbianadin) and essential oil; pharmacology shows anti-inflammatory, analgesic, antispasmodic, antihypertensive and photosensitizing activity.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Wind-damp arthralgia, low-back and knee pain
Headache including migraine of wind-damp type

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》以蛇床子素与二氢欧山芹醇当归酸酯作为含量测定指标。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chinese Pharmacopoeia uses osthole and columbianadin as assay markers.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 独活寄生汤为治疗慢性风寒湿痹经典方。
evidence_notes.note_en: Duhuo Jisheng Tang is the classical formula for chronic wind-cold-damp bi pattern.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 含呋喃香豆素，可能引起光敏反应并与某些药物相互作用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Contains furanocoumarins; potential photosensitivity and drug interaction risk.

description_en: The dried root of Angelica pubescens (Apiaceae). Enters the Kidney and Bladder channels. Pungent, bitter, and slightly warm, it excels at dispelling wind-dampness and relieving impediment pain in the lower body, with particular affinity for the Kidney channel. It stands in complementary pairing with qiang huo, which governs the upper body and greater yang wind-dampness. Zhang Zhongjing's Du Huo Ji Sheng Tang is the archetypal formula for chronic wind-cold-damp impediment with Liver-Kidney vacuity.

indications.zh: 风湿热痹，关节红肿热痛
indications.en: Wind-damp-heat painful obstruction with red, swollen, hot, painful joints
Edema with urinary difficulty
Foot-qi (beriberi) with swelling of the lower limbs
Wind-water exterior vacuity with generalized edema
Damp rash and toxic sores

contraindications_zh: 食欲不振及阴虚无湿热者忌服。脾胃虚寒者慎用。严禁误用广防己（马兜铃科，含马兜铃酸）以防肾毒。
contraindications_en: Contraindicated in loss of appetite and yin vacuity without damp-heat. Use with caution in spleen-stomach vacuity cold. Strictly prohibited to mistakenly use guang fang ji (Aristolochiaceae, containing aristolochic acid) to prevent nephrotoxicity.

dosage_range.notes: 宜用粉防己（汉防己，Stephania tetrandra），利水消肿力胜；木防己（青风藤类）偏于祛风通络止痛。

processing_methods.name: 生防己
processing_methods.effect: 祛风湿，利水消肿

safety_notes_zh: 本品所指为粉防己（汉防己，Stephaniae Tetrandrae Radix），非马兜铃科广防己（Aristolochia fangchi，含肾毒性马兜铃酸，已在多国禁用）。处方调剂须严格区分品种。
safety_notes_en: This herb refers to fen fang ji (han fang ji, Stephaniae Tetrandrae Radix), not the Aristolochiaceae-family guang fang ji (Aristolochia fangchi, which contains nephrotoxic aristolochic acid and is banned in many countries). Prescription dispensing must strictly differentiate between species.

classical_references.passage: 味辛，平。主风寒温疟，热气诸痫，除邪，利大小便。

description_zh: 防己科植物粉防己（汉防己）的干燥根。归膀胱、肺经。祛风湿、止痹痛兼利水消肿之要药，尤宜于风湿热痹之关节红肿热痛及风水、皮水之水肿。古有粉防己（汉防己，利水消肿为长）与木防己（青风藤类，祛风通络止痛为长）之分，临证须明辨所用品种，不可混同马兜铃科之广防己以防肾损。仲景防己黄芪汤、防己茯苓汤即其代表方。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 粉防己（汉防己，Stephania tetrandra）主含双苄基异喹啉生物碱（粉防己碱、防己诺林碱），药理示其钙通道阻滞、降压、抗炎、抗纤维化与抗肿瘤作用。
summary.en: Fangji (Stephania tetrandra) contains bisbenzylisoquinoline alkaloids (tetrandrine, fangchinoline); pharmacology shows calcium channel blockade, antihypertensive, anti-inflammatory, antifibrotic and antitumor activity.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》仅收载粉防己（Stephania tetrandra），严禁使用含马兜铃酸的广防己（Aristolochia fangchi）。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chinese Pharmacopoeia lists only Stephania tetrandra; Aristolochia fangchi (containing aristolochic acid) is prohibited.

evidence_notes.note_zh: WHO 与多国监管机构均警示广防己类马兜铃酸肾毒性及致癌风险。
evidence_notes.note_en: WHO and regulators warn of aristolochic acid nephrotoxicity and carcinogenicity from Aristolochia species.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 应严格区分粉防己与广防己以避免误用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Strict botanical identification is required to avoid substitution with Aristolochia species.

description_en: The dried root of Stephania tetrandra (Menispermaceae). Enters the Bladder and Lung channels. Bitter, pungent, and cold, it dispels wind-dampness and alleviates pain while promoting urination and reducing edema. Particularly suitable for wind-damp-heat impediment with red, swollen, hot, painful joints and for wind-water or skin-water edema. Han fang ji and mu fang ji must be distinguished, and Aristolochia fangchi must be absolutely avoided due to nephrotoxicity.

indications.en: Wind-damp painful obstruction with soreness and weakness of the lower back and knees
Liver and Kidney insufficiency with weak sinews and bones
Dizziness and blurred vision from Liver-Kidney vacuity

indications.zh: 冲任不固，胎动不安，崩漏下血
indications.en: Insecurity of the Chong and Ren with restless fetus, metrorrhagia and metrostaxis
Vaginal bleeding during pregnancy and threatened miscarriage

contraindications_zh: 无明显禁忌，但须来源于桑树者为佳。寄生于有毒树木（如马桑、夹竹桃）者不可药用。
contraindications_en: No significant contraindications, but specimens sourced from mulberry trees are preferred. Parasites on toxic trees (such as ma sang and oleander) must not be used medicinally.

processing_methods.name: 生桑寄生
processing_methods.effect: 祛风湿，补肝肾，强筋骨，安胎

safety_notes_zh: 寄生植物，须来源于桑树者安全。寄生于马桑等有毒植物者含马桑内酯等神经毒素，严禁药用。古方寿胎丸（张锡纯）以桑寄生配菟丝子、续断、阿胶治胎动不安，为安胎名方。
safety_notes_en: As a parasitic plant, specimens from mulberry trees are safe. Parasites on toxic plants such as ma sang contain neurotoxins like coriamyrtin and are strictly prohibited for medicinal use. The classical formula Shou Tai Wan (by Zhang Xichun) combines sang ji sheng with tu si zi, xu duan, and e jiao to treat restless fetus, and is a famous fetus-calming formula.

classical_references.passage: 味苦，平。主腰痛，小儿背强，痈肿，安胎，充肌肤，坚发齿，长须眉。

description_zh: 桑寄生科植物桑寄生的干燥带叶茎枝。归肝、肾经。祛风湿药中兼补肝肾、强筋骨者，特别适用于痹证日久、肝肾亏虚之腰膝酸痛；又为安胎要药，与续断、菟丝子、阿胶相配治冲任不固之胎漏胎动。仲景独活寄生汤即以之配独活、杜仲等治风寒湿痹日久、肝肾两虚之证。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 桑寄生含黄酮（槲皮素、扁蓄苷）及齐墩果酸等三萜，现代药理研究相对有限，所报道的降压、抗炎及改善微循环作用多为动物实验与小样本临床观察；其主要临床地位仍基于传统补肝肾、强筋骨、祛风湿之功。
summary.en: Taxillus (Sang Ji Sheng) contains flavonoids (quercetin, avicularin) and triterpenes such as oleanolic acid. Modern pharmacological data are comparatively thin — reports of antihypertensive, anti-inflammatory, and microcirculation effects come mainly from animal studies and small clinical observations; its principal role remains grounded in traditional liver-kidney tonifying and wind-damp dispelling use.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 肝肾亏虚、腰膝酸痛、风湿痹痛
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Liver-kidney vacuity with lumbar/knee soreness and wind-damp bi

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 胎动不安、习惯性流产辅助

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中药学》（Bensky）指出桑寄生现代药理数据有限，临床应用仍以传统功效为主导；并提醒须注意寄主植物（桑 vs 其他）带来的成分差异。
evidence_notes.note_en: Bensky notes that modern pharmacological data on Taxillus are limited and clinical use remains largely traditional; host-tree differences (true mulberry vs. others) can affect composition.

evidence_notes.note_zh: Chen & Chen 综述桑寄生提取物在动物模型中显示轻度降压及镇静作用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen summarize mild hypotensive and sedative effects of Taxillus extracts in animal models.

description_en: The dried leafy stem of Taxillus chinensis (Loranthaceae). Enters the Liver and Kidney channels. Bitter, sweet, and neutral, it is the wind-dampness-dispelling herb that simultaneously supplements the Liver and Kidney and strengthens sinews and bones. It is especially suited for chronic impediment patterns with Liver-Kidney vacuity causing lumbar and knee soreness, and is also a key fetus-calming herb used with xu duan and e jiao.

actions.en: Unblock the collaterals and alleviate pain

indications.en: Wind-damp painful obstruction with contracted sinews and vessels

indications.zh: 骨节烦痛，不论寒热新久
indications.en: Painful joints from either cold or heat, acute or chronic

indications.zh: 中风半身不遂，口眼喎斜
indications.en: Wind stroke with hemiplegia and deviation of the mouth and eye
Steaming bone tidal fever and childhood nutritional impairment fever
Damp-heat jaundice

contraindications_zh: 久痛虚羸、溲多、便滑者忌服。
contraindications_en: Contraindicated in chronic pain with emaciation, frequent urination, and slippery stool.

processing_methods.name: 生秦艽
processing_methods.effect: 祛风湿，通络止痛，退虚热

safety_notes_zh: 药性平和，寒热虚实之痹证皆可配伍使用，素有风药中润剂之称。大剂量偶见恶心。
safety_notes_en: Mild in nature; can be combined for bi-syndrome of cold, heat, vacuity, or repletion types, traditionally known as 'the moistening agent among wind herbs'. Occasional nausea with high doses.

classical_references.passage: 味苦，平。主寒热邪气，寒湿风痹，肢节痛，下水，利小便。

description_zh: 龙胆科植物秦艽、麻花秦艽、粗茎秦艽或小秦艽的干燥根。归胃、肝、胆经。祛风湿药中药性平和、寒热皆宜之品，有风药中润剂之称，与独活同能祛下半身风湿（对应羌活之长于上半身），但秦艽不燥不刚，又能退虚热、清湿热，故尤宜于痹证日久兼阴亏有热，及骨蒸潮热、湿热黄疸。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 秦艽含龙胆生物碱及苦苷，现代研究显示其具抗炎、镇痛、解热及升血糖（动物实验）作用，与传统祛风湿、清虚热之功相符。
summary.en: Gentiana macrophylla contains iridoid alkaloids (gentianine) and gentiopicroside, with anti-inflammatory, analgesic, antipyretic, and (in animal studies) glucose-elevating effects that parallel its traditional wind-damp dispelling and vacuity-heat clearing use.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Wind-damp arthralgia with red, swollen joints

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中药学》（Bensky）指出秦艽为风药中之润剂，可用于热痹。
evidence_notes.note_en: Bensky describes Qin Jiao as a moistening wind-medicine suitable even for hot bi (joint) patterns.

evidence_notes.note_zh: Chen & Chen 综述秦艽碱甲的抗炎镇痛作用，作用机制与肾上腺皮质激素样效应相关。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen summarize anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects of gentianine, partly mediated via adrenocortical-like mechanisms.

description_en: The dried root of Gentiana macrophylla, G. straminea, G. crassicaulis, or G. dahurica (Gentianaceae). Enters the Stomach, Liver, and Gallbladder channels. Bitter, pungent, and neutral, it is the mildest and most balanced of the wind-dampness-dispelling herbs, called the 'moistening agent among wind medicinals.' It treats both heat and cold impediment patterns while also withdrawing vacuity heat and clearing damp-heat, making it especially suitable for chronic impediment with yin vacuity and heat.

actions.en: Relax the sinews and unblock the collaterals
Harmonize the Stomach and transform dampness
Eliminate dampness and stop painful obstruction
Generate fluids and open the appetite

indications.zh: 风湿痹痛，筋脉拘挛，腰膝关节酸重疼痛
indications.en: Wind-damp painful obstruction with contracted sinews and heavy, sore pain of the lower back and knees
Foot-qi (beriberi) with swelling and pain of the legs and knees

indications.zh: 吐泻转筋，腹痛腓肠拘急
indications.en: Vomiting and diarrhea with muscle cramping and contracted calf muscles
Summerheat-damp injury to the middle with vomiting and diarrhea
Indigestion with poor appetite

contraindications_zh: 胃酸过多者不宜。内有郁热、小便短赤者忌用。
contraindications_en: Not suitable in excessive gastric acid. Contraindicated in suppressed internal heat and scanty dark urine.

processing_methods.name: 生木瓜
processing_methods.effect: 舒筋活络，和胃化湿

processing_methods.name: 炒木瓜
processing_methods.effect: 缓和酸敛之性，偏于和胃止呕

safety_notes_zh: 味酸入肝，舒筋活络，为治湿痹筋脉拘挛、脚气转筋之要药。药用宣木瓜（Chaenomeles speciosa）非食用番木瓜（Carica papaya），两者科属、功用迥异。
safety_notes_en: Sour in flavor and enters the liver; relaxes sinews and activates the network vessels; a key herb for damp bi-syndrome with sinew cramping and leg qi with muscle cramps. The medicinal variety is xuan mu gua (Chaenomeles speciosa), not the edible papaya (Carica papaya); the two differ greatly in taxonomy and function.

classical_references.passage: 木瓜，治脚气冲心，腹胀不仁，筋脉拘挛，足胫肿痛。

description_zh: 蔷薇科植物贴梗海棠（宣木瓜）的干燥近成熟果实。归肝、脾经。味酸入肝，善舒筋活络，为治风湿痹痛、筋脉拘急及脚气转筋之要药；又能和胃化湿、生津开胃，用于暑湿吐泻、转筋腹痛。须与食用番木瓜（Carica papaya，蕃木瓜）严格区分。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

active_constituents.name_zh: 有机酸（苹果酸、柠檬酸、酒石酸）

summary.zh: 木瓜（Chaenomeles）含有机酸、三萜酸及黄酮，具抗炎、解痉、舒筋活络及保肝作用。
summary.en: Chaenomeles fruit contains organic acids, triterpene acids, and flavonoids with anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, muscle-relaxing, and hepatoprotective effects.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 湿痹筋脉拘挛及腓肠肌痉挛
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Damp-bi with muscle cramping and calf spasm

evidence_notes.note_zh: 木瓜酸温入肝脾，舒筋活络化湿和胃，为湿痹腰腿痛常用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Sour and warm, entering liver and spleen — relaxes sinews, activates channels, transforms damp, and harmonizes stomach; common for damp-bi.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 齐墩果酸具保肝作用；有机酸缓解肌肉痉挛。
evidence_notes.note_en: Oleanolic acid is hepatoprotective; organic acids relieve muscle spasm.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》以齐墩果酸和熊果酸总量（不少于 0.50%）为质量指标。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chinese Pharmacopoeia specifies combined oleanolic and ursolic acid (≥0.50%) as the quality marker.

description_en: The dried nearly ripe fruit of Chaenomeles speciosa (Rosaceae). Enters the Liver and Spleen channels. Sour and warm, its sour flavor enters the Liver, where it excels at relaxing sinews and unblocking network vessels. It is the key herb for wind-damp impediment pain, sinew-vessel contracture, and leg qi with cramping. It must be strictly distinguished from the edible papaya (Carica papaya) of the Caricaceae family.

actions.en: Harmonize the middle and stop vomiting
Release the exterior and relieve summerheat
Open the appetite and promote eating

indications.zh: 湿浊中阻，脘腹痞满，食欲不振
indications.en: Damp turbidity obstructing the middle with epigastric and abdominal fullness and poor appetite
Nausea and vomiting from dampness obstructing the middle jiao

indications.zh: 暑湿表证，发热头痛，胸闷
indications.en: Summerheat-damp exterior pattern with fever, headache, and chest oppression
Early-stage damp-warm disease with fever and fatigue

indications.zh: 寒湿闭暑之外感风寒、内伤生冷
indications.en: Cold-damp blocking summerheat from external wind-cold invasion and internal injury from raw and cold foods

contraindications_zh: 阴虚血燥、胃热作呕者不宜。
contraindications_en: Not suitable in yin vacuity with blood dryness and stomach heat with vomiting.

dosage_range.notes: 鲜品加倍（15–30g）。治暑湿宜鲜藿香，解表化湿力胜；干品多用于湿阻中焦。不宜久煎以免香气散失。

processing_methods.name: 鲜藿香
processing_methods.effect: 发表解暑、芳香化湿力胜，夏令暑湿证尤宜

processing_methods.name: 干藿香
processing_methods.effect: 化湿和中，止呕开胃

processing_methods.name: 藿香叶
processing_methods.effect: 偏于发表解暑

processing_methods.name: 藿香梗
processing_methods.effect: 偏于和中止呕

safety_notes_zh: 现代药用藿香多为唇形科广藿香（Pogostemon cablin）。古方藿香正气散为代表方，治外感风寒、内伤湿滞之暑湿证。不宜久煎，后下或短煎以存其芳香之气。
safety_notes_en: The modern medicinal huo xiang is mostly guang huo xiang (Pogostemon cablin) of the Lamiaceae family. The classical formula Huo Xiang Zheng Qi San is the representative formula for treating exterior wind-cold with internal dampness stagnation in summerheat-dampness patterns. Should not be decocted for too long; add near the end or decoct briefly to preserve its aromatic properties.

classical_references.passage: 藿香正气散，治伤寒头疼，憎寒壮热，上喘咳嗽，五劳七伤，八般风痰，五般膈气，心腹冷痛，反胃呕恶，气泻霍乱。

description_zh: 唇形科植物广藿香的干燥地上部分。归脾、胃、肺经。化湿药代表药，芳香性温而不燥烈，既能芳化中焦湿浊、和中止呕，又能外解暑湿表邪，为暑月外感风寒、内伤生冷之要药，局方藿香正气散即以之为君。鲜品较干品发表解暑之力更胜，夏季暑湿证尤宜。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 藿香挥发油（广藿香醇等）具抗菌、抗真菌、解痉、调节胃肠动力及抗炎作用。
summary.en: Agastache/Pogostemon essential oil (notably patchoulol) shows antibacterial, antifungal, antispasmodic, gut-motility modulating, and anti-inflammatory activity.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 口腔及皮肤真菌感染外用
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Topical oral and dermal fungal infection

evidence_notes.note_zh: 广藿香醇抑制金黄色葡萄球菌和白色念珠菌；调节胃肠平滑肌。
evidence_notes.note_en: Patchoulol inhibits S. aureus and Candida albicans and modulates GI smooth muscle tone.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 藿香芳香化湿，醒脾开胃，暑月常用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Aromatic herb that transforms damp and revives the spleen; a summer-season staple.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》以广藿香醇和广藿香酮为质量控制指标。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chinese Pharmacopoeia specifies patchoulol and pogostone as quality markers.

description_en: The dried aerial part of Pogostemon cablin (Lamiaceae). Enters the Spleen, Stomach, and Lung channels. Pungent and slightly warm, it is the representative aromatic dampness-transforming herb: warm yet not harshly drying, it aromatically transforms middle-burner dampness-turbidity, harmonizes the middle and stops vomiting, and releases summerheat exterior patterns. It serves as the chief in Huo Xiang Zheng Qi San for summer external wind-cold with internal dampness.

actions.en: Arouse the Spleen and open the appetite
Release the exterior and relieve summerheat

indications.en: Damp turbidity obstructing the middle with epigastric fullness and nausea
Sweet-greasy taste in the mouth and halitosis (Spleen dan disorder)
Summerheat-damp exterior pattern with head distention and chest oppression
Early-stage damp-warm disease
Poor appetite and fatigue with thick white greasy tongue coating

contraindications_zh: 阴虚血燥者、气虚者不宜。
contraindications_en: Not suitable in yin vacuity with blood dryness and qi vacuity.

dosage_range.notes: 鲜品加倍（15–30g），暑湿、湿温证以鲜佩兰为佳。不宜久煎。

processing_methods.name: 鲜佩兰
processing_methods.effect: 化湿解暑力强，夏令暑湿证尤宜

processing_methods.name: 干佩兰
processing_methods.effect: 芳香化湿，醒脾开胃

safety_notes_zh: 《黄帝内经·素问·奇病论》载有治脾瘅一方：治之以兰，除陈气也，即指本品。为治口甘苔腻、湿浊困脾之专药。不宜久煎以免香气散失。
safety_notes_en: Huangdi Neijing Suwen (Qi Bing Lun) records a formula for spleen-dampness: 'treat with lan to eliminate stale qi,' referring to this herb. A specialized herb for treating sweet taste in the mouth with greasy tongue coating and turbid dampness encumbering the spleen. Should not be decocted for too long to prevent loss of aromatic properties.

classical_references.passage: 有病口甘者，病名为何？何以得之？岐伯曰：此五气之溢也，名曰脾瘅。治之以兰，除陈气也。

description_zh: 菊科植物佩兰的干燥地上部分。归脾、胃、肺经。芳香化湿之要药，与藿香相须为用，然佩兰性平，偏于化湿醒脾，藿香微温，偏于和中止呕。《内经》治脾瘅口甘一证首推本品，谓之除陈气，即后世湿浊内蕴化热之的对之药。暑湿、湿温初起，鲜品力胜。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 佩兰为菊科泽兰，含挥发油及香豆素类成分，具抗炎、祛痰与抑菌作用，对应传统芳香化湿、醒脾开胃之功。
summary.en: Eupatorium (Pei Lan) yields a volatile oil and coumarin compounds with anti-inflammatory, expectorant, and antimicrobial actions, corresponding to its traditional aromatic dampness-transforming and appetite-rousing use.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Summer-damp colds with chest oppression and epigastric fullness
Sweet/sticky taste in mouth from middle-jiao dampness

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中药学》（Bensky）强调佩兰常与藿香同用化湿，宜后下以保存挥发油。
evidence_notes.note_en: Bensky notes Pei Lan is typically paired with Huo Xiang for dampness transformation and should be added late in decoction to preserve its volatile oil.

evidence_notes.note_zh: Chen & Chen 记载佩兰挥发油对多种呼吸道病原菌有抑制作用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen report that Pei Lan volatile oil inhibits several respiratory pathogens in vitro.

description_en: The dried aerial part of Eupatorium fortunei (Asteraceae). Enters the Spleen, Stomach, and Lung channels. Pungent and neutral, it is the key aromatic dampness-transforming herb, often paired with huo xiang in synergy. While pei lan is neutral and excels at transforming dampness and arousing the Spleen, huo xiang is slightly warm and excels at harmonizing the middle and stopping vomiting. The Nei Jing recommends pei lan for Spleen impediment (pi dan) with sweet taste in the mouth.

actions.en: Warm the middle and stop diarrhea
Regulate qi and quiet the fetus
Open the appetite and disperse food stagnation

indications.en: Dampness obstructing the middle jiao with epigastric and abdominal fullness

indications.zh: 脾胃气滞，食少纳呆，嗳气呕恶
indications.en: Spleen-Stomach qi stagnation with poor appetite, belching, and nausea
Spleen-Stomach vacuity cold with vomiting, diarrhea, and abdominal pain
Morning sickness and restless fetus in pregnancy
Cold-damp diarrhea and dysentery

contraindications_zh: 阴虚血燥、火热内盛者慎用。不宜久煎。
contraindications_en: Use with caution in yin vacuity with blood dryness and exuberant internal fire-heat. Should not be decocted for too long.

dosage_range.notes: 入煎剂宜后下（在其他药煎成前5分钟加入），以存其芳香之气。打碎使用。过早入煎则有效挥发油散失。

processing_methods.name: 生砂仁
processing_methods.effect: 化湿行气，温中安胎

processing_methods.name: 盐砂仁
processing_methods.effect: 引药下行，偏于温肾缩尿

safety_notes_zh: 后下药——在其他药煎至将成时（约最后5分钟）打碎投入，以免挥发性有效成分（乙酸龙脑酯等）散失。阴虚火旺者不宜。为妇科安胎良药，与白术相配治妊娠气滞恶阻、胎动不安。
safety_notes_en: An 'add near the end' herb — crush and add during the last approximately 5 minutes of decoction to prevent loss of volatile active constituents (bornyl acetate, etc.). Not suitable in yin vacuity with effulgent fire. An excellent herb for calming the fetus in gynecology; when combined with bai zhu, it treats qi stagnation with morning sickness and restless fetus during pregnancy.

classical_references.passage: 缩砂蜜，治一切气，宿食不消，赤白泻痢，腹中虚痛，消化水谷，温暖脾胃。

description_zh: 姜科植物阳春砂、绿壳砂或海南砂的干燥成熟果实。归脾、胃、肾经。化湿药中兼行气温中、安胎之要药，为醒脾调胃、行气止痛之常用品。入煎剂须打碎后下，以存其芳香挥发之气。与白术相配善治妊娠气滞恶阻及胎动不安，香砂六君子汤、香砂养胃丸皆以之为主药。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 砂仁为姜科豆蔻属果实，主含挥发油（乙酸龙脑酯、樟脑等），现代研究显示促胃肠动力、抗溃疡、抗炎及利胆作用，对应传统化湿行气、温中止泻、安胎之功。
summary.en: Amomum fruit is dominated by a volatile oil rich in bornyl acetate, camphor, and limonene, with preclinical evidence for prokinetic, antiulcer, anti-inflammatory, and choleretic effects that align with its traditional dampness-transforming, middle-warming, and fetus-calming use.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 脾胃气滞湿阻、脘腹胀满
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Spleen-stomach qi stagnation with dampness and epigastric fullness

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中药学》（Bensky）强调砂仁宜打碎后下以保存挥发油，阴虚血燥者慎用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Bensky notes Sha Ren should be crushed and added late in decoction to preserve its volatile oil; use cautiously in yin-deficient/blood-dry patients.

evidence_notes.note_zh: Chen & Chen 综述砂仁挥发油可促进胃酸分泌与胃肠蠕动，对应其醒脾开胃之效。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen summarize that Sha Ren volatile oil promotes gastric secretion and GI motility, matching its traditional appetite-rousing action.

description_en: The dried ripe fruit of Amomum villosum, A. villosum var. xanthioides, or A. longiligulare (Zingiberaceae). Enters the Spleen, Stomach, and Kidney channels. Pungent and warm, it transforms dampness, moves qi, warms the middle, stops diarrhea, and calms the fetus. It must be crushed and added near the end of decoction to preserve its aromatic volatile constituents. Paired with bai zhu for pregnancy qi stagnation with nausea and threatened miscarriage.

actions.en: Strengthen the Spleen and stop diarrhea
Dispel painful obstruction and relax sinews

indications.en: Edema, beriberi, and difficult urination
Spleen vacuity with dampness, diarrhea and loose stools

indications.zh: 湿痹筋脉拘挛，屈伸不利
indications.en: Damp painful obstruction with cramping of sinews and impaired flexion and extension
Lung abscess with chest pain and coughing of purulent phlegm
Intestinal abscess with abdominal pain

contraindications_zh: 津液不足者及孕妇慎用。脾虚无湿、大便燥结者不宜。
contraindications_en: Use with caution in fluid insufficiency and during pregnancy. Not suitable in spleen vacuity without dampness and dry bound stool.

dosage_range.notes: 常用9–30g；本品力缓，用量宜大。健脾止泻宜炒用，余皆生用。

processing_methods.name: 生薏苡仁
processing_methods.effect: 长于利水渗湿、清热排脓、除痹

processing_methods.name: 炒薏苡仁
processing_methods.effect: 健脾止泻作用增强，多用于脾虚泄泻

safety_notes_zh: 现代研究未见明显毒性。孕妇慎用，因本品滑利渗下，有学说谓其可致子宫收缩。
safety_notes_en: Modern research shows no significant toxicity. Use with caution during pregnancy; this herb is slippery and percolating, and some theories suggest it may cause uterine contractions.

classical_references.passage: 味甘，微寒。主筋急拘挛不可屈伸，风湿痹，下气。久服轻身益气。

description_zh: 禾本科植物薏苡的干燥成熟种仁。归脾、胃、肺经。为利水渗湿兼健脾之要药，药食两用，《金匮要略》薏苡附子败酱散治肠痈、麻杏苡甘汤治风湿身痛为其代表应用。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 薏苡仁含薏苡仁酯、薏苡素及多糖，具有抗肿瘤、抗炎、镇痛、免疫调节和降糖作用；康莱特注射液（薏苡仁油乳剂）为国内肿瘤辅助治疗上市品种。
summary.en: Coix seed contains coixenolide, coixol and polysaccharides with antitumor, anti-inflammatory, analgesic, immunomodulatory and hypoglycemic effects; Kanglaite injection (a coix seed oil emulsion) is a marketed adjunct oncology product in China.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 肺癌、肝癌等恶性肿瘤辅助治疗
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Adjuvant treatment of lung, liver and other malignancies
Diarrhea and edema from spleen vacuity with damp accumulation

evidence_notes.note_zh: Chen & Chen 介绍康莱特注射液为薏苡仁油脂肪乳剂，联合化疗可改善晚期癌症患者生存质量。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen describe Kanglaite injection as a coix seed oil lipid emulsion that improves quality of life when combined with chemotherapy in advanced cancer.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》收载薏苡仁，以脂肪油含量为质量指标之一。
evidence_notes.note_en: The Chinese Pharmacopoeia lists Coix with fatty oil content among its quality criteria.

description_en: The dried mature seed kernel of Coix lacryma-jobi var. ma-yuen (Poaceae). Enters the Spleen, Stomach, and Lung channels. Sweet, bland, and cool, it promotes urination and percolates dampness while strengthening the Spleen, making it both food and medicinal. Zhang Zhongjing's Yi Yi Fu Zi Bai Jiang San treats intestinal abscess, and Ma Xing Yi Gan Tang addresses wind-damp body pain.

actions.en: Promote urination and unblock painful urinary dribbling
Clear the Liver and brighten the eyes
Clear the Lung and transform phlegm

indications.en: Heat-type painful dribbling urination and difficult urination
Edema with distention and fullness
Summerheat-damp diarrhea with scanty reddish urine
Red swollen painful eyes and dim blurry vision
Phlegm-heat cough

contraindications_zh: 肾虚精滑及内无湿热者慎用。孕妇慎用。
contraindications_en: Use with caution in kidney vacuity with seminal efflux and absence of internal damp-heat. Use with caution during pregnancy.

dosage_range.notes: 入汤剂宜包煎，以防其黏滑影响药液。

processing_methods.name: 生车前子
processing_methods.effect: 长于利水通淋、清肝明目

processing_methods.name: 盐车前子
processing_methods.effect: 引药下行入肾，增强益肝明目之功

safety_notes_zh: 本品性寒而滑利，肾虚精滑者及无湿热之小便不利者忌用。孕妇慎用。包煎以避黏糊。
safety_notes_en: Cold and slippery in nature; contraindicated in kidney vacuity with seminal efflux and difficult urination without damp-heat. Use with caution during pregnancy. Wrap-decoct (bao jian) to prevent sticking.

classical_references.passage: 味甘，寒。主气癃止痛，利水道小便，除湿痹。久服轻身耐老。

description_zh: 车前科植物车前或平车前的干燥成熟种子。归肝、肾、肺、小肠经。为利水通淋之常用药，通利水道而不伤阴，兼能明目祛痰，历代多用于淋证与暑湿泄泻。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 车前子富含黏液质多糖、环烯醚萜苷（桃叶珊瑚苷）及黄酮类，具有利尿、缓泻、镇咳祛痰、抗炎及降尿酸作用。
summary.en: Plantaginis Semen contains mucilaginous polysaccharides, iridoid glycosides (aucubin), and flavonoids, with diuretic, bulk-laxative, antitussive/expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and uricosuric activity.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 功能性便秘（黏液质容积性缓泻）
湿热淋证（尿路感染）辅助治疗
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Adjunct for damp-heat urinary tract infection

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 高脂血症（Plantago ovata 膳食纤维研究)
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Hyperlipidemia (evidence from Plantago ovata dietary fiber studies)

evidence_notes.note_zh: WHO 专论收录车前子（Plantago）黏液质用于便秘、腹泻（双向调节）和高胆固醇血症的临床证据。
evidence_notes.note_en: WHO monographs document clinical evidence for Plantago seeds/husks in constipation, diarrhea (bidirectional), and hypercholesterolemia.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中药学》（Bensky）指出车前子入煎剂应包煎，以防黏液质溢出影响汤液。
evidence_notes.note_en: Bensky notes that Che Qian Zi should be decocted in a cloth bag (bao jian) to prevent mucilage from clouding the decoction.

description_en: The dried ripe seed of Plantago asiatica or P. depressa (Plantaginaceae). Enters the Liver, Kidney, Lung, and Small Intestine channels. Sweet and cold, it is a widely used herb for promoting urination and relieving strangury that disinhibits the water passages without injuring yin. It additionally brightens the eyes and expels phlegm, and has been used across dynasties for strangury patterns and summerheat-dampness diarrhea.

actions.en: Benefit the gallbladder and resolve jaundice
Course the Liver and benefit the gallbladder

indications.en: Jaundice with scanty urine and yellowing of the body and eyes
Yang-type jaundice from internal damp-heat accumulation
Yin-type jaundice from cold-damp obstruction
Damp skin lesions with itching
Damp-warm and summerheat-damp patterns with fever and heavy head

contraindications_zh: 蓄血发黄者及血虚萎黄者慎用。非因湿热者不宜。
contraindications_en: Use with caution in jaundice from blood stasis and sallow complexion from blood vacuity. Not suitable when the condition is not due to damp-heat.

dosage_range.notes: 外用适量，煎汤熏洗。治黄疸宜重用。

processing_methods.name: 生茵陈
processing_methods.effect: 清利湿热退黄力强，为退黄要药

safety_notes_zh: 本品苦泄寒凉，脾胃虚寒者慎用。现代药理研究显示有利胆、保肝作用，但过量可致胃肠不适。
safety_notes_en: Bitter, draining, and cold-cool; use with caution in spleen-stomach vacuity cold. Modern pharmacological research shows choleretic and hepatoprotective effects, but overdose may cause gastrointestinal discomfort.

classical_references.passage: 伤寒七八日，身黄如橘子色，小便不利，腹微满者，茵陈蒿汤主之。

description_zh: 菊科植物滨蒿或茵陈蒿的干燥地上部分。春季采收者习称绵茵陈，秋季采收者习称花茵陈。归脾、胃、肝、胆经。为治黄疸之要药，《伤寒论》茵陈蒿汤配栀子、大黄以治阳黄。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

active_constituents.name_zh: 6,7-二甲氧基香豆素 / 茵陈色原酮

summary.zh: 茵陈含 6,7-二甲氧基香豆素、茵陈色原酮及滨蒿内酯等成分，具有显著的利胆、保肝、降脂及解热作用，为清热利湿退黄的药理依据。
summary.en: Artemisia capillaris contains 6,7-dimethylesculetin, capillarisin and scoparone with strong choleretic, hepatoprotective, hypolipidemic and antipyretic activities, providing the pharmacological basis for its heat-clearing, damp-draining and jaundice-resolving actions.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 急慢性肝炎、胆囊炎之黄疸
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Jaundice in acute or chronic hepatitis and cholecystitis

evidence_notes.note_zh: Chen & Chen 引述茵陈蒿汤及茵陈单味制剂在中国广泛用于新生儿黄疸，临床研究支持。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen cite Yin Chen Hao Tang and single-herb preparations in widespread Chinese clinical use for neonatal jaundice supported by clinical studies.

evidence_notes.note_zh: Brinker 提示个别报告显示大剂量长期使用茵陈可能引起肝毒性，须注意监测。
evidence_notes.note_en: Brinker notes isolated reports of hepatotoxicity with large or prolonged doses; monitoring is advised.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》以滨蒿内酯为含量测定指标。
evidence_notes.note_en: The Chinese Pharmacopoeia uses scoparone as an assay marker.

description_en: The dried aerial part of Artemisia scoparia or A. capillaris (Asteraceae). Enters the Spleen, Stomach, Liver, and Gallbladder channels. Bitter, pungent, and cool, spring-harvested material is called 'mian yin chen' (cotton artemisia) and autumn-harvested is called 'hua yin chen' (flower artemisia). It is the essential herb for resolving jaundice. In Yin Chen Hao Tang it is combined with zhi zi and da huang to treat yang-type jaundice.

actions.en: Promote urination and unblock painful urinary dribbling

indications.en: Heat-type painful dribbling urination with scanty reddish urine
Stone-type painful urinary dribbling with bloody urine
Summerheat-damp with irritable thirst, fever and diarrhea
Damp rashes, damp sores, and prickly heat with itching
Early-stage damp-warm disease with pathogens in the qi level

contraindications_zh: 脾虚气弱，精滑及热病伤津者忌用。孕妇慎用。
contraindications_en: Contraindicated in spleen vacuity with qi weakness, seminal efflux, and fluid damage from febrile disease. Use with caution during pregnancy.

dosage_range.notes: 入汤剂宜包煎。外用适量，研末撒或调敷。

processing_methods.name: 生滑石
processing_methods.effect: 清热利湿、收湿敛疮，内服外用皆宜

safety_notes_zh: 本品为含水硅酸镁矿物，不可吸入粉尘。孕妇及脾虚者慎用。现代制剂应注意质量，防止滑石粉污染。
safety_notes_en: This herb is a hydrous magnesium silicate mineral; do not inhale its dust. Use with caution during pregnancy and in spleen vacuity. Modern preparations should ensure quality control to prevent talcum powder contamination.

classical_references.passage: 脉洪滑，面赤身热头晕，不恶寒但恶热，舌上黄滑苔，渴欲凉饮，饮不解渴，得水则呕，按之胸下痛，小便短，大便闭者，阳明暑温，水结在胸也，小陷胸汤加枳实主之；热入营中者，加银花滑石之属。

description_zh: 硅酸盐类矿物滑石族滑石，主含含水硅酸镁。归膀胱、肺、胃经。为治暑湿及热淋之要药，《伤寒论》猪苓汤、《医学心悟》六一散（滑石六、甘草一）皆以之为主。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 滑石为天然含水硅酸镁矿物，内服经肠道吸附水分与毒物、外用形成保护膜，药理示其清热利湿、吸附保护与抗菌辅助作用。
summary.en: Huashi is natural hydrated magnesium silicate (talc); orally it adsorbs fluid and toxins in the gut, topically it forms a protective film. Actions include clearing heat-damp, adsorbent protection and antibacterial support.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 湿热淋证（尿频尿痛）、暑湿泄泻
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Damp-heat lin pattern (dysuria) and summer-damp diarrhea
Topical eczema and heat rash (talc powder)

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》规定滑石不得检出石棉纤维，入煎剂须包煎。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chinese Pharmacopoeia requires talc to be free of asbestos fibers; decoct in a cloth bag.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 六一散（滑石:甘草=6:1）为清暑利湿经典方。
evidence_notes.note_en: Liu Yi San (talc:gancao = 6:1) is the classical summer-heat damp-resolving formula.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 外用含石棉的滑石存在致癌风险；药用滑石须严格检测纤维杂质。
evidence_notes.note_en: Asbestos-contaminated talc carries carcinogenic risk; medicinal talc must be tested for fibrous impurities.

description_en: Hydrated magnesium silicate mineral (talc). Enters the Bladder, Lung, and Stomach channels. Sweet, bland, and cold, it is the key mineral medicinal for summerheat-dampness and heat strangury. Featured in Zhang Zhongjing's Zhu Ling Tang and the Song-dynasty formula Liu Yi San (talc six parts, gan cao one part).

actions.en: Course the Liver and direct qi downward
Direct counterflow downward and stop vomiting

indications.en: Jueyin headache with dry retching and spitting of foamy saliva
Cold hernia abdominal pain and dysmenorrhea
Epigastric and abdominal distending pain with vomiting and acid regurgitation
Spleen-Kidney yang vacuity with daybreak diarrhea
Cold-damp beriberi with swelling and pain

contraindications_zh: 本品辛热燥烈，有小毒，不宜多服久服。阴虚火旺者忌用。孕妇慎用。
contraindications_en: This herb is acrid, hot, and intensely drying with slight toxicity; not suitable for excessive or prolonged use. Contraindicated in yin vacuity with effulgent fire. Use with caution during pregnancy.

dosage_range.notes: 内服用量宜小，一般2–5g；外用适量。因有小毒，不宜久服。

processing_methods.name: 生吴茱萸
processing_methods.effect: 多外用，内服少用，刺激性较强

processing_methods.name: 制吴茱萸
processing_methods.effect: 降低毒性与燥烈之性，多用于内服

safety_notes_zh: 本品有小毒，内服不宜多量久服，过量可致视力障碍、错觉、毛发脱落及腹痛等反应。阴虚火旺者忌用。孕妇慎用。
safety_notes_en: This herb is slightly toxic; internal use should not be excessive or prolonged, as overdose may cause visual disturbances, hallucinations, hair loss, and abdominal pain. Contraindicated in yin vacuity with effulgent fire. Use with caution during pregnancy.

classical_references.passage: 食谷欲呕者，属阳明也，吴茱萸汤主之。

description_zh: 芸香科植物吴茱萸、石虎或疏毛吴茱萸将近成熟的干燥果实。归肝、脾、胃、肾经。为治厥阴头痛与肝胃虚寒之要药，《伤寒论》吴茱萸汤为其代表方。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 吴茱萸主含吴茱萸碱、吴茱萸次碱等吲哚喹唑啉生物碱，具有镇痛、止呕、血管舒张及抗溃疡作用。
summary.en: Evodia fruit contains indoloquinazoline alkaloids (evodiamine, rutaecarpine) with analgesic, antiemetic, vasorelaxant and anti-ulcer effects.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 寒凝肝脉之头痛、胃脘冷痛
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Cold-stagnation Liver headache and cold epigastric pain
Jueyin headache with vomiting of clear fluid

evidence_notes.note_zh: 散寒止痛、疏肝下气、助阳止泻，为吴茱萸汤君药；小毒，不宜过量久服。
evidence_notes.note_en: Disperses cold, moves Liver qi downward, warms yang and stops diarrhea; sovereign herb of Wu Zhu Yu Tang; slightly toxic, should not be overdosed or used long-term.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 高剂量或长期使用有肝毒性报告；吴茱萸碱可影响多种 CYP 酶和 P-糖蛋白。
evidence_notes.note_en: Hepatotoxicity has been reported with high-dose or prolonged use; evodiamine modulates multiple CYP enzymes and P-glycoprotein.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》以吴茱萸碱和吴茱萸次碱总量作含量测定指标。
evidence_notes.note_en: The Chinese Pharmacopoeia uses combined evodiamine plus rutaecarpine as the assay marker.

description_en: The dried nearly ripe fruit of Evodia rutaecarpa, E. rutaecarpa var. officinalis, or E. rutaecarpa var. bodinieri (Rutaceae). Enters the Liver, Spleen, Stomach, and Kidney channels. Pungent, bitter, and hot, it is the key herb for jue yin headache and Liver-Stomach vacuity cold. Zhang Zhongjing's Wu Zhu Yu Tang is its representative formula, treating both vertex headache from jue yin cold and Stomach-cold vomiting.

actions.en: Tonify the fire and assist yang
Lead fire back to its source
Warm the channels and unblock the vessels

indications.zh: 肾阳不足，命门火衰，畏寒肢冷
indications.en: Kidney yang vacuity with debilitated Mingmen fire, aversion to cold and cold limbs
Cold painful lower back and knees, impotence and seminal emission

indications.zh: 虚阳上浮之面赤、虚喘、汗出
indications.en: Upward floating of deficient yang with red face, deficient wheezing and sweating
Cold epigastric and abdominal pain, cold hernia abdominal pain

indications.zh: 冲任虚寒之痛经经闭，阴疽漫肿
indications.en: Chong and Ren vacuity-cold dysmenorrhea and amenorrhea, yin-type sores with diffuse swelling

contraindications_zh: 阴虚火旺、里有实热、血热妄行者忌用。孕妇慎用。畏赤石脂。
contraindications_en: Contraindicated in yin vacuity with effulgent fire, internal repletion heat, and reckless movement of blood due to blood heat. Use with caution during pregnancy. Incompatible with chi shi zhi (Halloysitum Rubrum).

dosage_range.notes: 煎服1–5g，宜后下或焗服；研末冲服每次1–2g。因含挥发油，不宜久煎。

processing_methods.name: 肉桂
processing_methods.effect: 补火助阳、散寒止痛、温通经脉，为温里要药

safety_notes_zh: 本品大辛大热，纯阳燥烈，阴虚火旺、里有实热者忌用。孕妇慎用。不宜与赤石脂同用（十九畏）。含挥发油，煎煮宜后下以保药效。
safety_notes_en: This herb is extremely acrid and hot, purely yang and intensely drying; contraindicated in yin vacuity with effulgent fire and internal repletion heat. Use with caution during pregnancy. Should not be used concurrently with chi shi zhi per the Nineteen Incompatibilities (shijiu wei): rou gui is incompatible with chi shi zhi. Contains volatile oils; should be added near the end of decoction to preserve efficacy.

classical_references.passage: 牡桂，味辛，温。主上气咳逆，结气，喉痹吐吸，利关节，补中益气。久服通神，轻身不老。

description_zh: 樟科植物肉桂的干燥树皮，与桂枝（同株之嫩枝）来源同而部位、功效有别：桂枝偏表，温经通阳以解肌；肉桂入里，补火助阳以归元。归肾、脾、心、肝经。为温补命门、引火归元之要药，《金匮要略》肾气丸即以其配附子温补肾阳。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

active_constituents.notes: present at meaningful levels in Cinnamomum cassia — hepatotoxic at chronic high intake

summary.zh: 肉桂（官桂）富含桂皮醛、桂皮酸、香豆素及原花青素，现代研究显示其具抗菌、抗炎、扩张血管、改善胰岛素敏感性作用；中国药典所用为 Cinnamomum cassia，其香豆素含量较锡兰肉桂高，长期大剂量应关注肝毒风险。
summary.en: Cinnamomum cassia bark contains cinnamaldehyde, cinnamic acid, coumarin, and procyanidins, with reported antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, vasodilatory, and insulin-sensitizing effects. Cassia (the Pharmacopoeia species) contains meaningfully higher coumarin than Ceylon cinnamon; chronic high-dose use warrants attention to hepatotoxicity.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Kidney-yang vacuity, decline of mingmen fire

evidence_notes.note_zh: 中国药典 2020 版收载肉桂为樟科植物肉桂 Cinnamomum cassia 的干燥树皮，规定挥发油含量下限及桂皮醛含量。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chinese Pharmacopoeia 2020 defines Rou Gui as the dried bark of Cinnamomum cassia, specifying minimum volatile oil and cinnamaldehyde content.

evidence_notes.note_zh: Brinker 警示 Cassia 肉桂每克含香豆素数毫克，长期大剂量（尤其糖尿病辅助用法）可能引起肝损伤；建议与华法林合用时监测 INR。
evidence_notes.note_en: Brinker warns that Cassia cinnamon contains several mg/g of coumarin; chronic high-dose use (especially as a diabetes adjunct) can cause hepatotoxicity, and INR should be monitored if combined with warfarin.

evidence_notes.note_zh: Chen & Chen 综述桂皮醛具扩血管、抗凝及抗菌活性，阴虚火旺者与孕妇禁用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen summarize cinnamaldehyde's vasodilatory, antiplatelet, and antimicrobial activities; contraindicated in yin-vacuity heat and pregnancy.

description_en: The dried bark of Cinnamomum cassia (Lauraceae), from the same tree as gui zhi (the twig) but with different part and function: gui zhi acts on the exterior to warm channels and release the muscle layer, while rou gui enters the interior to supplement fire and assist yang. Enters the Kidney, Spleen, Heart, and Liver channels. Pungent, sweet, and hot, it is the essential herb for warming the gate of vitality and leading fire back to its source, as in Jin Gui Shen Qi Wan.

actions.en: Regulate qi and harmonize the Stomach
Warm the Liver and dissipate cold
Warm the Kidney and alleviate pain

indications.en: Cold hernia with abdominal pain
Testicular swelling with dragging pain
Cold pain in the lower abdomen, dysmenorrhea
Epigastric and abdominal distending pain, poor appetite with vomiting and diarrhea
Middle-jiao vacuity-cold with vomiting and poor appetite

contraindications_zh: 阴虚火旺者忌用。本品辛温，实热证者不宜。
contraindications_en: Contraindicated in yin vacuity with effulgent fire. This herb is acrid and warm; not suitable in repletion heat patterns.

dosage_range.notes: 煎服3–6g；外用适量，研末调敷或炒热布包熨患处。

processing_methods.name: 生小茴香
processing_methods.effect: 理气和胃，散寒止痛

processing_methods.name: 盐小茴香
processing_methods.effect: 引药入肾，增强温肾散寒止痛之功，多用于寒疝

safety_notes_zh: 现代研究毒性甚低。注意与大茴香（八角茴香，Illicium verum）区分：大茴香为木兰科八角的干燥果实，香气更烈，亦能温中散寒，但本品（小茴香）为伞形科茴香的干燥果实，作用更偏理气暖肝。
safety_notes_en: Modern research shows very low toxicity. Distinguish from da hui xiang (star anise, Illicium verum): da hui xiang is the dried fruit of the Magnoliaceae star anise with a more intense aroma, also warming the middle and dispersing cold, while this herb (xiao hui xiang) is the dried fruit of the Apiaceae fennel, with actions more oriented toward regulating qi and warming the liver.

classical_references.passage: 治肾经虚寒，小腹疼痛，阴核肿胀，及寒疝偏坠，宜用小茴香、川楝子、木香、吴茱萸等分为末，酒糊为丸。

description_zh: 伞形科植物茴香的干燥成熟果实。归肝、肾、脾、胃经。与八角茴香（大茴香，木兰科）不同科属，本品体小而气清香，为暖肝散寒、理气止痛之要药，《太平惠民和剂局方》天台乌药散为其代表应用。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

active_constituents.notes: dominant volatile oil component, carminative and mild estrogenic

summary.zh: 小茴香挥发油以反式茴香脑为主，具有促进胃肠蠕动、解痉、驱风、抗菌及轻度雌激素样作用，契合散寒止痛、理气和胃之功。
summary.en: Fennel volatile oil, dominated by trans-anethole, exhibits prokinetic, antispasmodic, carminative, antimicrobial and mild estrogenic effects, matching its role in warming the middle and regulating qi.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Cold hernia pain and stomach-cold vomiting

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 婴幼儿肠绞痛及功能性消化不良

evidence_notes.note_zh: WHO 专论收录茴香用于消化不良和婴儿绞痛，临床试验支持其功效。
evidence_notes.note_en: WHO monograph includes fennel for dyspepsia and infantile colic with supportive clinical trial data.

evidence_notes.note_zh: Brinker 提示茴香脑具有弱雌激素样作用，孕妇大剂量应慎用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Brinker notes anethole's weak estrogenic activity; large doses during pregnancy warrant caution.

description_en: The dried ripe fruit of Foeniculum vulgare (Apiaceae). Enters the Liver, Kidney, Spleen, and Stomach channels. Pungent and warm, it should be distinguished from ba jiao hui xiang (star anise, Illicium verum, Schisandraceae). This small, fragrant fruit is the key herb for warming the Liver and dissipating cold, regulating qi and alleviating pain, especially for cold-type lower abdominal and testicular pain, as in Tian Tai Wu Yao San.

actions.en: Course the Liver and relieve constraint
Regulate qi and relax the middle

indications.en: Liver constraint with qi stagnation, distending pain in the hypochondria
Epigastric and abdominal distending pain, hernia pain
Irregular menstruation with menstrual abdominal pain
Distending pain of the breasts
Cold congealment with qi stagnation, cold epigastric pain

contraindications_zh: 气虚无滞、阴虚血热者慎用。月经先期属血热者不宜。
contraindications_en: Use with caution in qi vacuity without stagnation and yin vacuity with blood heat. Not suitable in early menstruation due to blood heat.

dosage_range.notes: 煎服6–10g。醋炙止痛力增强。

processing_methods.name: 生香附
processing_methods.effect: 偏于行气解郁，多用于胸胁胀痛

processing_methods.name: 醋香附
processing_methods.effect: 引药入肝，增强疏肝止痛之功，为调经止痛常用品

processing_methods.name: 酒香附
processing_methods.effect: 通经脉，多用于妇人经闭、瘀滞

processing_methods.name: 香附炭
processing_methods.effect: 止血，多用于崩漏下血

safety_notes_zh: 现代研究未见明显毒性。气虚及阴虚血热者不宜单用，宜配伍补益或凉血之品。
safety_notes_en: Modern research shows no significant toxicity. Not suitable for sole use in qi vacuity and yin vacuity with blood heat; should be combined with supplementing or blood-cooling herbs.

classical_references.passage: 治一切气疾，心腹胀满，胸膈噎塞，噫气吞酸，胃中痰逆呕吐及宿酒不解，不思饮食，香附子、缩砂仁、甘草为末，名快气汤。

description_zh: 莎草科植物莎草的干燥根茎。归肝、脾、三焦经。为疏肝理气、调经止痛之要药，李时珍誉为气病之总司，女科之主帅。常与柴胡、川芎等相配以疏肝解郁。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

active_constituents.notes: characteristic volatile-oil component with smooth-muscle relaxant and potential estrogenic activity

summary.zh: 香附挥发油富含 α-香附酮等倍半萜类成分，具有解痉、镇痛、轻度雌激素样及抗炎作用，为传统调经止痛的主要活性基础。
summary.en: Cyperus rhizome volatile oil is rich in sesquiterpenes such as alpha-cyperone, which confer smooth-muscle antispasmodic, analgesic, mild estrogenic and anti-inflammatory activities underlying its classical use for menstrual regulation and pain.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 肝郁气滞之月经不调、痛经
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Menstrual irregularity and dysmenorrhea from liver qi stagnation

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 功能性消化不良辅助用药

evidence_notes.note_zh: 香附被称为『气病之总司，女科之主帅』，调理气机为其核心作用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Bensky describes Xiang Fu as chief herb for qi disorders and gynecology, emphasizing its qi-regulating role.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 药理研究提示香附具雌激素样作用，与其治疗妇科气滞证候的经验相符。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen note estrogen-like activity in pharmacological studies, consistent with empirical use in qi-stagnation gynecologic patterns.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》规定以挥发油含量及 α-香附酮为质量控制指标。
evidence_notes.note_en: The Chinese Pharmacopoeia uses volatile oil content and alpha-cyperone as quality markers.

description_en: The dried rhizome of Cyperus rotundus (Cyperaceae). Enters the Liver, Spleen, and Triple Burner channels. Pungent, slightly bitter, slightly sweet, and neutral, it is the key herb for coursing the Liver, relieving constraint, and regulating menstruation. Li Shizhen honored it as the 'Commander-in-Chief of qi diseases and the General of women's medicine.' Commonly paired with chai hu and chuan xiong for Liver-qi stagnation.

actions.en: Strengthen the Spleen and dissolve food stagnation
Course the Liver and benefit the gallbladder
Firm the intestines and stop diarrhea

indications.en: Epigastric and abdominal distending pain, hypochondriac distending pain
Undigested food stagnation with poor appetite
Diarrhea and dysentery with tenesmus
Jaundice with abdominal pain
Spleen-Stomach qi stagnation with belching and vomiting

contraindications_zh: 阴虚津亏、脏腑燥热者慎用。气虚血亏无气滞者不宜单用。
contraindications_en: Use with caution in yin vacuity with fluid depletion and organ dryness-heat. Not suitable for sole use in qi vacuity and blood insufficiency without qi stagnation.

dosage_range.notes: 煎服3–6g；煨用或后下。生用行气力强，煨用止泻力胜。

processing_methods.name: 生木香
processing_methods.effect: 行气止痛作用强，多用于气滞胀痛

processing_methods.name: 煨木香
processing_methods.effect: 行气力缓，实肠止泻，多用于泄痢后重

safety_notes_zh: 本品辛温香燥，阴虚火旺、津液不足者慎用。历史上曾用马兜铃科青木香，因含马兜铃酸有肾毒性，现药典所载木香为菊科云木香，应加以区分。
safety_notes_en: This herb is acrid, warm, aromatic, and drying; use with caution in yin vacuity with effulgent fire and fluid insufficiency. Historically, the Aristolochiaceae-family qing mu xiang was used, which contains nephrotoxic aristolochic acid; the current pharmacopoeia lists mu xiang as Asteraceae-family yun mu xiang, and the two must be distinguished.

classical_references.passage: 治一切气，心腹刺痛，胸膈痞塞，两胁胀满，木香、槟榔等分为末，名木香槟榔丸。

description_zh: 菊科植物木香的干燥根。归脾、胃、大肠、三焦、胆经。为行气止痛之要药，三焦气分之圣药，《太平惠民和剂局方》木香槟榔丸、《局方》香砂六君子汤为其代表应用。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 木香（云木香）富含倍半萜内酯及挥发油，现代研究显示其具有促胃肠动力、解痉、抗炎与抑菌作用，呼应传统行气止痛之功。
summary.en: Aucklandia root is rich in sesquiterpene lactones (notably costunolide and dehydrocostus lactone) and volatile oils, with modern studies indicating prokinetic, antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects consistent with its traditional qi-moving and pain-relieving use.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 功能性消化不良、腹胀腹痛
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Functional dyspepsia, bloating and abdominal pain
