modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 里急后重（痢疾、肠炎辅助）
作为复方中的行气药（如香砂六君子汤）
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Qi-regulator in classical compounds (e.g. Xiang Sha Liu Jun Zi Tang)

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中药学》（Bensky）记载木香挥发油对离体肠管低浓度兴奋、高浓度抑制，符合其双向调节气机之效。
evidence_notes.note_en: Bensky notes that Aucklandia volatile oil stimulates isolated intestinal smooth muscle at low concentration and inhibits it at high concentration, paralleling its bidirectional qi-regulating action.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中药学》（Chen & Chen）指出去氢木香内酯有抗炎及抑菌活性，对多种革兰氏阳性菌有效。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen report that dehydrocostus lactone shows anti-inflammatory activity and inhibits several Gram-positive bacteria.

description_en: The dried root of Aucklandia lappa (syn. Saussurea costus) (Asteraceae). Enters the Spleen, Stomach, Large Intestine, Triple Burner, and Gallbladder channels. Pungent, bitter, and warm, it is the primary herb for moving qi and alleviating pain, called the 'sage herb of the triple burner qi level.' Featured in Mu Xiang Bing Lang Wan and Xiang Sha Liu Jun Zi Tang.

actions.en: Warm the Kidney and dissipate cold
Regulate qi and direct counterflow downward

indications.en: Cold congealment with qi stagnation, chest and abdominal distending pain
Cold hernia with abdominal pain
Menstrual abdominal pain

indications.zh: 肾阳不足，膀胱虚冷，小便频数，遗尿
indications.en: Kidney yang vacuity with cold deficient Bladder, frequent urination and enuresis
Qi counterflow with hurried wheezing

contraindications_zh: 气虚及内热者慎用。孕妇慎用。
contraindications_en: Use with caution in qi vacuity and internal heat. Use with caution during pregnancy.

processing_methods.name: 生乌药
processing_methods.effect: 行气止痛、温肾散寒，为常用饮片

processing_methods.name: 麸炒乌药
processing_methods.effect: 降低燥烈之性，和胃止痛

safety_notes_zh: 本品辛温香散，气血虚而无气滞或内热者不宜。孕妇慎用。现代研究毒性较低。
safety_notes_en: This herb is acrid, warm, aromatic, and dispersing; not suitable in qi and blood vacuity without qi stagnation or internal heat. Use with caution during pregnancy. Modern research shows relatively low toxicity.

classical_references.passage: 治小肠疝气，牵引脐腹疼痛，天台乌药、木香、小茴香（炒）、青皮（去白）、良姜（炒）各半两，槟榔二个，川楝子十个，巴豆七十粒，名天台乌药散。

description_zh: 樟科植物乌药的干燥块根。归肺、脾、肾、膀胱经。为行气散寒止痛之良药，兼温肾缩尿，《太平惠民和剂局方》天台乌药散及缩泉丸为其代表应用。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 乌药主含倍半萜类成分（乌药烷、乌药内酯），具有行气止痛、温肾散寒及抗炎、解痉作用。
summary.en: Lindera root contains sesquiterpenes (linderanes and linderalactones) with qi-moving analgesic, kidney-warming, anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 寒凝气滞之胸腹胁肋胀痛
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Cold-stagnation qi-bound pain in chest, abdomen and flanks

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 肾阳虚之遗尿、小便频数
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Kidney-yang deficient enuresis and urinary frequency

evidence_notes.note_zh: 行气止痛、温肾散寒，常用于寒凝气滞诸痛及肾虚尿频。
evidence_notes.note_en: Moves qi to relieve pain and warms the Kidney to disperse cold; commonly used for cold-stagnation pain and Kidney-deficient urinary frequency.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 挥发油成分为其解痉及胃肠调节作用的主要活性来源。
evidence_notes.note_en: Its essential oil sesquiterpenes are considered the main active components for antispasmodic and GI-regulating effects.

description_en: The dried tuberous root of Lindera aggregata (Lauraceae). Enters the Lung, Spleen, Kidney, and Bladder channels. Pungent and warm, it moves qi, disperses cold, and alleviates pain while additionally warming the Kidney and consolidating urine. Its representative formulas include Tian Tai Wu Yao San for cold-qi hernia pain and Suo Quan Wan for Kidney-yang vacuity enuresis and urinary frequency.

actions.en: Warm the middle and stop vomiting
Direct counterflow downward and regulate the middle

indications.en: Chest and abdominal distention with oppressive pain
Cold Stomach with vomiting and hiccup
Kidney vacuity with qi counterflow wheezing
Vacuity-cold of the lower back and knees
Large Intestine vacuity constipation

contraindications_zh: 阴虚火旺、气虚下陷者慎用。孕妇慎用。
contraindications_en: Use with caution in yin vacuity with effulgent fire and qi vacuity with sinking. Use with caution during pregnancy.

dosage_range.notes: 煎服1–5g，宜后下；入丸散每次0.5–1g，多作冲服或磨汁服。因含挥发油，不宜久煎。

processing_methods.name: 沉香
processing_methods.effect: 行气止痛、纳气平喘、温中止呕

safety_notes_zh: 本品为瑞香科植物白木香（国产沉香）或沉香（进口）含树脂的木材，真伪优劣差异极大：国产白木香 Aquilaria sinensis 为药典所载正品，进口沉香多来自 A. agallocha 及 A. malaccensis，因野生资源稀少，市场伪品、次品繁多，入药须鉴别。阴虚火旺者不宜。
safety_notes_en: This herb is the resin-bearing wood of Thymelaeaceae Aquilaria sinensis (domestic chen xiang) or imported chen xiang; quality varies greatly: domestic Aquilaria sinensis is the pharmacopoeia-listed authentic product, while imported varieties mostly come from A. agallocha and A. malaccensis; due to scarce wild resources, counterfeit and inferior products are common on the market, and authentication is essential for medicinal use. Not suitable in yin vacuity with effulgent fire.

classical_references.passage: 治一切冷气，攻冲心腹刺痛，及虚寒喘急，用沉香、木香、乌药等分，磨汁温服，名四磨汤。

description_zh: 瑞香科植物白木香（国产沉香）含树脂的木材。归脾、胃、肾经。为沉降之品，行气而不伤正，温中而不燥烈，既能行气止痛，又能温肾纳气平喘，《太平惠民和剂局方》四磨汤、《济生方》沉香降气散为其代表应用。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 沉香含沉香螺旋醇等倍半萜、2-(2-苯乙基)色酮及挥发油，具有降气温中、镇静催眠、抗炎及促胃肠动力作用。
summary.en: Aquilariae Lignum Resinatum contains agarospirol and related sesquiterpenes, 2-(2-phenylethyl)chromones, and volatile oil, with qi-descending, sedative/hypnotic, anti-inflammatory, and prokinetic activity.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 胸腹气滞胀痛、呕吐呃逆
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Qi stagnation chest/abdominal distension, vomiting, and hiccup
Deficient wheeze from kidney failing to grasp qi

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中药学》（Bensky）指出沉香入煎剂应后下或研末冲服，以免有效挥发成分损失。
evidence_notes.note_en: Bensky notes that Chen Xiang should be added late or taken as a powder to preserve volatile constituents.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 陈氏药理学记载沉香提取物在动物实验中具有镇静、抗哮喘及抗抑郁活性。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen report sedative, antiasthmatic, and antidepressant activity of agarwood extracts in animal studies.

description_en: The resinous heartwood of Aquilaria sinensis (Thymelaeaceae). Enters the Spleen, Stomach, and Kidney channels. Pungent, bitter, and warm, it is a sinking and descending substance that moves qi without injuring the upright, warms the middle without drying harshness. It both moves qi to alleviate pain and warms the Kidney to grasp qi and calm wheezing. Featured in Si Mo Tang and Chen Xiang Jiang Qi San.

actions.en: Course the Liver and break up qi
Course the Liver and alleviate pain

indications.en: Liver constraint with qi stagnation, distending pain in the hypochondria
Distending pain or nodules of the breasts
Hernia pain
Food accumulation with qi stagnation, epigastric and abdominal distending pain
Abdominal masses and accumulations, lingering malaria with masses

contraindications_zh: 气虚者慎用。阴虚火旺、无气滞者不宜。汗多者不宜。
contraindications_en: Use with caution in qi vacuity. Not suitable in yin vacuity with effulgent fire or without qi stagnation. Not suitable in excessive sweating.

dosage_range.notes: 煎服3–10g。醋炙止痛力增强，多用于肝郁胁痛。

processing_methods.name: 生青皮
processing_methods.effect: 破气消积之力强

processing_methods.name: 醋青皮
processing_methods.effect: 引药入肝，增强疏肝止痛之功

safety_notes_zh: 本品性较峻烈，有破气耗气之虞，气虚及汗多者慎用。与陈皮同出一物，陈皮为成熟果皮，理气健脾缓和；青皮为幼果或未成熟果皮，破气散结峻烈，临证应区别使用。
safety_notes_en: This herb has a rather drastic nature with the risk of breaking and consuming qi; use with caution in qi vacuity and excessive sweating. Derived from the same plant as chen pi: chen pi is the mature peel with milder qi-regulating and spleen-strengthening effects, while qing pi is the immature or unripe peel with drastic qi-breaking and nodule-dispersing actions; the two should be used differently in clinical practice.

classical_references.passage: 青皮大能发汗，有汗及气虚人不可用。陈皮浮而升入脾、肺治高；青皮沉而降入肝、胆治低。

description_zh: 芸香科植物橘及其栽培变种的干燥幼果或未成熟果实的果皮。归肝、胆、胃经。较陈皮（成熟果皮）峻烈，陈皮偏升而理脾肺，青皮偏降而破肝胆，为疏肝破气、消积化滞之要药。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 青皮为未成熟橘的果皮，含辛弗林、橙皮苷及挥发油，现代研究显示促胃肠动力、利胆与升压作用，与传统疏肝破气、消积化滞之功相符。
summary.en: Unripe tangerine peel contains synephrine, hesperidin, and limonene-rich volatile oil, with reported prokinetic, choleretic, and pressor effects that parallel its traditional liver-soothing and qi-breaking use.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Liver qi stagnation with hypochondriac distention and pain

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中药学》（Bensky）强调青皮破气力强，气虚者慎用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Bensky cautions that Qing Pi strongly breaks qi and should be used carefully in qi-deficient patients.

evidence_notes.note_zh: Chen & Chen 记载辛弗林对心血管具轻度兴奋作用，使用含青皮制剂应关注高血压患者。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen note that synephrine has mild cardiovascular stimulant activity; hypertensive patients should be monitored when using Qing Pi preparations.

description_en: The dried immature fruit or unripe pericarp of Citrus reticulata and its cultivars (Rutaceae). Enters the Liver, Gallbladder, and Stomach channels. Pungent, bitter, and warm, it is more potent than chen pi (the mature peel): chen pi tends upward and regulates the Spleen and Lung, while qing pi tends downward and breaks through Liver and Gallbladder qi. It is the key herb for coursing the Liver, breaking up qi, and dispersing food accumulation.

actions.en: Reduce food stagnation and fortify the Stomach
Move qi and disperse blood stasis

indications.en: Meat and fatty-food stagnation with epigastric and abdominal fullness
Diarrhea or dysentery with abdominal pain; diarrhea from food stagnation

indications.zh: 瘀血经闭，产后瘀阻腹痛
indications.en: Amenorrhea and postpartum abdominal pain from blood stasis
Stabbing pain in the chest and abdomen; hernia pain
Hyperlipidemia; chest bi with heart pain

contraindications_zh: 脾胃虚弱而无积滞者、胃酸过多者慎服。孕妇慎用（其行气散瘀之力可能动胎）。不宜空腹及长期大量服用，以免损伤胃气、蚀齿。
contraindications_en: Use with caution in spleen and stomach vacuity without food stagnation and in excessive gastric acid. Use with caution during pregnancy (its qi-moving and stasis-dispersing action may disturb the fetus). Should not be taken on an empty stomach or in large amounts over prolonged periods to avoid damaging stomach qi and eroding tooth enamel.

dosage_range.notes: 消食导滞常用9–12g；活血化瘀、降脂可用至15–30g；小儿减量。生用活血化瘀，炒用消食化积，焦用止泻止痢。

processing_methods.name: 生山楂
processing_methods.effect: 长于活血化瘀，消食化浊降脂，多用于瘀血经闭、高脂血症、胸痹

processing_methods.name: 炒山楂
processing_methods.effect: 酸味减弱，长于消食化积，多用于肉食积滞、脘腹胀满

processing_methods.name: 焦山楂
processing_methods.effect: 长于消食止泻止痢，多用于食积泄泻、痢疾

processing_methods.name: 山楂炭
processing_methods.effect: 收涩止血止泻，多用于便血、久泻久痢

safety_notes_zh: 含枸橼酸、山楂酸等有机酸，空腹或过量服用可刺激胃黏膜，胃酸过多、胃溃疡者忌空腹服。孕妇慎用。未成熟果实含较多鞣酸与果胶，易与胃酸结合形成胃石。
safety_notes_en: Contains citric acid, crataegolic acid, and other organic acids; taking on an empty stomach or in repletion may irritate the gastric mucosa; contraindicated on an empty stomach in excessive gastric acid and peptic ulcer. Use with caution during pregnancy. Unripe fruit contains more tannins and pectin, which may combine with gastric acid to form gastric bezoars.

classical_references.passage: 《神农本草经》未载山楂专条，后世本草及《本草纲目》集解引前贤论其消肉食积滞、化瘀血之功。

description_zh: 蔷薇科植物山里红或山楂的干燥成熟果实。归脾、胃、肝经。为消食要药，尤善消肉食油腻之积，兼能活血化瘀，现代广泛用于高脂血症、冠心病。炮制不同功效有别：生用活血，炒用消食，焦用止泻。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 山楂（Crataegus）富含低聚原花青素及黄酮类化合物，是西方植物药中具 RCT 证据的心力衰竭辅助药物；同时富含有机酸与消化酶促进因子，对应中医消食化积之功。
summary.en: Hawthorn (Crataegus) is rich in oligomeric procyanidins and flavonoids (vitexin-rhamnoside, hyperoside) and is one of the few Western botanicals with RCT-level evidence as an adjunct in chronic heart failure; its organic acid and digestive-enzyme-promoting profile also supports the TCM claim of resolving food stagnation.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: NYHA II–III 级慢性心力衰竭辅助
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Adjunct for NYHA class II–III chronic heart failure
Food stagnation, especially from fatty/meat foods

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 高脂血症、动脉粥样硬化辅助

evidence_notes.note_zh: WHO 专著收录 Folium cum Flore Crataegi，支持其用于早期心功能不全（NYHA I–II）的辅助治疗。
evidence_notes.note_en: WHO monograph on Folium cum Flore Crataegi supports its use as an adjunct in early cardiac insufficiency (NYHA I–II).

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《草药禁忌》（Brinker）指出山楂可能增强地高辛及其他心血管药效应，理论上存在药物相互作用，应医护监护下使用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Brinker notes that hawthorn may potentiate digoxin and other cardiovascular medications; theoretical interactions warrant medical supervision.

evidence_notes.note_zh: Chen & Chen 综述焦山楂更擅消食止泻，生山楂偏活血化瘀降脂。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen note that charred Shan Zha is preferred for food stagnation/diarrhea while raw Shan Zha emphasizes blood-moving and lipid-lowering effects.

description_en: The dried ripe fruit of Crataegus pinnatifida or C. pinnatifida var. major (Rosaceae). Enters the Spleen, Stomach, and Liver channels. Sour, sweet, and slightly warm, it is the primary herb for food stagnation, excelling at digesting meat and greasy foods, while also invigorating blood and transforming stasis. Processing determines function: raw it invigorates blood, stir-fried it digests food, and charred it stops diarrhea.

actions.en: Reduce food stagnation and harmonize the middle
Suppress lactation and relieve breast distension
Course the Liver and resolve constraint
Fortify the Spleen and open the appetite

indications.zh: 米面薯芋食滞，脘腹胀满
indications.en: Food stagnation from grains, noodles, and starchy foods with epigastric fullness
Spleen vacuity with reduced intake and poor appetite

indications.zh: 妇女断乳，乳汁郁积，乳房胀痛
indications.en: Weaning, lactation stasis, and breast distention and pain
Liver depression with rib-side pain; Liver–Stomach disharmony
Pediatric milk stagnation and food accumulation

contraindications_zh: 哺乳期妇女不宜服用（大量服用可回乳）。无积滞、脾胃虚弱而非因食停者慎用。《药品化义》言其能消肾，久服损肾。
contraindications_en: Not suitable during lactation (large doses may suppress lactation). Use with caution when there is no food stagnation and in spleen-stomach vacuity not due to food retention. Yao Pin Hua Yi states it may consume kidney essence; prolonged use may damage the kidneys.

dosage_range.notes: 消食健胃常用10–15g；回乳或治疗乳汁郁积宜大剂量30–60g。生用偏于疏肝消胀，炒用偏于消食和中，焦麦芽偏于消食止泻。

processing_methods.name: 生麦芽
processing_methods.effect: 长于健脾和胃、疏肝行气，多用于肝郁胁痛、肝胃不和

processing_methods.name: 炒麦芽
processing_methods.effect: 性偏温，长于消食化滞、回乳消胀，多用于米面食积、断乳

processing_methods.name: 焦麦芽
processing_methods.effect: 长于消食止泻，多用于食积不化、脘腹胀痛、泄泻

safety_notes_zh: 哺乳期妇女服用大剂量（60g以上）可回乳，需授乳者禁用。孕妇慎用。含淀粉酶，高温久煎可降低其消食之效，宜后下或不宜久煎。
safety_notes_en: Large doses (over 60g) during lactation may suppress milk production; contraindicated when breastfeeding is needed. Use with caution during pregnancy. Contains amylase; prolonged decoction at high temperature may reduce its food-digesting efficacy; should be added near the end or not decocted for too long.

classical_references.passage: 《神农本草经》未载，后世《名医别录》《本草纲目·谷部》载麦蘖，言其消食和中、下气、开胃健脾。

description_zh: 禾本科植物大麦的成熟果实经发芽干燥而成。归脾、胃、肝经。为消米面食积之要药，生用疏肝，炒用消食回乳，临床常与山楂、神曲相须为用（焦三仙）。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 麦芽含淀粉酶、麦芽糖及大麦芽碱，具助消化、回乳及调节胃肠动力作用。
summary.en: Germinated barley contains amylases, maltose, and hordenine with digestive-enzyme, lactation-suppressing, and prokinetic activity.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 麦芽消食健胃，尤善消米面薯芋之积；生用回乳，炒用消食。
evidence_notes.note_en: Reduces food stagnation, especially from grains and tubers; raw for lactation suppression, dry-fried for digestion.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 生麦芽含活性淀粉酶，炒后酶活下降但消食作用保留；临床大剂量可降低泌乳素。
evidence_notes.note_en: Raw malt contains active amylase; frying reduces enzyme activity but preserves digestive action; high doses clinically lower prolactin.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》以性状及淀粉酶活力为质量控制指标。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chinese Pharmacopoeia uses macroscopic identification and amylase activity as quality controls.

description_en: The dried germinated fruit of Hordeum vulgare (Poaceae). Enters the Spleen, Stomach, and Liver channels. Sweet and neutral, it is the key herb for digesting starchy food accumulation. Used raw it courses the Liver and relieves constraint; stir-fried it digests food and suppresses lactation. Commonly paired with shan zha and shen qu as the 'Three Charred Immortals' (jiao san xian).

actions.en: Reduce food stagnation and dispel distension
Direct qi downward and transform phlegm
Move qi and loosen the middle

indications.zh: 饮食停滞，脘腹胀满疼痛
indications.en: Food stagnation with distention and pain in the epigastrium and abdomen
Belching and acid regurgitation, abdominal distention and constipation
Phlegm obstruction with counterflow qi, cough with copious phlegm
Wheezing and chest oppression; phlegm-panting in the elderly
Qi-stagnation diarrhea or dysentery with tenesmus

contraindications_zh: 气虚无食积、痰滞者慎用。不宜与人参同用，恐减人参补气之力（相恶）。脾胃虚弱而无积滞者忌服。
contraindications_en: Use with caution in qi vacuity without food stagnation or phlegm retention. Should not be used concurrently with ren shen, as it may reduce ren shen's qi-tonifying effect (mutual antagonism). Contraindicated in spleen-stomach vacuity without food stagnation.

dosage_range.notes: 消食除胀常用5–12g；降气化痰定喘可用至15g。生用长于涌吐风痰，炒用长于消食下气化痰，入汤剂宜打碎。

processing_methods.name: 生莱菔子
processing_methods.effect: 长于涌吐风痰，多用于痰涎壅盛、喉痹、食积气滞之重证

processing_methods.name: 炒莱菔子
processing_methods.effect: 药性缓和，长于消食除胀、降气化痰，多用于食积气滞、痰喘咳嗽

safety_notes_zh: 气虚者不宜单用或久用，以免耗气。不宜与人参同用。现代研究提示其有降压作用，低血压者慎服。种子需打碎入煎。
safety_notes_en: Not suitable for sole or prolonged use in qi vacuity, to avoid consuming qi. Should not be used concurrently with ren shen. Modern research suggests it has blood pressure-lowering effects; use with caution in hypotension. Seeds should be crushed before decoction.

classical_references.passage: 《本草纲目》载：莱菔子之功，长于利气。生能升，熟能降。升则吐风痰、散风寒、发疮疹；降则定痰喘咳嗽、调下痢后重、止内痛。

description_zh: 十字花科植物萝卜的干燥成熟种子。归脾、胃、肺经。为消食除胀、降气化痰之要药，代表方如保和丸、三子养亲汤，三子养亲汤中与紫苏子、白芥子同用治老年痰喘。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 莱菔子含芥子碱、脂肪油及莱菔素，具促进胃肠动力、祛痰、降压及抗菌作用。
summary.en: Raphanus seed contains sinapine, fixed oil, and raphanin with prokinetic, expectorant, antihypertensive, and antimicrobial effects.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Food stagnation with qi-stagnation abdominal distension
Copious phlegm with cough and wheeze

evidence_notes.note_zh: 莱菔子促进胃肠蠕动、镇咳祛痰；莱菔素对革兰阳性菌及真菌有抑制作用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Raphanus seed enhances GI motility and is antitussive/expectorant; raphanin inhibits Gram-positive bacteria and fungi.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 莱菔子消食除胀、降气化痰，与人参相畏，不宜同用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Reduces food accumulation and distension and descends qi to transform phlegm; traditionally antagonistic to ginseng.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》以芥子碱硫氰酸盐含量（不少于 0.40%）为质量指标。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chinese Pharmacopoeia specifies sinapine thiocyanate (≥0.40%) as the quality marker.

description_en: The dried ripe seed of Raphanus sativus (Brassicaceae). Enters the Spleen, Stomach, and Lung channels. Pungent, sweet, and neutral, it is the key herb for digesting food stagnation, relieving distention, directing qi downward, and transforming phlegm. Featured in Bao He Wan and San Zi Yang Qin Tang, where it is combined with zi su zi and bai jie zi for elderly patients with phlegm-dyspnea.

actions.en: Direct qi downward and free stagnation

indications.zh: 绦虫、蛔虫、蛲虫、姜片虫等多种肠道寄生虫病
indications.en: Tapeworm, roundworm, pinworm, fasciolopsis, and other intestinal parasitic infections
Food stagnation with qi stasis; dysentery with tenesmus
Epigastric and abdominal distention and pain with incomplete defecation
Edema and leg qi (beriberi)
Malarial chills and fever

contraindications_zh: 脾虚便溏、气虚下陷者忌服。孕妇慎用。不宜与人参、麦冬等补气养阴药同用时过量。古有“槟榔耗气”之说，长期大量服用有损正气。
contraindications_en: Contraindicated in spleen vacuity with loose stools and qi vacuity with sinking. Use with caution during pregnancy. Should not be used in repletion when combined with ren shen, mai dong, and other qi-tonifying and yin-nourishing herbs. Traditionally said to 'consume qi'; prolonged high-dose use may damage the zheng qi.

dosage_range.notes: 常规消积行气3–10g；驱绦虫、姜片虫需大剂量30–60g，清晨空腹煎服，配伍南瓜子。

processing_methods.name: 生槟榔
processing_methods.effect: 力猛，长于驱虫、消积、截疟，多用于绦虫、食积气滞重证

processing_methods.name: 炒槟榔
processing_methods.effect: 药性缓和，减其辛烈之性，多用于脾胃虚弱而需行气消积者

processing_methods.name: 焦槟榔
processing_methods.effect: 长于消食导滞，多用于食积不化、泻痢后重

safety_notes_zh: 含槟榔碱有拟副交感神经作用，过量可致流涎、呕吐、心悸、腹痛、瞳孔缩小。现代研究证实槟榔果有致癌性（口腔癌），但药用槟榔煎剂经过炮制及煎煮，其安全性与长期咀嚼生果不同，仍应限量使用、不宜长服。
safety_notes_en: Contains arecoline with parasympathomimetic effects; overdose may cause salivation, vomiting, palpitations, abdominal pain, and pupil constriction. Modern research confirms that betel nut fruit is carcinogenic (oral cancer), but medicinal bing lang decoction, after processing and decoction, differs in safety from long-term chewing of the raw fruit; use should still be limited and prolonged use avoided.

classical_references.passage: 《名医别录》载其主消谷逐水，除痰澼，杀三虫，去伏尸，治寸白。

description_zh: 棕榈科植物槟榔的干燥成熟种子。归胃、大肠经。为驱虫要药，尤擅驱绦虫，与南瓜子配伍力尤宏。同时行气利水、消积截疟，古方达原饮、木香槟榔丸皆以之为主药。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

active_constituents.notes: principal active and toxic constituent, a muscarinic agonist

summary.zh: 槟榔的主要活性成分为槟榔碱（一种毒蕈碱受体激动剂），具有驱虫（尤其绦虫及姜片虫）、增强胃肠蠕动及缩瞳作用；长期嚼食与口腔黏膜病变及口腔癌相关。
summary.en: Arecae Semen contains arecoline, a muscarinic agonist, with taenicidal (especially for tapeworm and Fasciolopsis), prokinetic, and miotic effects; long-term betel chewing is linked to oral submucous fibrosis and oral cancer.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Food stagnation with qi stagnation abdominal distension

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中药学》（Bensky）记载槟榔配伍南瓜子治疗绦虫病的方案在中国临床长期使用，驱虫率较高。
evidence_notes.note_en: Bensky documents the Bing Lang–pumpkin seed combination as a long-standing and effective Chinese clinical protocol for tapeworm expulsion.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 陈氏药理学提到槟榔碱过量可致流涎、恶心、心动过缓及呼吸困难，儿童、孕妇及消化道溃疡者慎用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen note that arecoline overdose can cause salivation, nausea, bradycardia, and dyspnea; contraindicated in children, pregnancy, and GI ulcer disease.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《草药禁忌》（Brinker）指出嚼食槟榔为 IARC 认定的人类一级致癌物，与口腔黏膜下纤维化及口腔癌相关。
evidence_notes.note_en: Brinker notes that betel-quid chewing is classified by IARC as a Group 1 human carcinogen, causally linked to oral submucous fibrosis and oral cancer.

description_en: The dried ripe seed of Areca catechu (Arecaceae). Enters the Stomach and Large Intestine channels. Bitter, pungent, and warm, it is the foremost antiparasitic herb, with particular efficacy against tapeworms when combined with nan gua zi (pumpkin seed). It also moves qi, promotes urination, disperses accumulation, and interrupts malarial attacks. Featured in Da Yuan Yin and Mu Xiang Bing Lang Wan.

actions.en: Fortify the Spleen and open the appetite

indications.en: Roundworm with abdominal pain, sallow complexion, and emaciation
Pinworm with anal itching
Pediatric gan accumulation with abdominal distention and poor appetite
Worm accumulation with abdominal pain, nighttime crying, and tooth grinding
Spleen vacuity with food stagnation, diarrhea or dysentery

contraindications_zh: 不宜与热茶同服，服用后饮茶易致呃逆、眩晕、呕吐、腹泻等反应。大量服用可致呃逆、呕吐、眩晕。脾胃虚寒甚者慎用。
contraindications_en: Should not be taken with hot tea; drinking tea after ingestion may easily cause hiccups, dizziness, vomiting, and diarrhea. Large doses may cause hiccups, vomiting, and dizziness. Use with caution in severe spleen-stomach vacuity cold.

dosage_range.notes: 成人一次用量9–12g，打碎入煎；炒香嚼服每次3–5g，一日3次。小儿按年龄每岁1.0–1.5粒，每日总量不超过20粒。

processing_methods.name: 生使君子
processing_methods.effect: 力专，但易致呃逆、恶心，多入汤剂打碎先煎

processing_methods.name: 炒使君子
processing_methods.effect: 香气增加，便于嚼服，减其副作用，多用于小儿驱虫及疳积

safety_notes_zh: 主要成分为使君子氨酸钾，过量服用（超过20粒/日）可致呃逆、眩晕、呕吐、腹泻，严重者呼吸困难。切忌与热茶同服，易引发不良反应。小儿慎用，按体重/年龄严格计量。
safety_notes_en: The main constituent is potassium quisqualate; overdose (more than 20 seeds/day) may cause hiccups, dizziness, vomiting, diarrhea, and dyspnea in severe cases. Strictly avoid taking with hot tea, as it may easily trigger adverse reactions. Use with caution in children; dosage must be strictly calculated based on body weight and age.

classical_references.passage: 《开宝本草》载：主小儿五疳，小便白浊，杀虫，疗泻痢。《太平惠民和剂局方》以之为君药制使君子丸治小儿诸疳诸虫。

description_zh: 使君子科植物使君子的干燥成熟果实。归脾、胃经。为驱蛔要药，尤宜小儿蛔虫病及疳积，味甘气香，小儿乐服，嚼服即效，故历代儿科方中多用之。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 使君子主含使君子氨酸及其钾盐，具有驱蛔虫、驱蛲虫作用。
summary.en: Quisqualis fruit contains quisqualic acid and its potassium salt, acting as an ascaricidal and anthelmintic agent.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Childhood nutritional impairment with abdominal distention

evidence_notes.note_zh: 杀虫消积，主治蛔虫腹痛及小儿疳积，过量或与热茶同服易致呃逆。
evidence_notes.note_en: Kills parasites and reduces food stagnation; overdose or combination with hot tea often causes hiccups.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 使君子氨酸为已知AMPA受体激动剂，是神经药理学重要工具化合物。
evidence_notes.note_en: Quisqualic acid is a well-characterized AMPA receptor agonist used as a neuropharmacology tool compound.

description_en: The dried ripe fruit of Quisqualis indica (Combretaceae). Enters the Spleen and Stomach channels. Sweet and warm, it is the principal herb for expelling roundworms, especially suitable for pediatric ascariasis and nutritional impairment (gan ji). Its sweet flavor and pleasant aroma make it well-accepted by children, and chewing the raw seeds is effective, which accounts for its frequent appearance in pediatric formulas across dynasties.

actions.en: Tonify blood and strengthen the Heart (when steamed)

indications.zh: 咯血、吐血、衄血、便血、崩漏
indications.en: Hemoptysis, hematemesis, epistaxis, hematochezia, and flooding and spotting
Traumatic bleeding and injuries from falls and blows
Chest bi and heart pain, including heart pain from blood stasis
Blood-stasis swelling and pain; painful sores and abscesses
Postpartum blood dizziness and retained lochia

contraindications_zh: 孕妇慎用。血虚无瘀者不宜。出血证属阴虚火旺者应配伍凉血药。不宜与抗凝血药（如华法林、阿司匹林）同用，有增加出血风险。
contraindications_en: Use with caution during pregnancy. Not suitable in blood vacuity without stasis. In bleeding due to yin vacuity with effulgent fire, blood-cooling herbs should be combined. Should not be used concurrently with anticoagulants (such as warfarin or aspirin) due to increased bleeding risk.

dosage_range.notes: 研末吞服或冲服，每次1–3g，每日2–3次。入汤剂3–9g；外用适量，研末外敷。为粉末入药效佳，所谓“生打熟补”。

processing_methods.name: 生三七
processing_methods.effect: 长于散瘀止血、消肿定痛，多用于出血证、跌打损伤、瘀血证

processing_methods.name: 熟三七
processing_methods.effect: 长于补血强身，多用于气血虚弱、病后体虚、产后调理

safety_notes_zh: 过量或长期服用可引起口干、烦躁、恶心、出血倾向增加。三七含人参皂苷，与抗凝药合用需谨慎。孕妇慎用，手术前一周宜停服。
safety_notes_en: Excessive or prolonged use may cause dry mouth, irritability, nausea, and increased bleeding tendency. San qi contains ginsenosides; caution is needed when combining with anticoagulants. Use with caution during pregnancy; discontinue one week before surgery.

classical_references.passage: 《本草纲目》载：此药近时始出，南人军中用为金疮要药，云有奇功……凡杖扑伤损，瘀血淋漓者，随即嚼烂罨之即止；青肿者即消散。

description_zh: 五加科植物三七的干燥根及根茎。归肝、胃经。为止血化瘀、消肿止痛之要药，止血而不留瘀，化瘀而不伤正，有“止血之神品”“金不换”之誉。生打熟补：生用散瘀止血，熟用补血强身。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 三七（Panax notoginseng）含人参皂苷 Rg1/Rb1、三七皂苷 R1 及田七氨酸，兼具止血与化瘀的「双向」药理：生品富含田七氨酸缩短出血时间，同时总皂苷具抗血小板和扩张冠脉作用，对应「止血不留瘀」之传统论述。
summary.en: Panax notoginseng contains ginsenosides Rg1/Rb1, notoginsenoside R1, and dencichine. It displays a dual hemostatic–antiplatelet pharmacology: raw powder rich in dencichine shortens bleeding time while total saponins are antiplatelet and coronary-vasodilatory, paralleling the classical claim of 'stopping bleeding without causing stasis'.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 内外出血、创伤出血（生粉冲服）
冠心病心绞痛（三七总皂苷制剂）
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Coronary heart disease and angina (total notoginseng saponins preparations)

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 缺血性脑卒中辅助（注射用血塞通/血栓通）

evidence_notes.note_zh: 中国药典 2020 版收载三七，规定人参皂苷 Rb1、Rg1 及三七皂苷 R1 的总含量不低于 5.0%。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chinese Pharmacopoeia 2020 specifies that Sanqi must contain no less than 5.0% total ginsenoside Rb1 + Rg1 + notoginsenoside R1.

evidence_notes.note_zh: Brinker 提示三七可增强抗血小板与抗凝药效应，与阿司匹林、华法林合用应监测出血风险。
evidence_notes.note_en: Brinker notes that notoginseng may potentiate antiplatelet and anticoagulant drugs; bleeding risk should be monitored when combined with aspirin or warfarin.

evidence_notes.note_zh: Chen & Chen 综述田七氨酸促进血小板释放并缩短出血时间，而三七总皂苷抑制血小板聚集，从而实现双向调节。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen summarize that dencichine promotes platelet release and shortens bleeding time whereas total notoginseng saponins inhibit platelet aggregation, accounting for the bidirectional profile.

description_en: The dried root and rhizome of Panax notoginseng (Araliaceae). Enters the Liver and Stomach channels. Sweet, slightly bitter, and warm, it is the premier herb for simultaneously stopping bleeding and transforming stasis while reducing swelling and alleviating pain. It stops bleeding without leaving stasis and transforms stasis without injuring the upright, earning the name 'divine hemostatic' and 'gold-exchange herb.' Raw it disperses stasis; steamed it tonifies blood.

indications.zh: 吐血、衄血、咯血、便血、崩漏
indications.en: Hematemesis, epistaxis, hemoptysis, hematochezia, and flooding and spotting
Traumatic bleeding

indications.zh: 瘀血经闭，痛经，产后瘀痛
indications.en: Amenorrhea, dysmenorrhea, and postpartum pain from blood stasis
Blood-stasis pain of the chest and abdomen; chest bi and heart pain
Blood strangury with painful, inhibited urination

contraindications_zh: 孕妇忌服（其活血之力易动胎）。无瘀血者及阴虚血热者不宜。入汤剂宜包煎，以免刺激咽喉、混汤。
contraindications_en: Contraindicated during pregnancy (its blood-activating action may easily disturb the fetus). Not suitable when there is no blood stasis and in yin vacuity with blood heat. In decoctions, should be wrap-decocted (bao jian) to avoid irritating the throat and clouding the broth.

dosage_range.notes: 内服5–10g，包煎；外用适量，研末外敷。生用活血化瘀、利尿通淋；炒用收敛止血；炒炭偏于止血。

processing_methods.name: 生蒲黄
processing_methods.effect: 长于活血化瘀、利尿通淋，多用于瘀血痛证、血淋涩痛

processing_methods.name: 炒蒲黄
processing_methods.effect: 长于收敛止血而兼行血，多用于各种出血证而有瘀者

processing_methods.name: 蒲黄炭
processing_methods.effect: 性涩，专于止血，用于血热妄行之诸出血证

safety_notes_zh: 孕妇禁用。入煎剂宜用纱布包煎。部分人对蒲黄花粉可能过敏。与活血化瘀或抗凝药合用时应注意出血风险。
safety_notes_en: Contraindicated during pregnancy. In decoctions, should be wrapped in gauze for decoction. Some individuals may be allergic to pu huang pollen. When combining with blood-activating, stasis-resolving herbs or anticoagulants, be aware of bleeding risk.

classical_references.passage: 味甘，平。主心腹膀胱寒热，利小便，止血，消瘀血。久服轻身益气力，延年神仙。

description_zh: 香蒲科植物水烛香蒲、东方香蒲或同属植物的干燥花粉。归肝、心包经。止血化瘀两擅其长，与五灵脂相配为失笑散，治瘀血诸痛之要方。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 蒲黄为香蒲花粉，主含黄酮类及甾醇脂质，现代研究显示其有缩短凝血时间、抗血小板（炒炭生用双向）与降血脂作用，与传统化瘀止血之功相合。
summary.en: Typha pollen contains flavonoid glycosides (typhaneoside) and lipidic sterols; preclinical studies report hemostatic effects when charred and antiplatelet/lipid-lowering effects when raw, reflecting its dual traditional role of stopping bleeding and resolving blood stasis.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 瘀血痛经、产后瘀阻（生用）
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Blood-stasis dysmenorrhea and postpartum stasis (raw form)

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中药学》（Bensky）记载蒲黄生用活血、炒炭止血，孕妇禁用；入煎宜包煎。
evidence_notes.note_en: Bensky notes raw Typha invigorates blood while charred Typha stops bleeding; contraindicated in pregnancy and typically wrapped for decoction.

evidence_notes.note_zh: Chen & Chen 综述蒲黄黄酮具抗血栓与降血脂活性。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen summarize antithrombotic and lipid-lowering activities of Typha flavonoids.

description_en: The dried pollen of Typha angustifolia, T. orientalis, or related species (Typhaceae). Enters the Liver and Pericardium channels. Sweet and neutral, it excels at both stopping bleeding and transforming stasis. Paired with wu ling zhi in Shi Xiao San, a key formula for stasis pain syndromes.

actions.en: Warm the channels and stop bleeding
Dispel dampness and relieve itching (external use)

indications.en: Vacuity-cold bleeding, including flooding and spotting
Irregular menstruation and dysmenorrhea from cold in the menses
Infertility from cold uterus with cold pain in the lower abdomen
Restless fetus and bleeding during pregnancy

indications.zh: 湿疹瘙痒，疥癣（外用煎汤熏洗）
indications.en: Damp eczema with itching, scabies, and ringworm (used externally as a fumigating wash)

contraindications_zh: 阴虚血热者慎用。孕妇慎用，但胎动不安、妊娠下血属虚寒者为正治（如胶艾汤）。外敷可引起接触性皮炎。
contraindications_en: Use with caution in yin vacuity with blood heat. Use with caution during pregnancy, but in restless fetus and pregnancy bleeding due to vacuity cold, it is the correct treatment (e.g., Jiao Ai Tang). Topical application may cause contact dermatitis.

dosage_range.notes: 内服3–9g；外用适量，供灸治或熏洗用。生用温经散寒，炒炭止血，醋艾炭温经止血之力增强。

processing_methods.name: 生艾叶
processing_methods.effect: 长于温经散寒、调经安胎，多用于虚寒痛经、胎动不安、宫冷不孕

processing_methods.name: 艾叶炭
processing_methods.effect: 辛散之性减，专于温经止血，多用于虚寒性崩漏下血、吐衄便血

processing_methods.name: 醋艾炭
processing_methods.effect: 温经止血之力益增，多用于崩漏

safety_notes_zh: 内服过量或长期服用可引起肝损伤、神经毒性（含侧柏酮）。外用可能引起接触性皮炎。艾灸烟雾对呼吸道敏感者刺激，室内灸治需通风。阴虚火旺者禁服。
safety_notes_en: Excessive internal use or prolonged consumption may cause liver injury and neurotoxicity (contains thujone). External use may cause contact dermatitis. Moxa smoke irritates those with respiratory sensitivity; indoor moxibustion requires ventilation. Contraindicated in yin vacuity with effulgent fire.

classical_references.passage: 《金匮要略》胶艾汤：阿胶、艾叶、当归、芎䓖、芍药、甘草、干地黄，治妇人有漏下者，有半产后因续下血都不绝者，有妊娠下血者，假令妊娠腹中痛，为胞阻。

description_zh: 菊科植物艾的干燥叶。归肝、脾、肾经。为温经止血、安胎要药，又为艾灸之主材（陈艾）。生用散寒调经安胎，炒炭温经止血，与阿胶同用之胶艾汤为治妊娠下血之经典方。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 艾叶含挥发油（桉油精、侧柏酮、龙脑）、黄酮类及鞣质，具有温经止血、抗菌、抗炎及平喘作用；外用燃烧（艾灸）则以热力及挥发性成分发挥温通经络之效。
summary.en: Artemisiae argyi Folium contains essential oils (cineole, thujone, borneol), flavonoids, and tannins, with hemostatic, antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and mild bronchodilatory effects; moxibustion relies on combined heat and volatile components.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 慢性支气管炎（配伍使用）
艾灸用于寒凝经络性痛证

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中药学》（Bensky）指出艾叶水煎剂对革兰氏阳性菌及部分真菌有抑制作用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Bensky notes inhibitory action of Artemisia argyi decoctions against Gram-positive bacteria and some fungi.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 陈氏药理学提到艾叶挥发油中的侧柏酮含量较高时对中枢神经系统有一定神经毒性，提示大剂量或长期内服需谨慎。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen note that thujone in the essential oil may exert neurotoxic effects at high doses, cautioning against prolonged high-dose internal use.

evidence_notes.note_zh: Brinker 记载妊娠期应避免大剂量内服艾叶，因动物实验中显示子宫兴奋作用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Brinker advises against high-dose internal use during pregnancy due to uterine-stimulant activity in animal models.

description_en: The dried leaf of Artemisia argyi (Asteraceae). Enters the Liver, Spleen, and Kidney channels. Pungent, bitter, and warm, it is the key herb for warming the channels to stop bleeding and calming the fetus, and is the primary material for moxibustion (aged mugwort is preferred). Used raw it disperses cold and regulates menstruation; charred it warms the channels and stops bleeding. Combined with e jiao in Jiao Ai Tang for pregnancy bleeding.

actions.en: Tonify the Lung and heal sores

indications.en: Hemoptysis and hematemesis, including hemoptysis from pulmonary consumption
Traumatic bleeding
Gastric ulcer bleeding
Sores and toxic swellings, cracked and fissured skin

indications.zh: 烫火灼伤，溃疡久不收口
indications.en: Scalds, burns, and chronic non-healing ulcers

contraindications_zh: 不宜与乌头类药物（川乌、草乌、附子）同用（十八反）。外感咳血、肺痈初起脓未成及实热壅肺者不宜。
contraindications_en: Should not be used concurrently with aconite-type herbs (chuan wu, cao wu, fu zi) per the Eighteen Antagonisms (shiba fan). Not suitable in coughing blood from external pathogens, early-stage lung abscess before pus formation, and repletion heat congesting the lung.

dosage_range.notes: 内服6–15g；研末吞服每次3–6g。外用适量，研末涂敷。因其含黏液质，入汤剂易糊，宜研粉冲服或另煎。

processing_methods.name: 生白及
processing_methods.effect: 长于收敛止血、消肿生肌，为主要炮制品

safety_notes_zh: 含大量黏液质，可形成保护膜，护肺胃黏膜。不宜与乌头类药物同用（十八反）。实证、热证出血初起慎用。现代用于肺结核、胃溃疡出血及外科创口愈合。
safety_notes_en: Contains large amounts of mucilage that can form a protective film over the lung and stomach mucosa. Should not be used concurrently with aconite-type herbs per the Eighteen Antagonisms (shiba fan). Use with caution in early-stage bleeding from repletion or heat patterns. In modern use, applied for pulmonary tuberculosis, gastric ulcer bleeding, and surgical wound healing.

classical_references.passage: 味苦，平。主痈肿恶疮败疽，伤阴死肌，胃中邪气，贼风鬼击，痱缓不收。

description_zh: 兰科植物白及的干燥块茎。归肺、肝、胃经。为收敛止血、生肌敛疮之要药，尤擅治肺胃出血，与三七同用治咯血，现代广泛用于消化性溃疡出血、肺结核空洞咯血及外科止血。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

active_constituents.notes: mucilaginous fraction responsible for hemostatic and wound-healing effect

summary.zh: 白及的主要活性成分为白及多糖（甘露聚糖）及联苄、菲类化合物，具有止血、促进肉芽组织生长、抗菌及局部黏膜保护作用。
summary.en: Bletillae Rhizoma contains bletilla glucomannan, bibenzyl and phenanthrene derivatives, with hemostatic, granulation-promoting, antimicrobial, and mucosal-protective activity.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 上消化道出血（内服胶浆）
外伤出血及烧烫伤创面
肺结核空洞咯血辅助治疗
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Adjunct for hemoptysis in cavitary pulmonary tuberculosis

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中药学》（Bensky）记载白及曾在中国临床用于支气管扩张咯血及消化性溃疡出血，部分小样本研究显示良好止血效果。
evidence_notes.note_en: Bensky documents use in China for bronchiectasis-related hemoptysis and peptic ulcer bleeding, with small clinical series reporting effective hemostasis.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 陈氏药理学提到白及不可与乌头类同用（十八反），并注意其黏稠性可能影响其他药物吸收。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen note the 'eighteen incompatibilities' proscription against combining with Aconitum species and caution that its mucilage may affect absorption of co-administered drugs.

description_en: The dried tuber of Bletilla striata (Orchidaceae). Enters the Lung, Liver, and Stomach channels. Bitter, sweet, astringent, and cool, it is the key herb for astringent hemostasis and wound healing, with particular efficacy for Lung and Stomach hemorrhage. Combined with san qi for hemoptysis, it is extensively used in modern practice for peptic ulcer bleeding, pulmonary tuberculosis cavitary hemoptysis, and surgical hemostasis.

actions.en: Tonify vacuity and strengthen the body

indications.zh: 咯血、吐血、衄血、崩漏下血
indications.en: Hemoptysis, hematemesis, epistaxis, and flooding and spotting
Hematochezia and hematuria
Chronic diarrhea and red-and-white dysentery
Malarial chills and fever

indications.zh: 脱力劳伤，神疲乏力，血小板减少
indications.en: Taxation fatigue with spirit exhaustion and weakness; also used for thrombocytopenia

contraindications_zh: 外感初起、邪实积滞者慎用。收涩之性，不宜用于实证出血兼有瘀滞者（需配伍化瘀药）。
contraindications_en: Use with caution in early-stage external contraction and repletion pathogen accumulation. Due to its astringent nature, not suitable in repletion-pattern bleeding with stasis (stasis-resolving herbs should be combined).

dosage_range.notes: 一般内服6–12g；大剂量可用30–60g，尤治脱力劳伤、血小板减少、顽固性出血。外用适量，捣敷或煎汤熏洗。

processing_methods.name: 生仙鹤草
processing_methods.effect: 长于收敛止血、截疟止痢、解毒，多用于各种出血证、疟疾、痢疾

processing_methods.name: 仙鹤草炭
processing_methods.effect: 收涩止血之力更强，多用于各类慢性出血

safety_notes_zh: 药性平和，毒性小，可较大剂量长期服用。高剂量（30–60g）治血小板减少性紫癜、再生障碍性贫血、脱力劳伤有效。单味煎汤亦可作补益。
safety_notes_en: Mild in nature with low toxicity; can be used in larger doses for extended periods. High doses (30-60g) are effective for thrombocytopenic purpura, aplastic anemia, and fatigue-exhaustion injuries. A single-herb decoction can also serve as a tonic.

classical_references.passage: 《滇南本草》载：治妇人月潮，赤白带下，面寒腹痛，日久赤白血痢。《伪药条辨》言其止血、强心、补虚。

description_zh: 蔷薇科植物龙芽草的干燥地上部分。归心、肝经。为广谱止血要药，兼能补虚、截疟、止痢，民间有“脱力草”之称，大剂量治疗脱力劳伤、血小板减少有良效。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 仙鹤草主含鞣质类（仙鹤草素）及间苯三酚类（仙鹤草酚），具有止血、抗菌、驱绦虫及抗肿瘤研究活性。
summary.en: Agrimonia herb contains tannins (agrimoniin) and phloroglucinols (agrimophol) with hemostatic, antimicrobial, taeniacidal and investigational antitumor activity.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 各种出血（咯血、吐血、便血、崩漏）
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Various hemorrhages (hemoptysis, hematemesis, hematochezia, uterine bleeding)

evidence_notes.note_zh: 收敛止血、补虚、消积、止痢、杀虫，通治各种出血不论寒热。
evidence_notes.note_en: Astringes bleeding, tonifies vacuity, reduces accumulation, stops dysentery and kills parasites; used for bleeding of either cold or hot nature.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 仙鹤草酚在中国曾作为驱绦虫药研究及使用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Agrimophol has been studied and historically used in China as a taeniacidal agent.

description_en: The dried aerial part of Agrimonia pilosa (Rosaceae). Enters the Heart and Liver channels. Bitter, astringent, and neutral, it is a broad-spectrum hemostatic herb that additionally tonifies vacuity, interrupts malaria, and stops dysentery. Known colloquially as 'fatigue-relieving herb' (tuo li cao), it is effective in large doses for exhaustion and fatigue conditions and for thrombocytopenia.

actions.en: Promote hair growth and darken hair

indications.zh: 血热妄行之吐血、衄血、咯血、便血、崩漏
indications.en: Reckless bleeding from heat in the blood — hematemesis, epistaxis, hemoptysis, hematochezia, and flooding and spotting
Lung-heat cough with blood-streaked sputum
Premature graying and hair loss from blood heat
Damp-heat vaginal discharge
Scalds, abscesses, and sores (topical use)

contraindications_zh: 虚寒性出血者慎用。脾胃虚寒者不宜久服。外用引起接触性皮炎者停用。
contraindications_en: Use with caution in vacuity-cold type bleeding. Not suitable for prolonged use in spleen-stomach vacuity cold. Discontinue if external use causes contact dermatitis.

dosage_range.notes: 内服6–12g；外用适量，煎水洗或捣敷。止血多炒炭用，其他用途多生用。

processing_methods.name: 生侧柏叶
processing_methods.effect: 长于清热凉血、化痰止咳、生发乌发，多用于肺热咳嗽、须发早白

processing_methods.name: 侧柏炭
processing_methods.effect: 寒凉之性减，涩性增，专于收敛止血，多用于血热出血证

safety_notes_zh: 含侧柏酮等挥发油，过量可致胃肠不适。长期大剂量可致肝损害。外用生发可配桑叶、何首乌浸酒外涂。
safety_notes_en: Contains thujone and other volatile oils; overdose may cause gastrointestinal discomfort. Prolonged high-dose use may cause liver injury. For external use to promote hair growth, can be combined with sang ye and he shou wu soaked in wine for topical application.

classical_references.passage: 柏实，味甘，平。主惊悸，安五脏，益气，除风湿痹。久服令人润泽美色，耳目聪明，不饥不老，轻身延年。

description_zh: 柏科植物侧柏的干燥枝梢及叶。归肺、肝、脾经。为凉血止血之要药，尤宜血热妄行之出血证，代表方四生丸（生侧柏叶、生荷叶、生艾叶、生地黄）。外用浸酒可生发乌发。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 侧柏叶含挥发油、黄酮类（穗花杉双黄酮、槲皮苷）及鞣质，具有止血、镇咳平喘、抗菌及促毛发生长作用。
summary.en: Platycladi Cacumen contains volatile oils, flavonoids (amentoflavone, quercitrin), and tannins, with hemostatic, antitussive, antimicrobial, and hair-growth-promoting effects.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 血热妄行之咯血、吐血、衄血
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Bleeding from blood heat (hemoptysis, hematemesis, epistaxis)

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中药学》（Bensky）指出侧柏叶炒炭后止血作用增强，传统上用于各种出血证。
evidence_notes.note_en: Bensky notes that charring (Ce Bai Ye Tan) enhances the hemostatic effect and the herb is traditionally used for various bleeding patterns.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 陈氏药理学提到侧柏叶提取物在动物实验中可缩短凝血时间并延长毛发生长期。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen report that Platycladus extracts shorten clotting time in animals and extend the hair anagen phase in rodent models.

description_en: The dried branch tip and leaf of Platycladus orientalis (Cupressaceae). Enters the Lung, Liver, and Spleen channels. Bitter, astringent, and cold, it is the key herb for cooling blood and stopping bleeding, especially for blood-heat reckless movement. Featured in Si Sheng Wan with fresh he ye, ai ye, and sheng di huang. Applied externally steeped in wine, it promotes hair growth and darkens hair.

indications.en: Hematochezia, hemorrhoidal bleeding, and bloody dysentery
Flooding and spotting with profuse menstruation
Heat-in-the-blood hemorrhage and hematuria
Scalds and burns (topical use)
Sores, toxic swellings, and eczema

contraindications_zh: 虚寒性出血、冷痢及下焦虚寒者慎用。大面积烫伤外用时应慎重（含鞣质大量吸收可致肝损害）。
contraindications_en: Use with caution in vacuity-cold type bleeding, cold dysentery, and lower jiao vacuity cold. Caution with external application on large burn areas (absorption of large amounts of tannins may cause liver injury).

dosage_range.notes: 内服9–15g，大剂量可用30g；外用适量，研末涂敷。止血多炒炭用，解毒敛疮多生用。

processing_methods.name: 生地榆
processing_methods.effect: 长于清热凉血解毒、敛疮，多用于烫伤、湿疹、疮疡及便血初起

processing_methods.name: 地榆炭
processing_methods.effect: 增强收敛止血之力，多用于下焦血热出血证，如便血、崩漏、痔血

safety_notes_zh: 含大量鞣质（约17%），大面积烧伤外用可经创面吸收致中毒性肝炎。虚寒血证忌用。不宜与含铁剂及生物碱类药物同服。
safety_notes_en: Contains large amounts of tannins (approximately 17%); external use on extensive burns may lead to absorption through wounds causing toxic hepatitis. Contraindicated in vacuity-cold type bleeding. Should not be taken concurrently with iron preparations or alkaloid-containing medications.

classical_references.passage: 味苦，微寒。主妇人乳痓痛，七伤，带下病，止痛，除恶肉，止汗，疗金疮。

description_zh: 蔷薇科植物地榆或长叶地榆的干燥根。归肝、大肠经。为凉血止血之要药，尤擅治下焦血热出血（便血、痔血、崩漏），兼能解毒敛疮，外用治烫伤有卓效（地榆膏）。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 地榆富含鞣质（地榆鞣质、没食子酸）与三萜皂苷，药理研究示其收敛止血、抗菌、抗炎及促进创面愈合作用。
summary.en: Diyu is rich in tannins (sanguiin H-6, gallic acid) and triterpene saponins; pharmacology shows astringent hemostatic, antibacterial, anti-inflammatory and wound-healing effects.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Topical treatment of burns and scalds

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》以鞣质含量作为质量评价指标之一；炒炭后鞣质含量变化，止血作用增强。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chinese Pharmacopoeia uses tannin content as a quality marker; charring modifies tannin content and enhances hemostatic action.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 炒炭用于下焦出血与烧伤外敷为经典应用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Char-fried preparation is classical for lower-burner bleeding and topical burn care.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 大面积烧伤外用时须防止鞣质过度吸收致肝损，已有个案报道。
evidence_notes.note_en: Case reports warn against excessive tannin absorption with hepatotoxicity when applied to large burn surfaces.

description_en: The dried root of Sanguisorba officinalis or S. officinalis var. longifolia (Rosaceae). Enters the Liver and Large Intestine channels. Bitter, sour, astringent, and cold, it is the key herb for cooling blood and stopping bleeding, with particular efficacy for lower-burner blood-heat hemorrhage including bloody stool, hemorrhoidal bleeding, and flooding and spotting. It also resolves toxicity and heals sores, with notable external efficacy for burns (Di Yu Gao).

actions.en: Dispel stasis and generate new blood

indications.en: Irregular menstruation, dysmenorrhea, and amenorrhea

indications.zh: 产后恶露不尽，瘀滞腹痛
indications.en: Retained lochia and postpartum abdominal pain from blood stasis
Edema with scanty urination
Edema due to acute nephritis
Traumatic injury with stasis, swelling, and pain
Sores, carbuncles, and skin rashes from toxic heat

contraindications_zh: 孕妇禁用。血虚无瘀者及阴虚血少者忌用。
contraindications_en: Contraindicated during pregnancy. Contraindicated in blood vacuity without stasis and yin vacuity with scanty blood.

dosage_range.notes: 煎服常用9–30g；鲜品可用至12–40g；外用适量捣敷或煎汤外洗。大剂量(30–60g)可用于顽固性水肿。

processing_methods.name: 生益母草
processing_methods.effect: 活血调经、利水消肿、清热解毒之力较强

processing_methods.name: 益母草膏
processing_methods.effect: 便于妇科长期服用，调经止痛作用持久

safety_notes_zh: 孕妇禁用，有明确兴奋子宫、促进宫缩作用。大剂量或久服可致肾损害，不宜长期超量服用。现代药理显示含益母草碱，对肾脏有一定毒性。
safety_notes_en: Contraindicated during pregnancy; has definite uterine-stimulating and contraction-promoting effects. High doses or prolonged use may cause kidney damage; prolonged excessive use should be avoided. Modern pharmacology shows it contains leonurine, which has some nephrotoxicity.

classical_references.passage: 味辛，微温。主瘾疹痒，可作浴汤。一名益母，一名益明，一名大札。生池泽。

description_zh: 唇形科植物益母草的干燥地上部分。归肝、心包、膀胱经。为妇科经产要药，善治经产诸疾，故名益母。既能活血祛瘀以调经止痛，又能利水消肿，瘀水同治。常与当归、川芎、赤芍等相配。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 益母草主含益母草碱和水苏碱，具有兴奋子宫、改善微循环、利尿、保护心肌等作用，为活血调经、利水消肿的药理依据。
summary.en: Leonurus contains leonurine and stachydrine, producing uterotonic, microcirculation-improving, diuretic and cardioprotective effects that underlie its blood-invigorating, menstruation-regulating and edema-reducing actions.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 产后子宫复旧不全、恶露不净
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Postpartum uterine involution failure and retained lochia

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》以盐酸水苏碱为含量测定指标，益母草注射液用于产后出血。
evidence_notes.note_en: The Chinese Pharmacopoeia uses stachydrine hydrochloride as an assay marker; Yi Mu Cao injection is used for postpartum hemorrhage.

evidence_notes.note_zh: Chen & Chen 引述益母草碱收缩子宫作用明确，孕妇禁用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen note leonurine's clear uterotonic activity; contraindicated in pregnancy.

description_en: The dried aerial part of Leonurus japonicus (Lamiaceae). Enters the Liver, Pericardium, and Bladder channels. Bitter, pungent, and cool, it is the essential gynecological herb for menstrual and postpartum disorders, hence its name 'benefit-the-mother herb.' It invigorates blood and dispels stasis to regulate menstruation and also promotes urination to reduce edema, treating stasis and water simultaneously.

actions.en: Invigorate blood and unblock the channels
Tonify Liver and Kidney, strengthen sinews and bones
Promote urination and unblock painful urinary dribbling

indications.zh: 瘀血阻滞之经闭、痛经、产后腹痛
indications.en: Amenorrhea, dysmenorrhea, and postpartum abdominal pain from blood stasis

indications.zh: 肝肾不足之腰膝酸痛，筋骨无力
indications.en: Sore lumbus and weak knees from Liver and Kidney vacuity
Lin syndrome, edema, and dysuria
Headache and dizziness from ascendant Liver yang
Hematemesis and epistaxis from heat in the blood
Toothache and mouth sores from Stomach fire

contraindications_zh: 孕妇禁用。月经过多者及梦遗滑精者忌用。中气下陷者慎用（其性下行）。
contraindications_en: Contraindicated during pregnancy. Contraindicated in excessive menstruation and nocturnal emission with seminal efflux. Use with caution in middle qi sinking (its nature is descending).

dosage_range.notes: 煎服常用5–12g；引血下行多用15g。怀牛膝偏补肝肾、强筋骨；川牛膝偏活血通经、祛风利湿。

processing_methods.name: 生牛膝
processing_methods.effect: 活血祛瘀，引血下行，利水通淋

processing_methods.name: 酒牛膝
processing_methods.effect: 增强活血通经、补肝肾、强筋骨之力，多用于风湿痹痛、腰膝酸软

processing_methods.name: 盐牛膝
processing_methods.effect: 引药入肾，增强补肝肾、强筋骨之功，多用于肝肾亏虚

safety_notes_zh: 孕妇禁用，有兴奋子宫作用。怀牛膝（河南焦作产）补益力胜，川牛膝（四川产）活血力强，临床应分别使用。其性下行，不宜用于中气下陷、阴虚火旺而无瘀滞者。
safety_notes_en: Contraindicated during pregnancy; has uterine-stimulating effects. Huai niu xi (from Jiaozuo, Henan) is superior in tonifying, while chuan niu xi (from Sichuan) is stronger in activating blood; the two should be used differently in clinical practice. Its nature is descending; not suitable in middle qi sinking and yin vacuity with effulgent fire without stasis.

classical_references.passage: 味苦，酸。主寒湿痿痹，四肢拘挛，膝痛不可屈伸，逐血气，伤热火烂，堕胎。

description_zh: 苋科植物牛膝的干燥根。归肝、肾经。有怀牛膝与川牛膝之分：怀牛膝长于补肝肾、强筋骨；川牛膝长于活血通经、祛风利湿。本品性善下行，能引血、引火、引药下行，为治下焦瘀血及肝阳上亢之要药。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 怀牛膝与川牛膝含蜕皮甾酮、三萜皂苷与多糖，现代研究显示其具有抗炎、免疫调节、促骨形成及改善微循环作用，对应传统活血、补肝肾、强筋骨之功。
summary.en: Achyranthes/Cyathula root contains phytoecdysteroids, triterpenoid saponins, and polysaccharides, with preclinical data on anti-inflammatory, immunomodulatory, osteogenic, and microcirculation-enhancing effects that parallel its traditional blood-moving and liver-kidney tonifying use.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 腰膝酸软、骨关节退行性病变
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Lumbar and knee weakness, degenerative joint conditions

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 引血下行（高血压头痛辅助）
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Directing blood downward (adjunct for hypertension-related headache)

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中药学》（Bensky）强调怀牛膝长于补益，川牛膝偏于活血，孕妇禁用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Bensky distinguishes Huai-niu-xi (tonifying emphasis) from Chuan-niu-xi (blood-moving emphasis) and contraindicates both in pregnancy.

evidence_notes.note_zh: Chen & Chen 记载蜕皮甾酮在动物模型中显示促蛋白合成与保护软骨细胞作用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen cite ecdysterone's anabolic and chondroprotective effects in animal models.

description_en: The dried root of Achyranthes bidentata (Amaranthaceae). Enters the Liver and Kidney channels. Bitter, sweet, sour, and neutral. Two varieties are distinguished: huai niu xi (from Henan) excels at supplementing the Liver and Kidney and strengthening sinews and bones, while chuan niu xi excels at invigorating blood and dispelling wind-dampness. Its nature is strongly descending, capable of directing blood, fire, and other medicinals downward.

actions.en: Clear the Heart and cool blood
Benefit the Gallbladder and resolve jaundice

indications.zh: 气滞血瘀之胸胁胀痛，胸痹心痛
indications.en: Chest and hypochondriac pain, chest bi and Heart pain from qi stagnation and blood stasis
Irregular menstruation, dysmenorrhea, and amenorrhea
Febrile disease with clouded spirit, mania and epilepsy from phlegm obstruction

indications.zh: 血热妄行之吐血、衄血、尿血
indications.en: Hematemesis, epistaxis, and hematuria from heat in blood
Damp-heat jaundice and gallstones

contraindications_zh: 阴虚失血及无气滞血瘀者忌用。孕妇慎用。不宜与丁香、母丁香同用（十九畏）。
contraindications_en: Contraindicated in yin vacuity with bleeding and absence of qi stagnation or blood stasis. Use with caution during pregnancy. Should not be used concurrently with ding xiang (clove) or mu ding xiang per the Nineteen Incompatibilities (shijiu wei).

dosage_range.notes: 煎服常用3–10g；研末服每次2–5g。广郁金（黄丝郁金）长于行气解郁；川郁金（黑郁金）长于活血祛瘀。

processing_methods.name: 生郁金
processing_methods.effect: 活血行气、清心凉血、利胆退黄之力较强

processing_methods.name: 醋郁金
processing_methods.effect: 增强疏肝止痛之功，多用于肝郁气滞胁痛

safety_notes_zh: 十九畏：郁金畏丁香，不宜与丁香、母丁香同用。孕妇慎用。阴虚失血及无气滞血瘀者忌服。广郁金与川郁金功效有别，临证宜区分使用。
safety_notes_en: Per the Nineteen Incompatibilities (shijiu wei): yu jin is incompatible with ding xiang; should not be used with ding xiang or mu ding xiang. Use with caution during pregnancy. Contraindicated in yin vacuity with bleeding and absence of qi stagnation or blood stasis. Guang yu jin and chuan yu jin differ in efficacy; they should be differentiated in clinical use.

classical_references.passage: 郁金味辛苦，性寒，入心、肺、肝经，能行气解郁，凉血破瘀。

description_zh: 姜科植物温郁金、姜黄、广西莪术或蓬莪术的干燥块根。归肝、心、肺经。有广郁金（黄丝郁金，产浙江）与川郁金（黑郁金，产四川、广西）之分：广郁金偏行气解郁，川郁金偏活血祛瘀。本品性寒入血分，既能活血，又能凉血、清心、利胆，为血中气药。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 郁金含姜黄素类及倍半萜类成分，具有利胆、保肝、抗炎、降脂及抗血小板聚集作用，为行气解郁、活血利胆的药理基础。
summary.en: Curcuma rhizome (Yu Jin) contains curcuminoids and sesquiterpenes with choleretic, hepatoprotective, anti-inflammatory, hypolipidemic and antiplatelet effects, underlying its qi-moving, blood-invigorating and bile-promoting actions.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Hypochondriac pain and dysmenorrhea from qi stagnation and blood stasis

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 肝胆湿热之黄疸、胆石症辅助
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Adjunct for jaundice and cholelithiasis from liver-gallbladder damp-heat

evidence_notes.note_zh: Bensky 区分郁金与姜黄，指出郁金性寒而姜黄性温，两者不可混用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Bensky distinguishes Yu Jin (cooling) from Jiang Huang (warming), noting they are not interchangeable.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》收载四种来源的郁金，均以挥发油和姜黄素类含量为质量指标。
evidence_notes.note_en: The Chinese Pharmacopoeia lists four Yu Jin source species, with volatile oil and curcuminoid content as quality markers.

description_en: The dried tuberous root of Curcuma wenyujin, C. longa, C. kwangsiensis, or C. phaeocaulis (Zingiberaceae). Enters the Liver, Heart, and Lung channels. Pungent, bitter, and cool. Two main varieties are distinguished: guang yu jin (yellow-silk, from Zhejiang) excels at moving qi and resolving constraint, while chuan yu jin (black, from Sichuan and Guangxi) excels at invigorating blood and dispelling stasis. Cold in nature and entering the blood level, it is described as 'qi within the blood.'

indications.zh: 气滞血瘀诸痛，胸胁脘腹疼痛
indications.en: Various pain from qi stagnation and blood stasis, including chest, hypochondriac, and epigastric pain
Dysmenorrhea and irregular menstruation
Postpartum abdominal pain from stasis
Pain from traumatic injury
Bi syndrome with wind-damp
Headache and lumbar pain

contraindications_zh: 孕妇禁用。血热气虚及月经先期、血崩者忌用。
contraindications_en: Contraindicated during pregnancy. Contraindicated in blood heat with qi vacuity, early menstruation, and uterine flooding.

dosage_range.notes: 煎服常用3–10g；研末吞服每次1.5–3g。止痛以醋制品为佳。

processing_methods.name: 生延胡索
processing_methods.effect: 活血行气之力较强

processing_methods.name: 醋延胡索
processing_methods.effect: 增强行气止痛之功，醋制后生物碱易溶出，止痛效果显著增强，为临床常用品

safety_notes_zh: 孕妇禁用。现代药理研究显示含延胡索乙素等生物碱，具明显镇痛作用，但大剂量可致嗜睡、眩晕等。醋制可增强止痛效果并降低副作用。
safety_notes_en: Contraindicated during pregnancy. Modern pharmacological research shows it contains dl-tetrahydropalmatine and other alkaloids with significant analgesic effects, but high doses may cause drowsiness and dizziness. Vinegar-processing enhances the analgesic effect and reduces side effects.

classical_references.passage: 延胡索能行血中气滞，气中血滞，故专治一身上下诸痛。

description_zh: 罂粟科植物延胡索的干燥块茎。归肝、脾经。善于活血行气止痛，能行血中气滞、气中血滞，专治一身上下诸痛，为活血行气止痛之要药。李时珍谓其「能行血中气滞，气中血滞，故专治一身上下诸痛，用之中的，妙不可言」。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

active_constituents.name_zh: 四氢帕马丁 / 延胡索乙素
active_constituents.notes: principal central analgesic; dopamine D2 receptor antagonist, non-addictive

summary.zh: 延胡索主含异喹啉类生物碱，其中四氢帕马丁（罗通定）通过阻断多巴胺 D2 受体发挥中枢性镇痛作用，无成瘾性，是其活血行气止痛功效的物质基础。
summary.en: Corydalis contains isoquinoline alkaloids; tetrahydropalmatine (rotundine) is the principal central analgesic, acting through dopamine D2 receptor blockade without addiction liability, providing the pharmacological basis for its blood-invigorating, qi-moving pain-relieving action.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 气滞血瘀诸痛证（痛经、脘腹痛、头痛）
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Pain from qi stagnation and blood stasis (dysmenorrhea, epigastric pain, headache)

evidence_notes.note_zh: 罗通定片收录于《中国药典》，为非成瘾性中枢镇痛药。
evidence_notes.note_en: Rotundine tablets are listed in the Chinese Pharmacopoeia as a non-addictive central analgesic.

evidence_notes.note_zh: Chen & Chen 引述中国多项临床研究证实四氢帕马丁镇痛疗效可达吗啡 40%。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen cite multiple Chinese clinical studies showing THP analgesia reaches roughly 40% the potency of morphine.

evidence_notes.note_zh: Bensky 称延胡索为『活血行气止痛之良药』，现代研究与传统经验相印证。
evidence_notes.note_en: Bensky calls Yan Hu Suo a superb blood-invigorating, qi-moving analgesic, with modern research corroborating classical experience.

description_en: The dried tuber of Corydalis yanhusuo (Papaveraceae). Enters the Liver and Spleen channels. Pungent, bitter, and warm, it excels at invigorating blood and moving qi to alleviate pain, treating qi stagnation within blood and blood stasis within qi. Li Shizhen stated it 'specifically treats all pain throughout the body, above and below,' making it the foremost herb for combined blood-invigorating and qi-moving pain relief.

actions.en: Invigorate blood, move qi, and alleviate pain
Relax sinews and unblock the collaterals

indications.zh: 气滞血瘀之胸痹心痛，脘腹疼痛
indications.en: Chest bi and epigastric pain from qi stagnation and blood stasis

indications.zh: 痛经，经闭，产后瘀阻腹痛
indications.en: Dysmenorrhea, amenorrhea, and postpartum abdominal pain from stasis
Wind-damp bi syndrome with sinew contracture
Traumatic injury with stasis, swelling, and pain
Sores and carbuncles, including unhealed ulcerations

contraindications_zh: 孕妇禁用。胃弱者慎用。痈疽已溃者不宜多用。无瘀滞者忌用。
contraindications_en: Contraindicated during pregnancy. Use with caution in those with weak stomach. Not suitable for excessive use in already-ulcerated abscesses. Contraindicated when there is no stasis.

dosage_range.notes: 煎服常用3–10g，多入丸散；外用适量，研末调敷。常与没药相须为用。

processing_methods.name: 生乳香
processing_methods.effect: 活血消肿、外用生肌之力较强，多用于外科疮疡

processing_methods.name: 醋乳香
processing_methods.effect: 减轻刺激性，降低恶心呕吐之弊，增强活血止痛之功，为内服常用品

safety_notes_zh: 气味浓烈，对胃有较强刺激性，可引起恶心呕吐，故胃弱者慎服。孕妇禁用。醋炒后可降低胃肠刺激。过敏体质者慎用。常与没药相须为用，二者合称「乳没」。
safety_notes_en: Strong aroma with significant gastric irritation; may cause nausea and vomiting, so use with caution in those with weak stomach. Contraindicated during pregnancy. Vinegar-frying reduces gastrointestinal irritation. Use with caution in those with allergic constitution. Often used together with mo yao in mutual enhancement; the two are collectively called 'ru mo' (frankincense and myrrh).

classical_references.passage: 乳香活血定痛，消肿生肌，治心腹诸痛，跌打损伤，疮疡肿毒。

description_zh: 橄榄科植物乳香树及同属植物皮部渗出的油胶树脂。归心、肝、脾经。善于活血行气止痛，外用能消肿生肌，为伤科、外科要药。常与没药相须为用（「乳没」），如活络效灵丹、七厘散。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 乳香为橄榄科 Boswellia 树脂，主含乳香酸（尤其 AKBA）及挥发油，现代研究显示其为 5-脂氧合酶抑制剂，具抗炎、抗关节炎及抗肿瘤的临床前与部分临床证据，对应传统活血止痛、消肿生肌之功。
summary.en: Frankincense (Boswellia) resin contains boswellic acids (notably AKBA) and volatile oils. It acts as a 5-lipoxygenase inhibitor with preclinical and some clinical evidence for anti-inflammatory, antiarthritic, and antitumor effects, aligning with its traditional blood-moving and swelling-reducing use.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 骨关节炎、类风湿关节炎辅助
跌打损伤、痛经、痈疽肿痛
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Traumatic injury, dysmenorrhea, sores and swellings

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 溃疡性结肠炎辅助（Boswellia 提取物）

evidence_notes.note_zh: WHO 专著收录 Olibanum (Boswellia)，支持其用于炎症性关节疾病及消化道炎症。
evidence_notes.note_en: WHO monograph on Olibanum supports its use in inflammatory joint disease and GI inflammation.

evidence_notes.note_zh: Chen & Chen 记载乳香为胃肠道刺激物，易致恶心呕吐，与没药同用减量；有接触性皮炎与过敏反应报告。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen note that frankincense is gastrointestinally irritating and often paired with myrrh at reduced dose; contact dermatitis and allergic reactions have been reported.

evidence_notes.note_zh: Brinker 提示 Boswellia 提取物可能增加 CYP3A4 底物代谢，与化疗药合用需谨慎。
evidence_notes.note_en: Brinker notes that Boswellia extracts may increase CYP3A4 substrate metabolism; caution is advised with concurrent chemotherapy agents.

description_en: The oleogum resin exuded from the bark of Boswellia carterii and related species (Burseraceae). Enters the Heart, Liver, and Spleen channels. Pungent, bitter, and warm, it excels at invigorating blood, moving qi, and alleviating pain, while externally it reduces swelling and generates flesh. It is a key herb in trauma and external medicine, commonly paired with mo yao ('frankincense and myrrh') as in Huo Luo Xiao Ling Dan and Qi Li San.

actions.en: Dispel stasis and unblock the menses

indications.en: Traumatic injury with blood stasis, swelling, and pain
Chest, abdominal, and hypochondriac pain from qi and blood stasis

indications.zh: 经闭痛经，产后瘀阻腹痛
indications.en: Amenorrhea, dysmenorrhea, and postpartum abdominal pain from stasis
Wind-damp bi syndrome with sinew contracture
Sores, carbuncles, scrofula, and goiter

contraindications_zh: 孕妇禁用。胃弱者慎用。无瘀滞及痈疽已溃者不宜用。
contraindications_en: Contraindicated during pregnancy. Use with caution in those with weak stomach. Not suitable when there is no stasis or in already-ulcerated abscesses.

dosage_range.notes: 煎服常用3–10g，多入丸散；外用适量，研末调敷。常与乳香相须为用。

processing_methods.name: 生没药
processing_methods.effect: 破血散瘀、消肿生肌之力较强，多外用

processing_methods.name: 醋没药
processing_methods.effect: 降低刺激性及恶心呕吐之弊，增强活血止痛之功，为内服常用品

safety_notes_zh: 味苦气香，对胃刺激较强，易引起恶心呕吐，胃弱者慎用。孕妇禁用。醋炒后刺激性减轻。常与乳香同用，功效相辅相成，为伤科、外科要药。
safety_notes_en: Bitter in flavor and aromatic; strongly stimulates the stomach and may easily cause nausea and vomiting; use with caution in those with weak stomach. Contraindicated during pregnancy. Vinegar-frying reduces its irritating nature. Often used together with ru xiang with complementary effects; a key herb in traumatology and surgery.

classical_references.passage: 没药破血止痛，消肿生肌，治金疮折伤，心腹瘀痛，癥瘕经闭。

description_zh: 橄榄科植物地丁树或哈地丁树的干燥树脂。归心、肝、脾经。功似乳香而破血之力较胜。常与乳香相须为用（「乳没」），共奏活血散瘀、消肿止痛之功，为治跌打损伤、瘀血肿痛之要药。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

active_constituents.name_zh: 呋喃倍半萜（呋喃桉-1,3-二烯）

summary.zh: 没药为橄榄科树脂，含呋喃倍半萜和挥发油，具镇痛、抗炎、抗菌及促进伤口愈合作用。
summary.en: Myrrh is an oleo-gum-resin with furanosesquiterpenes and essential oil producing analgesic, anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and wound-healing effects.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 跌打损伤和瘀血疼痛（常与乳香同用）
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Traumatic injury and blood-stasis pain (commonly paired with frankincense)

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 口腔溃疡与牙龈炎漱口

modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Topical treatment of chronic non-healing wounds

evidence_notes.note_zh: 没药活血止痛、消肿生肌，常与乳香配伍治跌打疮疡。
evidence_notes.note_en: Invigorates blood, stops pain, and promotes tissue healing; paired with frankincense for trauma and sores.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 呋喃倍半萜显示阿片样镇痛作用及抗炎活性；挥发油对口腔细菌有抑制作用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Furanosesquiterpenes show opioid-like analgesic and anti-inflammatory activity; essential oil inhibits oral bacteria.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 孕妇忌用——可能刺激子宫；大剂量偶见皮疹和胃肠不适。
evidence_notes.note_en: Contraindicated in pregnancy — may stimulate the uterus; high doses occasionally cause rash and GI upset.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》以挥发油含量及外观性状为质量指标。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chinese Pharmacopoeia uses essential oil content and macroscopic identification as quality markers.

description_en: The dried resin of Commiphora myrrha or C. molmol (Burseraceae). Enters the Heart, Liver, and Spleen channels. Bitter, pungent, and neutral, its function resembles that of ru xiang but with stronger stasis-breaking capacity. The two are commonly paired as 'frankincense and myrrh' (ru mo) for synergistic blood-invigorating, stasis-dispersing, and pain-relieving action in traumatic injury and stasis-related swelling and pain.

actions.en: Reduce swelling and alleviate pain when applied externally

indications.en: Cough with stubborn phlegm and chest oppression
Dizziness from wind-phlegm and phlegm obstruction in wind-stroke
Epilepsy, childhood convulsions, and facial paralysis
Tetanus with opisthotonos

indications.zh: 痈疽肿痛，毒蛇咬伤（外用）
indications.en: Carbuncles, swelling, and snakebite (external use)

contraindications_zh: 孕妇禁用。生品内服宜慎，多作外用。阴虚燥咳、热极、血虚动风者禁用。不宜与附子、川乌、草乌同用（十八反相关注意，虽不在传统十八反之列，临床当慎重配伍）。
contraindications_en: Contraindicated during pregnancy. Raw herb should be used cautiously for internal administration; mostly for external use. Contraindicated in yin-deficient dry cough, extreme heat, and wind-stirring from blood vacuity. Should not be used concurrently with fu zi, chuan wu, or cao wu (related to the Eighteen Antagonisms precautions; although not in the traditional Eighteen Antagonisms list, clinical combination requires caution).

dosage_range.notes: 内服须用制品：制南星3–10g；胆南星1.5–6g。生品一般外用，研末调敷或鲜品捣敷。

processing_methods.name: 生天南星
processing_methods.effect: 有毒，内服宜慎，多作外用以消肿止痛，治痈疽肿痛、毒蛇咬伤

processing_methods.name: 制天南星
processing_methods.effect: 降低毒性，减轻刺激性，增强燥湿化痰之功，为内服常用品，主治顽痰咳嗽、风痰眩晕

processing_methods.name: 胆南星
processing_methods.method: 制南星粉末与牛胆汁（或胆膏）拌和发酵
processing_methods.effect: 药性由温转凉，功能清热化痰、息风定惊，主治痰热惊风、癫痫、中风痰迷

safety_notes_zh: 本品有毒（生品毒性较强），误服或生用内服过量可致舌、咽、口腔麻木肿痛，严重者可致窒息。孕妇禁用。内服必须用炮制品。生品一般仅供外用。中毒可用生姜汁、白矾、醋等解之。
safety_notes_en: This herb is toxic (raw form has stronger toxicity); accidental ingestion or excessive internal use of raw herb may cause numbness, swelling, and pain of the tongue, throat, and mouth; severe cases may lead to asphyxiation. Contraindicated during pregnancy. Internal use must use processed products. Raw herb is generally only for external use. Poisoning can be treated with fresh ginger juice, alum, and vinegar.

classical_references.passage: 味苦，温。主心痛，寒热结气，积聚伏梁，伤筋痿拘缓，利水道。

description_zh: 天南星科植物天南星、异叶天南星或东北天南星的干燥块茎。归肺、肝、脾经。有毒。生品外用消肿止痛；制南星燥湿化痰、祛风止痉，用治顽痰咳嗽、风痰诸证；胆南星经牛胆汁制后药性转凉，清热化痰、息风定惊，为治痰热惊风之要药。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 天南星生品含草酸钙针晶及刺激性成分，有强烈喉舌麻辣和黏膜刺激性，必须炮制后使用；炮制品具化痰、祛风、解痉作用。
summary.en: Raw Arisaema contains calcium oxalate raphides and irritant constituents causing severe mucosal and oropharyngeal injury; it must be processed before use. Processed product transforms phlegm, extinguishes wind, and relieves spasm.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 中风痰壅、破伤风惊厥（胆南星）
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Wind-stroke phlegm obstruction and tetanus convulsion (as Dan Nan Xing)

evidence_notes.note_zh: 生品有毒，内服必须炮制；制南星燥湿化痰、祛风止痉，主治风痰证。
evidence_notes.note_en: Raw form is toxic and must be processed before internal use; processed Nan Xing dries damp, transforms phlegm, extinguishes wind and stops spasms.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》明确区分生天南星（限外用）、制天南星、胆南星，并规定炮制工艺。
evidence_notes.note_en: The Chinese Pharmacopoeia distinguishes raw Arisaema (external use only), processed Arisaema, and bile-processed Dan Nan Xing, with defined processing procedures.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 毒性主要来源于黏膜刺激性针晶和刺激性蛋白质，炮制可使之大部分失活。
evidence_notes.note_en: Toxicity arises from raphide crystals and irritant proteins; processing largely inactivates these.

description_en: The dried tuber of Arisaema erubescens, A. heterophyllum, or A. amurense (Araceae). Enters the Lung, Liver, and Spleen channels. Bitter, pungent, warm, and toxic. The raw form is used externally to reduce swelling and alleviate pain; the processed form (zhi nan xing) dries dampness and transforms phlegm for stubborn phlegm patterns; bile-processed (dan nan xing) becomes cool in nature and clears heat, transforms phlegm, and settles wind for phlegm-heat convulsions.

indications.en: Cough with copious phlegm and chest oppression
Sore throat and loss of voice
Lung abscess with purulent expectoration and chest pain
Cough from wind-cold or wind-heat invasion

indications.zh: 痰壅气滞，癃闭不通（开提肺气以通调水道）
indications.en: Phlegm obstruction and urinary retention (via diffusing Lung qi to regulate waterways)

contraindications_zh: 阴虚久咳及咯血者不宜用。胃溃疡者慎用。气机上逆、呕吐、眩晕、阴虚火旺者忌用。
contraindications_en: Not suitable in chronic yin-deficient cough and hemoptysis. Use with caution in peptic ulcer. Contraindicated in counterflow qi, vomiting, dizziness, and yin vacuity with effulgent fire.

dosage_range.notes: 煎服常用3–10g。为诸药之舟楫，能载药上行，用量不宜过大以免引起恶心呕吐。

processing_methods.name: 生桔梗
processing_methods.effect: 宣肺祛痰、利咽排脓，为临床常用品

processing_methods.name: 炒桔梗
processing_methods.effect: 缓和其宣散之性，用于咽喉不利兼有气滞者

safety_notes_zh: 含桔梗皂苷，大剂量可刺激胃黏膜引起恶心呕吐，故用量不宜过大。胃溃疡、咯血者慎用或忌用。素有「舟楫之剂」之称，在方中多作引经药，载诸药上行于肺。
safety_notes_en: Contains platycodin saponins; large doses may irritate the gastric mucosa causing nausea and vomiting, so dosage should not be excessive. Use with caution or avoid in peptic ulcer and hemoptysis. Traditionally called "the boat and oar herb"; in formulas it often serves as a channel-guiding herb, directing other herbs upward to the lung.

classical_references.passage: 少阴病二三日，咽痛者，可与甘草汤；不差者，与桔梗汤。桔梗汤方：桔梗一两，甘草二两。

description_zh: 桔梗科植物桔梗的干燥根。归肺经。为诸药之舟楫，能载药上行达肺，善于宣通肺气、祛痰利咽、排脓消肿。常用于咳嗽痰多、咽喉肿痛及肺痈。与甘草相配（桔梗汤）为治咽痛之基础方。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 桔梗含三萜皂苷（桔梗皂苷 D 等），刺激呼吸道黏膜反射性促进排痰，并具抗炎、降脂及免疫调节作用。
summary.en: Platycodon contains triterpenoid saponins (notably platycodin D) that reflexively stimulate bronchial mucous secretion with expectorant, anti-inflammatory, lipid-lowering, and immunomodulatory effects.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Acute and chronic bronchitis with productive cough

evidence_notes.note_zh: 皂苷刺激胃黏膜反射性增加支气管分泌，达到祛痰效果。
evidence_notes.note_en: Saponins irritate the gastric mucosa and reflexively increase bronchial secretion, producing expectoration.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 桔梗开宣肺气、载药上行，为上焦引经要药。
evidence_notes.note_en: Opens and disseminates lung qi and guides other herbs upward — a principal upper-burner channel-leading herb.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》以桔梗皂苷 D 含量（不少于 0.10%）为质量指标。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chinese Pharmacopoeia specifies platycodin D (≥0.10%) as the quality marker.

description_en: The dried root of Platycodon grandiflorus (Campanulaceae). Enters the Lung channel. Bitter, pungent, and neutral, it is described as the 'boat for all medicinals' (zhu yao zhi zhou ji) because it carries other herbs upward to the Lung. It excels at diffusing Lung qi, expelling phlegm, benefiting the throat, and expelling pus. Paired with gan cao in Jie Geng Tang as the foundational formula for sore throat.

actions.en: Clear heat and moisten the Lung

indications.en: Dry cough from Lung heat with scanty phlegm
Taxation cough from yin vacuity with blood-streaked sputum
Chronic cough from consumption with scanty phlegm and dry throat
Scrofula and phlegm nodules
Breast abscess and early-stage Lung abscess

contraindications_zh: 寒痰、湿痰不宜用。脾胃虚寒便溏者慎用。不宜与乌头类药物同用（十八反：贝母反乌头）。
contraindications_en: Not suitable for cold-phlegm and damp-phlegm. Use with caution in spleen-stomach vacuity cold with loose stools. Should not be used concurrently with aconite-type herbs per the Eighteen Antagonisms (shiba fan): bei mu antagonizes wu tou.

dosage_range.notes: 煎服3–10g；研粉冲服每次1–2g效佳。川贝母价贵，多用于虚证、燥证之咳嗽；实证热痰宜用浙贝。

processing_methods.name: 川贝母
processing_methods.method: 生用，拣净杂质，打碎或研粉
processing_methods.effect: 清热润肺、化痰止咳，多用于研粉冲服或入丸散，以保存药效

safety_notes_zh: 十八反：贝母反乌头，不宜与川乌、草乌、附子等同用。本品性偏凉润，寒痰、湿痰咳嗽及脾胃虚寒者不宜。川贝母资源稀少，价格昂贵，不可滥用。常与梨同炖以治燥咳（川贝炖梨）。
safety_notes_en: Per the Eighteen Antagonisms (shiba fan): bei mu antagonizes wu tou; should not be used with chuan wu, cao wu, fu zi, etc.. This herb is cool and moistening in nature; not suitable in cold-phlegm, damp-phlegm cough, and spleen-stomach vacuity cold. Chuan bei mu is a scarce resource and expensive; it should not be used indiscriminately. Often stewed with pear to treat dry cough (chuan bei stewed pear).

classical_references.passage: 味辛，平。主伤寒烦热，淋沥邪气，疝瘕，喉痹，乳难，金疮风痉。

description_zh: 百合科植物川贝母、暗紫贝母、甘肃贝母或梭砂贝母的干燥鳞茎。归肺、心经。与浙贝相较，川贝偏于润肺止咳、养阴化痰，尤宜于虚劳久咳、肺热燥咳及阴虚咳嗽；浙贝则偏于清热化痰、开郁散结，多用于实证热痰。川贝价贵，临床多研粉冲服。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 川贝母的主要活性成分为甾体生物碱（贝母素甲、贝母素乙、川贝碱），具有镇咳、祛痰、平喘及降压作用。
summary.en: Fritillariae cirrhosae Bulbus contains steroidal alkaloids (peimine, peiminine, imperialine) with antitussive, expectorant, bronchodilatory, and hypotensive activity.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Yin-deficient dry cough and chronic cough with scanty sputum

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 慢性支气管炎（川贝枇杷膏等制剂）
modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Chronic bronchitis (as in Chuan Bei Pi Pa Gao)

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中药学》（Bensky）指出川贝母与乌头（附子）列入十八反，不宜同用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Bensky notes the 'eighteen incompatibilities' prohibition against combining Fritillaria with Aconitum species (fu zi).

evidence_notes.note_zh: 陈氏药理学记载川贝母生物碱在动物实验中显示剂量依赖性的镇咳和祛痰作用。
evidence_notes.note_en: Chen & Chen report dose-dependent antitussive and expectorant activity of Fritillaria alkaloids in animal studies.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》2020年版规定川贝母以贝母素甲和贝母素乙总量不得少于 0.050% 计。
evidence_notes.note_en: The 2020 Chinese Pharmacopoeia requires total peimine plus peiminine of not less than 0.050% in Chuan Bei Mu.

description_en: The dried bulb of Fritillaria cirrhosa, F. unibracteata, F. przewalskii, or F. delavayi (Liliaceae). Enters the Lung and Heart channels. Bitter, sweet, and cool. Compared with zhe bei mu, chuan bei mu excels at moistening the Lung, stopping cough, nourishing yin, and transforming phlegm, making it most suitable for consumptive chronic cough, Lung-heat dryness cough, and yin-vacuity cough. It is expensive and usually taken as powder rather than decocted.

actions.en: Direct qi downward and stop cough

indications.zh: 风热或痰热咳嗽，痰黄黏稠
indications.en: Cough from wind-heat or phlegm-heat with thick yellow sputum
Lung abscess with purulent expectoration
Scrofula, phlegm nodules, and goiter
Breast abscess and toxic sores
Sore and swollen throat

contraindications_zh: 寒痰、湿痰及脾胃虚寒者不宜用。不宜与乌头类药物同用（十八反：贝母反乌头）。
contraindications_en: Not suitable in cold-phlegm, damp-phlegm, and spleen-stomach vacuity cold. Should not be used concurrently with aconite-type herbs per the Eighteen Antagonisms (shiba fan): bei mu antagonizes wu tou.

dosage_range.notes: 煎服5–10g。浙贝清热散结之力胜于川贝，多用于实证；川贝润肺化痰之力胜于浙贝，多用于虚证。

processing_methods.name: 浙贝母
processing_methods.effect: 清热化痰、散结消痈，为临床常用品

safety_notes_zh: 十八反：贝母反乌头，不宜与川乌、草乌、附子等同用。本品苦寒，脾胃虚寒、寒痰湿痰咳嗽者不宜。清热散结之功胜于川贝，多用于风热痰火及痈疽瘰疬等实证。
safety_notes_en: Per the Eighteen Antagonisms (shiba fan): bei mu antagonizes wu tou; should not be used with chuan wu, cao wu, fu zi, etc.. This herb is bitter and cold; not suitable in spleen-stomach vacuity cold and cold-phlegm or damp-phlegm cough. Superior to chuan bei mu in clearing heat and dissipating nodules; mostly used for wind-heat phlegm-fire and repletion patterns such as abscesses and scrofula.

classical_references.passage: 浙贝清降肺胃之热，开郁散结，善治风热痰火之咳嗽及瘰疬痈肿。

description_zh: 百合科植物浙贝母的干燥鳞茎。归肺、心经。浙贝母原名象贝，产浙江，与川贝母相对。与川贝相较，浙贝苦寒较甚，偏于清热化痰、开郁散结，多用于外感风热、痰火郁结之咳嗽，以及瘰疬、瘿瘤、乳痈、肺痈等实证；川贝则偏于润肺止咳，多用于虚证燥咳。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

summary.zh: 浙贝母主含贝母素甲（peimine）和贝母素乙（peiminine）等甾体异喹啉类生物碱，具有镇咳、祛痰、平喘、解痉及抗炎作用，较川贝母清热散结力更强。
summary.en: Fritillaria thunbergii bulb contains steroidal isoquinoline alkaloids including peimine (verticine) and peiminine with antitussive, expectorant, antiasthmatic, antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects; compared with Chuan Bei Mu it more strongly clears heat and disperses nodules.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_en: Scrofula, phlegm nodules and suppurative sores

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中药学》（Bensky）强调浙贝母反乌头类，十八反禁忌须遵守。
evidence_notes.note_en: Bensky emphasizes that Zhe Bei Mu is part of the eighteen antagonisms and is incompatible with Aconitum species.

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》以贝母素甲和贝母素乙的总含量为质量指标。
evidence_notes.note_en: The Chinese Pharmacopoeia uses combined peimine and peiminine content as a quality marker.

description_en: The dried bulb of Fritillaria thunbergii (Liliaceae). Enters the Lung and Heart channels. Bitter and cold. Originally called xiang bei (elephant bulb), from Zhejiang. Compared with chuan bei mu, zhe bei mu is more bitter and cold, excelling at clearing heat and transforming phlegm, opening constraint and dispersing nodules. It is used primarily for external wind-heat, phlegm-fire constraint cough, and for repletion-pattern conditions such as scrofula, goiter, breast abscess, and lung abscess.

actions.en: Expand the chest and dissipate nodules
Moisten the Lung and stop cough
Moisten the intestines and unblock the bowels

indications.en: Cough from phlegm-heat with thick yellow sputum
Chest bi-obstruction with cardiac pain, chest oppression, and hypochondriac distension
Chest bind with focal distention and fullness
Lung abscess with cough expectorating pus and blood
Breast abscess with swelling and pain
Constipation due to intestinal dryness

contraindications_zh: 脾胃虚寒、大便溏泄及湿痰、寒痰者不宜。反乌头（十八反），不宜与川乌、草乌、附子同用。
contraindications_en: Not suitable in spleen-stomach vacuity cold, loose stools and diarrhea, and damp-phlegm or cold-phlegm. Antagonizes wu tou per the Eighteen Antagonisms (shiba fan); should not be used with chuan wu, cao wu, or fu zi.

dosage_range.notes: 全瓜蒌9–15g；瓜蒌皮6–10g偏于宽胸；瓜蒌仁9–15g偏于润肠。打碎入煎。

processing_methods.name: 生瓜蒌
processing_methods.effect: 清热化痰、宽胸散结、润肠通便并重

processing_methods.name: 瓜蒌霜
processing_methods.effect: 减其滑润之性，长于化痰而不致滑肠

safety_notes_zh: 含脂肪油，滑肠作用明显，脾虚便溏者慎用。与乌头类药物配伍有毒性协同风险，属十八反禁忌。
safety_notes_en: Contains fatty oils with notable intestinal-lubricating effects; use with caution in spleen vacuity with loose stools. Combining with aconite-type herbs poses a risk of toxicity synergy, classified under the Eighteen Antagonisms prohibition.

classical_references.passage: 味苦，寒。主消渴，身热，烦满，大热，补虚安中，续绝伤。

description_zh: 葫芦科植物栝楼或双边栝楼的干燥成熟果实。归肺、胃、大肠经。既能清肺化痰以治痰热咳嗽，又能宽胸散结以治胸痹结胸，兼润肠通便，为治疗胸痹要药，常与薤白、半夏相配（瓜蒌薤白白酒汤、瓜蒌薤白半夏汤）。

verification.reviewer: Bencaodian Editorial / 本草典编辑部

active_constituents.name_zh: 天花粉蛋白（主要来源于根）
active_constituents.notes: Chiefly in root (tianhuafen); fruit and peel emphasized below

summary.zh: 瓜蒌（含瓜蒌皮、瓜蒌仁及全瓜蒌）主含脂肪油、三萜皂苷、葫芦素类与黄酮苷，药理示其祛痰、扩冠、抗炎、润肠通便与抗血小板作用。
summary.en: Gualou (peel, seed, whole fruit) contains fatty oils, triterpene saponins, cucurbitacins and flavonoid glycosides; pharmacology shows expectorant, coronary-dilating, anti-inflammatory, laxative and antiplatelet effects.

modern_clinical_uses.indication_zh: 痰热咳嗽、胸痹（冠心病心绞痛）

evidence_notes.note_zh: 《中国药典》分别收载瓜蒌、瓜蒌皮与瓜蒌子，并规定各自的质量标准。
